tta nilam itta••••• velikku kiḻpāṟkellai kā-
kku meṟku teṉpāṟkellai karai vāykkālukku vaṭakku vaṭapāṟkellai vātūḷi
cantira ṉārurāṉ [kiḷa]ṟtalai [kaṇi] nāṉmāvukkut teṟkum ivvicainta perunāṉkellai
vanta iṟaiyum eccoṟum veṭṭivetiṉaiyum maṟṟum koṟṟa vācali-
ṟ ponta kuṭimai ellām nāṅkaḷe iṟuppatāka iccaṇṭe
kkaṭṭuva•• ca•• ṅkaḷāṟ camaitta palakai koṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 471.
(A.R. No. 440 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On another pillar in the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēś
vara temple.
Rājarāja I. Year 6: 990-91 A.D.

This records the gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp to the deity Tirumaṟaikkāṭṭu
Mahādēva, by Nāraṇaṉ Śāttan, a veḷḷāḷa of Eyiṉaṉūr.

inscription.head:
No. 472.
(A.R. No. 441 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On another pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdā-
raṇyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja. Year 9: 993-94 A.D.

This records the gift of 7 kāśu by Vīṟaikuḍi-kiḻavaṉ Vēḷāṉ Nārāyaṇaṉ alias Śembiyaṉ
Kīḻkārai Vēḷāṉ, of Muttūṟṟu-kūṟṟam, in Pāṇḍi-nāḍu, for offering daily 2 palams of
śarkkarai (country sugar) out of 12 akkam accruing as interest on the endowed amount,
to the god Tirumaṟaikkāḍ-uḍaiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 473.
(A.R. No. 442 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara
temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 6.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 128.

inscription.head:
No. 474.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On another pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēś-
vara temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 30.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 317.

inscription.head:
No. 475.
(A.R. No. 444 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the third pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēś-
vara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 20: 926-27 A.D.
This inscription is dated in the reign of Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsari.

It records the gift of 60 kaḻañju of gold to god Mahādēva of Tirumaṟaikkāḍu,
a perundēvadāṉa in Umbaḷa-nāḍu for the maintenance (jīvitam) of one Brāhmaṇa well
versed in the vēdas and archchanāvidhi serving the god during the 3 sandhis, by Va.san
Dē[var]. Kaḍambaṉ of Māṉanilainallūr, a brahmadēya in Kaḷakkuḍi-nāḍu in Pāṇḍi-
nāḍu. The sābha of Naṟukaḍambiḍu-chchaturvēdimaṅgalam a mēṟṟuṇi (?) brahma-
dēya in the same nāḍu, received the amount and agreed to provide paddy for the purpose,
as interest on the amount endowed.

edition.p:
1) Māṉanilainallūr is the same as Māṉūr in Tirunelveli District. See Ep. Ind.
XXI, p. 9, Text, lines 6-8.

inscription.head:
No. 476.
(A.R. No. 445 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the third pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēś-
vara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 28: 934-35 A.D.
This inscription is dated in the reign of Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsari.

It records the endowment of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp to god Mahādēva in
Tirumaṟaikkāḍu, a perundēvadāna in Umbaḷa-nāḍu by Marudūruḍaiyān Aruṇidi
Kaliyan of Marudūr in Puṟaṅgarambai-nāḍu, an officer under the king. The measure
Tirumaṟaikkāḍan is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 477.
(A.R. No. 446 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the third pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēś-
vara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year [21]: 927-28 A.D.

This inscription is dated in the reign of Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsari. The date
which is not clear on the impression in the office had been subsequently read in situ.

It records an endowment of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp for god Āḷvar in Tiru-
maṟaikkāḍu, a perundēvadāṉa in Umbaḷa-nāḍu, by a native of Ārvala-kkūṟṟam. The
measure Tirumaṟaikkāḍaṉ is mentioned.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] [ matirai koṇ-
ṭa kopparakecaripaṉ]-

inscription.head:
No. 478.
(A.R. No. 447 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the third pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēś-
vara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 24: 930-31 A.D.
This inscription is dated in the reign of Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsarivarman.

It records that at the instance of Amudaṉ Kāñchaṉ, the administrator of the district
(nāḍu śeykiṉṟa), arrangement was made for the supply of curd-rice to god Āḷvār in
Tirumaṟaikkāḍu for which some fields were endowed by Śiyavūr Kāḍaṉaiyaṉ, [Kāna]
ṟṟambulavūr and Kāñchambūlavūr.

inscription.head:
No. 479.
(A.R. No. 448 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a third pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēś-
vara temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 6.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 83

inscription.head:
No. 480.
(A.R. No. 449 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the third pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdā-
raṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year [3]7: 943-44 A.D.
This inscription is dated in the reign of Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsari.

It records the gift of 96 sheep for a perpetual lamp for god Mahādēva in
Tirumaṟaikkāḍu, a perundēvadāna in Umbaḷa-nāḍu, by Irumuḍiśōḻa Aṇukki[li]
Chchāman-āchchan I.kaṉāch[cha]ṉ, for the merit of his sister. The sister's mother
[I]la[tta]naṅgai is stated to have been a servant in the big palace establishment
(periyavēḷam) at Tañjāvūr.

inscription.head:
No. 481.
(A.R. No. 450 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the third pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇ-
yēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 20: 926-27 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 90 sheep by Śūlapāṇi Bhaṭṭaśāli, a vaikhānasa
for burning a perpetual lamp with one uḻakku of ghee in the Vishṇu temple situated to
the north of the Tirumaṟaikkāṭṭu-mādēvar temple, in Umbaḷa-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 482.
(A.R. No. 451 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the fourth pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 1[8]: 925 A.D.
The details of date, viz., Makara, Monday, and Śoti and Saṅkrānti are irregular.

This inscription records the gift of 90 sheep, by Māṉinakka-pirāṭṭiyār, wife of
Śembiyaṉ Mūvēndavēḷār alias Śāttaṉ Ulagaṉ of Vaṇḍāḻañjēri in Niṉṟayūr-nāḍu for
burning a perpetual lamp to the deity with one uḻakku of ghee by the measure Tiru-
maṟaikkāḍaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 483.
(A.R. No. 452 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the fourth pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 25: 931-32 A.D.

This damaged inscription records the gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual
lamp to the deity Tirumaṟaikkāṭṭu-mahādēva, probably by Nakki, a female
resident of Nandipuram(?).

l.note: The continuation consisting of about 8 more lines of writing is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 484.
(A.R. No. 453 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the fourth pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇ-
yēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 13: 919-20 A.D.

This inscription records an endowmeṉt of 25 kaḻañju of gold on Saṅkrānti day by
Kōyilāṉāchchipiḍāriyār, wife of Śembiyan Mūvēndavēḷār for food offerings to the deity
Tirumaṟaikkāṭṭu-mādēvar from out of the interest accruing from the endowed
amount.

inscription.head:
No. 485.
(A.R. No. 454 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇ-
yēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 18: 924-25 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 90 sheep, entrusted to the care of the shepherds
of the Tirumaṟaikkāṭṭudēvar temple, by the Daṇḍīśvarattār for Chaṇḍiśvarāttār of the
temple for burning a perpetual lamp to the deity with one uḻakku of ghee daily.

inscription.head:
No. 486.
(A.R. No. 455 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNḌI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇ-
yēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 30: 936-37 A.D.

This damaged inscription records the gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp
to the deity with one uḻakku of ghee daily, by the measure Tirumaṟaikkāḍaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 487.
(A.R. No. 456 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdā-
raṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 27: 933-34 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 180 sheep by Eḻuvaṉ Vaḍukaṉ, for burning two
perpetual lamps to the deity Tirumaṟaikkāṭṭu-mahādēva, with one uri of ghee daily
by the measure called Tirumaṟaikkāḍaṉ.

l.ta: itaṉukku vacca āṭu nūṟṟeṇpatu i[vvāṭu] nū-

inscription.head:
No. 488.
(A.R. No. 457 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdā-
raṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 32: 938-39 A.D.

This mutilated inscription seems to record a gift of sheep for burning a perpetual
lamp for the merit of Kaḍambaṉ Eḻuvaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 489.
(A.R. No. 458 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēś-
vara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 30: 936-37 A.D.

This damaged inscription records the gift of day-lamp to the deity
Kōṭīśvaradēva.

edition.l: • itu panmāhe[ śva ra*]-

inscription.head:
No. 490.
(A.R. No. 459 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa of the central shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara
temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 5.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 119

inscription.head:
No. 491.
(A.R. No. 460 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājēndra III. Year 16: 1262 A.D.

The details of date, viz., Ṛishabha, śu. 11, Monday, Uttiram, regularly correspond
to 1262 A.D., May 1.

This records the gift of 24 vēli, 6 mā, and 3 kāṇi of land by Nārāyaṇaṉ of Paḍappai
in Malaimaṇḍalam for meeting the expenses for the conduct of a service called
Tiruvikkiramaṉ-sandhi on two occasions and for burning three perpetual lamps
to the deity Tirumaṟaikkāḍu Uḍaiyār.

l.ta: uttirattu nāḷ malaimaṇṭalattu paṭappai nārāyaṇaṉeṉ uṭaiy[ā*]r
yaruḷavum amutu ceytaruḷavum muṉṟu tirunantā viḷakku eriyavum ve-
ṇṭum nivantaṅkaḷukku eṉ kāṇiyil kuṭutta rājentiracoḻa vaḷanā[ṭṭu]
kkāṇiyāka kuṭutta ūṟṟaṅkāl kaṇṭamum naṟuvaṭṭaṇi āṟ-
ṟukku mel karaiyilum kiḻkaraiyilum maṟṟum nilaṅkalantavā-
kkiṉa kiṭaṅkum pākamum parakācirayamum potuvum potuvāva-
yākak kuṭutteṉ paṭappai nārāyaṇaṉeṉ eḻuttu ippaṭi [aṟi]-
veṉ ayarāṟṟuc caṉṉiyeṉ ippaṭi aṟiveṉ paṭappai••

inscription.head:
No. 492.
(A.R. No. 461 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara
temple.
Rājarāja I. Year 16, day 36: 1000 A.D.

This records that Rājarājavājya(for vādya)mārāyar enquired into the pre-
vious cash endowments to the temple and provided for an endowment of 10 kāśu for
bathing the god with 108 kalaśas on the day of Śadaiyam, the birth-day asterism of
the king.

l.note:
This hall (ambalam) stated to have been built by a Bhaṭṭar of Tirumṟaikkāḍu is probably the same
as the one referred to in No. 466 above.

inscription.head:
No. 493.
(A.R. No. 462 of 1904).
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja I. Year 19: 1003-04 A.D.

This inscription is damaged and mutilated. It begins with the praśasti commencing
with Tirumagaḷ pōla etc., and mentions Iḍaiyaḷa-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 494.
(A.R. No. 463 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara
temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 14.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 339.

inscription.head:
No. 495.
(A.R. No. 463-A of 1904).
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēś-
vara temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 4.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 81.

inscription.head:
No. 496.
(A.R. No. 464 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara
temple.
Year 16.

This inscription engraved in characters of the 11th century A.D. does not mention
any king. This records the gift of land (as kuḍinīṅgā-dēvadāna) made tax-free,
entrusted to Nāraṇan Kāḍaṉ, by the inhabitants of Perumuḻai for food and other
offerings to the value of 10 akkam in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 497.
(A.R. No. 465 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine, Vēdāraṇyēś-
vara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 10: 916-17 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of sheep by Naṅgaiyār Nakkaṉ Varagu[ṇi], a queen
of Śōḻamāṉār for a lamp to god Mādēva of Tirumaṟaikkāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 498.
(A.R. No. 466 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara
temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 3.
Published in S.I.I., XIII, No. 24.

inscription.head:
No. 499.
(A.R. No. 467 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine, in the Vēdāraṇyēś-
vara temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 8
Published in S.I.I. Vol., XIII, No. 175.

inscription.head:
No. 500.
(A.R. No. 468 of 1904).
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the first face of the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine
in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 17: 923-24 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp to be burnt before
the god at Tirumaṟaikkāḍu by Sōmaṉ-ṟūvēdi (dvivēdi), a śālāpattiyaṉ of Vāgaikkuḍi, a
brahmadēya in Umbaḷa-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 501.
(A.R. No. 468-A of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second face of the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja
shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 32: 938-39 A.D.

This inscription which is partly in verse describes the king Parāntaka
as one who destroyed the fortified (city of) Madurai. It seems to refer to
an encounter between the Chōḷa king and the king of the Siṅghaḷas (Siṅgaḷar-kōṉ).

It records the gift of 90 sheep by Guṇavaṉ, an illustrious native of Iḍaiyūr for a
perpetual lamp to the god at Tirumaṟaikkāḍu on the occasion of his return after
defeating the king of the Siṅgaḷas.

l.note: The right ends of this and the next four lines are engraved very faintly.

inscription.head:
No. 502.
(A.R. No. 468-B of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the third face of the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja
shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 32: 938-39 A.D.

This records the gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp by••• Kāḍan, the
headman of Piśār in Kaḷattūr.

inscription.head:
No. 503.
(A.R. No. 468-C-of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the fourth face of the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja
shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 25: 931-32 A.D.

This records the gift of sheep by the Nāṭṭār of Umbaḷa-nāḍu for a perpetual
lamp to the god Tirumaṟaikkāḍu-uḍaiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 504.
(A.R. No. 468-D of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the fifth face of the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja
shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 5.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 120.

inscription.head:
No. 505.
(A.R. No. 468-E of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the sixth face of the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja
shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 30: 936-37 A.D.

This records the gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp to the god Mādēva at
Tirumaṟaikkāḍu by Āṭṭi Śaḍaiyan, the headman of Kiḷiyūr.

edition.l: ccāṉṟom [|*] itu pa[n]-

inscription.head:
No. 506.
(A.R. No. 468-F of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the seventh face of the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja
shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 31: 937-38 A.D.

This records the gift of 12 sheep for an oil bath for the god Mahādēva at Tiru-
maṟaikkāḍu on every Saṅkrānti day, by Koṟṟam-Periyaṉ of Kuṉṟūr Pūśaṉūr.

l.note: The stone is worn out at the top and the bottom.

inscription.head:
No. 507.
(A.R. No. 469 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the eighth face of the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the
Tyāgarāja shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 28: 934-35 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 90 sheep by Śāttaṉ Tirumaṟaikkāḍaṉ, headman
of Pālpulam for burning a perpetual lamp to the deity Tirumaṟaikkāṭṭu Āḷvār, with
one uḻakku of ghee daily by the measure called Tirumaṟaikkāḍaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 508.
(A.R. No. 469-A of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the ninth face of the fourth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja
shrine in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 3.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 25.

inscription.head:
No. 509.
(A.R. No. 470 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the fifth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the
Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja I. Year 15: 999-1000 A.D.

This records the gift of 180 sheep by Ayyāṟan Ambalattāḍi of Perumbuliyūr
and Kaḷappāḷa Taṉṉichchai, the headman of Chiṟiñār in Iḍaiyaḷa-nāḍu for burning
two perpetual lamps with one uri of ghee daily by the kēraḷāntakaṉ-nāḻi, in the shrine
called Kēraḷāntakaṉ erected by the donor Ambalattāḍi for Bhuvaṉiviṭaṅkar of
Tirumaṟaikkāḍu.

l.note: The first few lines of the praśasti are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 510.
(A.R. No. 471 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the sixth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the Vēdāraṇ-
vēśvara temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 20.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 290.

inscription.head:
No. 511.
(A.R. No. 472 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the sixth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the
Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 6.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 156.

inscription.head:
No. 512.
(A.R. No. 473 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the seventh pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the
Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 19: 925-26 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 10 poṉ left in the hands of the temple autho-
rities and 90 sheep left under the care of the temple shepherds, by Iḍaiyaṉ Kiḻaṅgāri,
for burning two perpetual lamps to the deity Tirumaṟaikkāṭṭu Āḻvār.

inscription.head:
No. 513.
(A.R. No. 474 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the seventh pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the
Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 6.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 157.

inscription.head:
No. 514.
(A.R. No. 475 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a face of the seventh pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine
in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 9.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 217.

inscription.head:
No. 515.
(A.R. No. 475-A of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the seventh pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the
Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 10: 916-17 A.D.

This records the gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp to the Āḷvār at Tirumaṟai-
kkāḍu by Bāradāyan Uḻundan Kaṇḍan of Kumāramaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 516.
(A.R. No. 475-B of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the seventh pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the
Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 27: 933-34 A.D.

This records the gift of a perpetual lamp for the god Mahādēva of Tirumaṟaikkāḍu
by Māṇikkachcheṭṭi Duggaiyaṉāṭṭi alias Udaiyamā[r*]ttāṇḍa-ppēraraiyaṉ of Arumbūr
in Viḷa-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 517.
(A.R. No. 475-C of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the seventh pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the
Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 38: 944-45 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of a fly-whisk coated with gold and silver, by
Mūttakiḻāṉaḍigaḷ, the daughter of Kāḍupaṭṭigaḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 518.
(A.R. No. 476 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in the Vēdā-
raṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year [22]: [928-29] A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 90 sheep by Śāttaṉ Malli, a veḷḷāṭṭi (See No. 528
below) for burning a perpetual lamp for Vishṇukkaḷ in the temple at the north
(vaḍakkiṟṟaḷi) in Tirumaṟaikkāḍu.

l.ta: tu ivvāṇ ṭumpaḷa nāṭṭup peruntevatānam tirumaṟaikkāṭṭil vaṭakkiṟṟaḷiyi-

kkaḷukku aḷanāṭṭuk kiḻayilirukku veḷḷāṭṭi cāttaṉ malli vacca
nontāvi-

ḷakku oṉṟukku vacca āṭu toṇṇūṟuṅ koṇṭu tirumaṟaikkāṭaṉāl nicatam
uḻa-

inscription.head:
No. 519.
(A.R. No. 477 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in the Vēdā-
raṇyēśvara temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 27.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 293.

inscription.head:
No. 520.
(A.R. No. 478 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in the Vēdā-
raṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 30: 936-37 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 180 sheep by Iḷaiyāvañchāttaṉ of Talaikkāṇam
and Kāḍañchāttaṉ of Kuḍapulam for burning two perpetual lamps to the deity Tiru-
maṟaikkāṭṭu-mādēva, with one uri of ghee daily by the measure called Tirumaṟaik-
kāḍaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 521.
(A.R. No. 479 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in the Vēdā-
raṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 15: 921-22 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 90 sheep by Kāḍaṉ Kalitoṅgal, a merchant of
Tandiram in Tiruvārūr-kkūṟṟam for burning a perpetual lamp to the deity with one
uḻakku of ghee daily by the measure called Tirumaṟaikkāḍaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 522.
(A.R. No. 480 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the first pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in the
Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 15, 30th day: 921 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 180 sheep by Nakkaṉ Tūṇi of Mādānam in
the division of Aḷḷūr-kkuḷakkīḻ in Pāṇḍi-nāḍu and Kavaiyappi of Aḷḷūr in Pāṇḍi-
nāḍu for burning two perpetual lamps to the deity Tirumaṟaikkāṭṭu-mādēvar.

inscription.head:
No. 523.
(A.R. No. 481 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanātha shrine in the Vēdā-
raṇyēśvara temple.

This inscription in Sanskrit verse engraved in Grantha characters of about the 10th
century records the gift of 90 sheep for a lamp to lord Śiva by Kalikēsarin. (See No.
524 below).

l.note: This verse is in a variety of the Āryā metre.

inscription.head:
No. 524.
(A.R. No. 482 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in
the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 13: 919-20 A.D.

This inscription which is a fuller version of No. 523 above in Tamil records the gift
of 90 sheep by Nakkaṉ Kalikēsari, son of Śembiyaṉ Kāraikkāḍuḍaiyāṉ, for burning
a perpetual lamp to the deity with one uḻakku of ghee daily, by the measure called
Tirumaṟaikkāḍaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 525.
(A.R. No. 483 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in
the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 10.
Published in S.I.I., XIII, No. 205.

inscription.head:
No. 526.
(A.R. No. 484 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine, in
the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 8.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 195.

inscription.head:
No. 527.
(A.R. No. 485 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in
the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 7: 913-14 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 180 sheep by Amarakālaṅgāḍi, a shepherd,
for burning two perpetual lamps to the deity with one uri of ghee daily.

inscription.head:
No. 528.
(A.R. No. 486 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in
the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 32: 938-39 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 90 sheep by Śāttaṉ Malli, a veḷḷāṭṭi living at
Kīḻai of Aḷa-nāḍu(See No. 518 above) for burning a perpetual lamp to the deity, with
one uḻakku of ghee daily by the measure called Tirumaṟaikkāḍaṉ.

l.ta: āvatu ivvāṇ ṭumpaḷa nāṭṭup peruntevatāṉan tirumaṟaikkā-
ṇūṟuṅ koṇṭu tirumaṟaikkāṭaṉāl nicatam uḻakkup paṭi ney cantirātitta-

inscription.head:
No. 529.
(A.R. No. 487 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in the
Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 26.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 318.

inscription.head:
No. 530.
(A.R. No. 488 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmanāthēśvara shrine in the
Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Parāntaka I. [4]3: 949-50 A.D.

The date-portion of the record is badly damaged. The reference to the con-
quest of ceylon seems to indicate the year 43. This records the gift of 90
sheep by the children (makkaḷ) of Kāri-Śātti, a servant in the palace-establishment of
Kiḻāṉaḍigaḷ i.e., the queen, for burning a perpetual lamp to the deity with one uḻakku
of ghee daily, by the measure called Tirumaṟaikkāḍaṉ.

l.note:
There are a few lines below this engraved in characters of the same period, which seem to record a
similar gift.

inscription.head:
No. 531.
(A.R. No. 489 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the second gōpura (left of the entrance) in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Prauḍhadēvarāya. Śaka 1386—1465 A.D.

The details of date viz., Śaka 1386, Tāraṇa, Tai, 10 correspond to 1465 A.D.,
January 5.

This records the sale of 5 vēli of land for 200 paṇam by the Ādichaṇḍēśvara-dēvakaṉ-
mis of the Tirumaṟaikkāḍu temple to Vēdam Ōdiṉār Puliyūr-nāṭṭu Vēḷār, who made a
gift of it to God Tambirāṉār of the same temple for conducting the Śivarātri festival.

l.ta:
vetam otiṉār puliyūr nāṭṭu
veḷāṟku tirumaṟaikkāṭukoyil

āticaṇṭecura tevarkaṉmikaḷom nilavilai piṟamāṇam paṇṇik kuṭuttapaṭi tirumaṟaikkā
ṭuṭaiya tampirāṉāṟku civarā-

ttiṟit tipatam tammuṭai[ya]••••• ka naṭantu potukaiyil intat tipatattukkum•••••
meṉac ce••••••••• [amutu]paṭi kaṟi-

yamutukkum maṟṟum uṇṭāṉatukkum uṭalākakkoṇṭu cantirātittavaraiyum naṭatta•••••••••
kāṇiyāṉa

edition.l:
(ṉa) paṅkum vilavilai Read ‘nilavilai’• niṟ[ṇayam] Read nirṇayam. paṇṇiṉa nilam āvatu nattattukkum āṟṟukkum naṭuppaṭṭa
kaṭṭaḷai oṉṟil 5- li nilam ayvelik-

l.ta:
ippaṇam irunūṟum paṟṟikkoṇṭamaikku inta
nilattil mutalāka mutal koṇṭu canti-

rātittavaraiyum tammuṭaiya upayam āka naṭattip potakkaṭavom ākavum u ippaṭikku
ivai vetavaṉappir(ā)mārāyaṉ eḻuttu ivai

tirumaṟaikkāṭupaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ivai koyil nāyakam vetavaṉanampi eḻuttu māṭāpattiyam
takaṭṭūr kiḻavar veṇāvuṭaiyār eḻuttu

ivai māṭāpattiyam takaṭṭūr kiḻavar vaḷavataraiyaṉ eḻuttu ivai tirumāḷikaikkūṟu takaṭṭūr
kiḻavar villavataraiyaṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 532.
(A.R. No. 490 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the second gōpura (left of the entrance) of the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Pratāpadēva-manārāya. 1466 A.D.

The details of date viz. Viya (Vyaya) Mithuna śu. 10, Monday correspond to
1466 A.D., June 23.

This inscription is damaged. It seems to record the gift of the proceeds from
the levies viz. śūlavari, ōlai-eḻuttuvattaṉai, avatara vattaṉai, adhikāripēṟu i.e., koṟṟilakkai,
pāḍikāval, śādi(jāti)vari, iṉavari, veṭṭiv, etc, to the deity, by Devarāya-Mūvarāyar.

l.ta: kkut tappuvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ mūvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭa nāṭu
•••••••• paccima uttara camuttirā[ti*]pati śrīvirappiṟatāpa tevama[k][ā*]r[ā]yar piṟitivirācciyam
•••••••• vaippaṭṭaikkum pūcai mikuti cūlavari irekai muṉpilāṇṭum māṉṉiyam
•••••••• cūṭu pāṭikāval cātivariiṉavari veṭṭivari maṟṟum ucāvaṭiyil kūṭṭum
••••••••• ṟātittavaraiyum caṟuvamāṉṉiyam āka naṭakkak kaṭavatu ākavum

inscription.head:
No. 533.
(A.R. No. 491 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab built into the floor in front of the second gōpura in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara
temple.
Tuḷajā-mahārāja.

This inscription in Marāṭhī language and Nāgarī script of the seventeenth century,
records the obeisance of Aṇājā-Rāv-sēṭ and Bhagavant-Rāv-sēṭ, the ministers of the
king Tuḷajā-mahārāja.

l.de: 1 || śrīman advaitāgra[sa]ra rājaśrī setubāvāsvāmi ciraṁjīva rā-
2 || jaśrī bāvā gosāṁ vi || rājādhirāja m[ā]hārājarāje śrīma-
3 || tāṁ pratāpasiṁha m(ā)hārāja || ciraṁjīva rāje śrītuḷajā m(ā)hārājā
6 || raṇāraviṁ viṁ sāṣṭāṁga namaskāra niraṁtara kelo ase ||

inscription.head:
No. 534.
(A.R. No. 492 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājēndrachōḷa III. Year 33: 1279 A.D.
The details of date viz. Vṛiśchika śu. 10, Monday, Uttiraṭṭādi are irregular.

This inscription is damaged and mutilated. It seems to record a gift of land
made tax-free by the Nāṭṭavar of Umbaḷa-nāḍu, for [services] to the god and goddess
in the temple.

edition.l:

viruccika nāyaṟṟu••••••• [ta]camiyum tiṅkaṭki-

inscription.head:
No. 535.
(A.R. No. 493 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the first prākāra of the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Date Lost.

This damaged inscription records the gift of 1/4 vēli of land, in Vikramaśōḻaṉ-
Kaḍambūr left in the hands of the śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple, by Maṇaṟkkuḍaiyā[ṉ]
••of Iḍaiyaḷa-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, for burning a perpetual lamp.
Other details are lost.

edition.l: [svasti śrī] [||*] [tiri]bhuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī rācarācadēvaṟku yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 536.
(A.R. No. 494 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājēndrachōḷa III. Year 18: 1263 A.D.
The details of date, viz., Makara ba••••, Wednesday, Tiruvōṇam are irregular.

This records the gift of 2(1/2) vēli and māgāṇi of land, originally purchased partly
from Kuṇamalaippāḍi Uḍaiyān••••Pallavadaraiyar and partly from Nāgāṇḍāṉ
of Anapāya-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Iḍaiyaḷa-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu by Tiru-
maṟaikkāḍuḍaiyāṉ Kaṟpagapperumān of Koḍumaḷūr, who owned lands in Vāyalūṟ
Paḷḷi, in Kuṉṟūr-nāḍu, as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the god Tirumaṟaikkāḍu-uḍaiyār.

l.ta:
vatu makara nāyaṟṟu[a]para pakṣattu•ti[yai]yum putaṉkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvoṇa[t]tu
[nā]ḷ umpaḷa[nāṭṭu]••••kuṉṟūr nāṭṭu vāyalūṟp paḷ[ḷi]yil kāṇiyuṭaiya koṭumaḷūr
kiḻavaṉ tirumaṟaikkāṭuṭai-

yāṉ kaṟpakapperumāneṉ uṭaiyār tirumaṟaikkāṭṭuṭaiyārkku nāṉ tirunāmattukkāṇiyākak
kuṭutta nilamāvatu iṉnāṭṭuk [kāna]ttūr nāṭṭuk kānattūr ka•• raṉnaṟka••••tu kuṇamalaip
pāṭi uṭaiyā[n]••••ruvumuṭaiyarāṉa pallavatarayar pa-

viṭṭa nilam oṉ[ṟ]e mukkā[leoru]māvum i[ṉṉila]••••pāti ūr [na]ttattu maṉaikaḷilum
maṉaippaṭaippaikaḷum uṭpaṭavum [rā]ceṉtiracoḻa[va]ḷanāṭṭu iṭai[yaḷanāṭṭu] aṉapāyac
catuppetimaṅkalattu

nākāṇṭāṉ [pa]kkal vilaikoṇṭuṭaiyeṉāy anupavittu varukiṟa [k]oḻicceriyā neṉ[ṟu*]
perkūvap[paṭṭa] nilattuk••••ttam eṟṟi nilam mu[kkā]le kāṇiyum ākanilam iraṇṭaraiye

[kkā]ṭuṭaiyāṉā••••• neṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 537.
(A.R. No. 495 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 27: 1243 A.D.
The details of date, viz, Makara ba. 15, Monday, Pūśam are irregular.

This records the gift of 6 mākkāṇi land in Vikramaśōḻaṉ Kaḍambūr, as tiruviḷak-
kuppuṟam, by AraiyaṉUyyavandāṉ, of Kāṉattūr, for burning a perpetual lamp to the
deity Tirumaṟaikkāḍu-uḍaiyār, in Umbaḷa-nāḍu.
The gift-land was purchased from Tuṇaiyirundāḷ Śāni, by the donor.

l.ta: ra pakṣattu pañcate[ci]yum tiṅkaḷ kiḻamai[yum] peṟṟa pūcattu nāḷ umpaḷa[nā]ṭṭu u[ṭai]-
[yā]r tirumaṟaikkāṭuṭaiyārkku kāṉattūrnāṭṭu akkaḷū[r]kkāṇi uṭaiya k[āṉa]ttū[r*] kiḻa-
jantiracoḻavaḷanāṭṭut teṉkāl vikkiramacoḻaṉ kaṭampūril nāṉ k[o]ṇṭu [vi]ṭṭa nilam
innilam āṟumākkāṇiyum kaṭamai iṟuttu iṟaimikiti ko[ṇ]ṭu canti[rātit]-
tavaraiyum [ivvi]ḷakkoṉṟum celvatāka vaitteṉ kāṉattūr kiḻavaṉ araiyaṉ
[u]yyavantāṉeṉ koṇṭu viṭṭa nilam māvatu innāyanā[r] tevatānattu••••
ṭaiyāy kom[māra]ttut tiruvaṭikaḷ paṭṭaṉai mutukaṇṇāka uṭaiya tuṇai-
yiruntāḷc cāni pakkal ṉāṉ vilai koṇṭuṭaiya nilam āṟumākkāṇiyum tiruvi[ḷa]kkup-

inscription.head:
No. 538.
(A.R. No. 496 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 19: 1234 A.D.

The details of date viz., Mithuna śu 13, Sunday, Kēṭṭai, correspond to 1234 A.D.,
June 11, Sunday, ·28; ·92.

This registers the agreement regarding a gift of 1/4 vēli of land in Anapāya-chatur
vēdimaṅgalam in Iḍaiyaḷa-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu to the donor Vēḷāṉ Kuḍan-
dai Nāyakaṉ alias Pañchavarāyaṉ of Śiṟaikkāvūr, in Kiḻāy-kkūṟṟam, in Nittavinōda-
vaḷanāḍu by the temple authorities. The land was made over to the śivabrāh-
maṇas for burning a perpetual lamp with one uḻakku of oil daily in the temple. The
weight of the lamp is stated to be 80 palam.

l.ta:
[vatu] mi[tu]ṉa nāyaṟṟup
[pū]rva pakṣattu tiraiyoteciyum nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa keṭṭ[ai] nāḷ u[ṭaiyār

kuttiviḷakku 1- ṉāl iruppu n[ā*]rācam uṭpaṭa eṭai 80- la This symbol stands for ‘palam’. [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 539.
(A.R. No. 497 of 1904).
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājēndrachōḷa III. Year 2[6]: 1272 A.D.

The details of date viz., Makara ba. 6, Friday, Chittirai corresponds to 1272 A.D.
January 22, Friday, f.d.t., ·18: ·90.

This records the gift of 3/4 vēli of land made tax-free by the ūravar of Śiṟumut-
tarai and Pāṟpunaṅkiḻaiyāṉ Karikālaśōḻa-ppallavaraiyaṉ, for food offerings to the
deities Āḷuḍaiyār and Nāyakar and their Nāchchiyārs, whose images were set up by
Āṇḍanāyakaṉ alias Piramīśvaramuḍaiyār, the headman of Karaikkaḍaippu Eḻumūr in
the western division of Umbaḷa-nāḍu in the temple of Iḷamaṟaikkāḍuḍaiya
Nāyaṉār.

yum veḷḷikkiḻamai[yu]m [cittir]aiyu[mā]ṉa nāḷ
rā[ja*]rā[ja*]ppāṇṭi nāṭṭu ma[tu]r[otaiya] va[ḷa][nāṭṭu]••••• ṟṟūr nāṭṭu [pā]ṟpuṉaṅ

inscription.head:
No. 540.
(A.R. No. 498 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājādhirāja II. Year 14: 1177-78 or 1180-81 A.D.

The details of date given are Mithuṉa ba. 5, Wednesday, Śōdi (Svāti). The
combination of ba. 5 and Svāti is impossible in the month of Mithuṉa.

This records the gift of 1(1/2) vēli and 1 mā of land made free of taxes fixed by
rāja-niyōgam, nāṭṭuvivattai (vyavasthai), ūr-vivattai, etc., as tirunandāviḷakkupuṟam,
by Pāṅganvāy-uḍaiyāṉ alias Rājarāja-mūvēndavēḷāṉ, of Kiḷiyūr for burning a
perpetual lamp to the deity Tirumaṟaikkāḍu-uḍaiyār, in Umbaḷa-nāḍu.

In detailing the boundaries of the land the record mentions a piece of land sold
by the donor to Āḷuḍai Nāchchi Amudāḻvi, (a dēvaraḍiyār), and a piece of land leased
to Arindavaṉ Pallavaraiyaṉ by Chittarāyan.

l.ta:
vatu
mituṉa nāyaṟṟu apara pakṣattup pañcamiyum putaṉ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa [c]oti nāḷ
umpaḷa nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumaṟaikkāṭuṭaiyārkku innāṭṭuk kiḷiyūril pāṅkanvāyuṭai
yāṉ

tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ kiṭaṅkaṉāṉa rā[ja*]rā[ja*] muventaveḷāṉ vaitta tirunontā
viḷakku oṉṟukku ikkiḷiyūril eṉkāṇiyāṉa nilattu viṭṭa nilattukkuk kiḻpāṟ
k[e]llai cittarāyar [kā]ṇiyāy arintavaṉ pallavarayaṉ paṟṟi yiṟuttu varukiṟa nilat
tukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai

edition.l:
karaip[pula]ttellaikku vaṭakkum me[l]pā[ṟkel]lai āḷuṭainācci amutāḻvikku
nāṉ [vi]ṟṟa nilamāy innāyaṉāṟku iva[ḷ] tirunontāviḷakkup puṟamāka viṭṭa
nilattukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟ kellai miṇṭaṉ vāykkālukkut teṟkum naṭuvupaṭṭa
kuḻi 500- ṉāl nilamvam itaṉ ki[ḻakku]-

l.ta:
iṭaiviṭṭu viṭṭa nilattukkuk kiḻpāṟkellai cittarāyar kāṇiyāy arintavaṉ pallavarayaṉ
paṟṟi iṟuttu varukiṟa nilattu(varukiṟa nilattukku) meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai karaip
pil ellaikku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai cittarāyar kāṇiyā yarintavaṉ pallavarayaṉ
paṟṟi iṟukkiṟa nilattukku kiḻakkum [va]-

ṉā[l*]
nilamm māṉa[vā]r karaippula[t]tu viṭṭa nilattukkuk kiḻpāṟkellai vaṭukaṉṟeṉ
nilattukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai vaṭukanṟ[e]ṉ nilattukku vaṭakkum melpāṟ
kellai amutāḻvi nilamāy ivaṉ viṭṭa ni[lat]-

ṭiyāril āḷuṭai nācci amutā-

ḻvi vaitta tirunontāviḷakku oṉṟukku ikkiḷiyūril eṉ pakkal vilai koṇṭu
ṭaiya nilam•• va viṭṭa [kā]raṭi nilattukkuk kiḻpāṟkellai inta irā[ca*]rā
[ca*]muventaveḷāṉeṉ tiru[n]o[ntā]vi[ḷa]kkuppuṟamāka viṭṭa nilattukku meṟkum
teṉpāṟkellai karaippula ellaik[ku vaṭa]kkum-

melpāṟkellai tikka••••• llaikkum karumāṇikka viṇṇakarāḻvār tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam
rā[ja*]rā[ja*] muventaveḷāṉ kāṇiyāṉa [ni]lattukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai
miṇṭaṉ vāykkālukku teṟkum naṭuvu[pa]ṭṭa kuḻi-

karaippulattu viṭṭa nilattukku kiḻpāṟkellai [i]nta rā[ja*]rā[ja*] muventaveḷāṉ tiru
nontāviḷakkuppuṟamāka viṭṭa nilattukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai meṟpaṭiyāṉ kāṇi
yāṉa nilattukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai cittarāyar kāṇiyā yarintavaṉ [pal]lavarayaṉ

paṟṟiyiṟuttu varukiṟa nilattukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai kāraṭi nilattellaikkut teṟkum
naṭuvupaṭṭa kuḻi

pa innilam oṉṟaraiye
oru māvum innāyaṉāṟkut tirunāmattu-

kkāṇiyāka iṟaiyili ceytamaiyil i[n]ta nilattukku ūrtarappaṭi ne[llu] aṟukalaṉe iru
tūṇikkuṟuṇi muṉṉāḻiyum muvuḻakkum [i]nnilattāl vanta [kaṭa]mai kuṭimaikaḷ irāca
niyo[kamu]m nāṭṭu vivattai ūrvivattai-

[paṭi]yum vanta kaṭamai kuṭimai epperp paṭṭaṉavum ivvāṇṭu mutal ivvūril eṉ kāṇiyāṉa
nilattile eṟṟi iṟukkakkaṭavatākavum ivvūril eṉ kāṇiyāṉa nilam eṉ pakkal•••
ṟṟuṭaiyārum koṇṭuṭai[yā*](ṉa)-

rum pirāttikaḷāl peṟuvārum nila viḻukkāṭṭuppaṭiye eṟṟi iṟukkakkaṭavarāka [ce]tu kuṭu[ttu]
ippaṭi cantirātittavaṟ cellakkaṭavatākavum ippaṭi c(ā)mmatittu iṉṉilam
ceytu kuṭutteṉ pāṅka[n]vāyuṭaiyāṉāṉa rājarāja muventa veḷāṉeṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 541.
(A.R. No. 499 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 3: 1218-19 A.D.

This records the sale (āḷvilai-pramāṇa-iśaivu-tīṭṭu) of 10 slaves with their associates
of the Śadaiyāṇḍi-kūṭṭam for 1,000 kāśu by Ariyāṉ Pichchaṉ alias Ediriliśōḻa-
Gaṅgaināḍāḻvān, the kāval officer of Mēṟpuram in Kuṉṟūr-nāḍu to the authorities
of the temple of Tirumaṟaikkāḍu-uḍaiyār. Five of these ten slaves are stated to
have been once sold to the god by Mudaliyār Gaṅgappiḷḷai alias Rājarāja-Gaṅgai-
nāḍāḻvār.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājarā[ja*]tevaṟku yāṇṭu 3- vatu umpaḷaṉāṭṭuk
kuṉṟū[r*]nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumaṟaikkāṭuṭai[yār] k[o]yil

l.ta:
ātitaṇṭecura tevarkaṉmikaḷukku innāṭṭu meṟpuram kāval ariyāṉ piccaṉāṉa etirilicoḻa
kaṅkaināṭāḻvāṉ[eṉ] āḷvilaippiramā-

ṇa icaivu tiṭṭuk kuṭutta paricāvatu eṉ a[ṭi]yāril cataiyāṇṭi [kū]ṭṭattu ve[ta]ṉāṉa paṭṭa
nāyaka kaṇṇappaṉaiyum [ku]rampaṉ ātināyaka-

kkaṇṇappaṉai[yu]m uṭaiyāṉāṉa cokkanāyakak kaṇṇappaṉaiyum kaṅkaṉaiyum tiruvaṉaiyum muṉṉā-
ḷile eṅkaḷ mutaliyār ka[ṅ]kappiḷḷai āṉa rā[ja*]rā[ja*]-

kkaṅkai nāṭāḻvār nāyanāṟku aṭimaiyāka kuṭuttamaiyil ivarkaḷaiyum piṉpu kaḻani āṇṭiyai
yum [p]eṟṟāṉaiyum ā[ṇ]ṭāḷiyaiyum āṇ-

ṭāṉaiyum tirukkaḷammaḻakiyā[ṉaiyu]m ivarkaḷaiyum ivar(ṟka)kaḷ vaṟkkattārai[yu]m viṟṟuk
kuṭuttu koḷvatāṉa kācu āyiramu-

m cipaṇṭārattu kai[k*]koṇṭu ivarkaḷai viṟṟukku[ṭu*]tteṉ ariyāṉ piccaṉāṉa etirili
coḻak kaṅkaināṭāḻvāṉeṉ ippaṭikku ivai-

etirilicoḻak kaṅkai nāṭāḻvāṉeḻuttum coḻaṉ āṉaikāttāṉ āṉa coḻiya varaiyaṉum
kūttaṉ tiruve[ka]mpa muṭaiyāṉ rājarājak kaṅkai nāṭāḻvāṉu[m]
[ca]yaññaiyātaṉamaikkum ippaṭikku ivai ariyanāyakaveḷāṉ eḻuttu u u

inscription.head:
No. 542.
(A.R. No. 500 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 7+1: 1223-24 A.D.

This records the gift of 2 vēli of land in three plots, made tax-free in Kulōttuṅga-
śōḻanallūr in Umbaḷa-nāḍu by Maḻuvāḍi Chelvaṉ of Pūṇḍi for burning a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Tirumaṟaikkāḍu-uḍaiyār.

Among the boundaries of the second plot of land is mentioned the dēvadāṉa land
of Tiruttāṉtōṉṟīśvaram-uḍaiyār of Vēṭkuṉṟūr and among those of the third are
mentioned the piece of land puḻaikkaḍaichchey belonging to Teṉṉavaṉ Mūvēnda-
vēḷāṉ, land in Vāyalūr and a highway (peruvaḻi.)

edition.l:
svastiśrī |||— tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī rājarājatevaṟku yāṇṭu eḻāvatiṉ etirāmāṇṭu
uṭaiyār tirumaṟaikkāṭuṭaiyārkku pūṇṭiyuṭaiyāṉ maḻuvāṭi

l.ta:
celvaṉ vaitta tirunan[tā]viḷakku oṉṟukku umpaḷanāṭṭu kulottuṅkacoḻanallūriṟ
koṇṭu iṟaiyili ceytu viṭṭa nilattukkuk kiḻpāṟkellai

kaṇapati yuḷḷiṭṭār nilattukku meṟkun teṉpāṟkellai kiḻakku meṟkāṉa peruvaḻi oḻukkaikku
vaṭakku melpāṟkellai ciṅkaṉ pulattu vāykkālukku kiḻakkum vaṭa

pāṟkellai kuḷamaṭaic ceykkuṅ kaṇ[ṇa] vetaṉ ceykkut teṟkum āka icainta perunāṉ
kellaiyu[ḷ*] naṭuvupaṭṭa nilam oṉṟarai innilam oṉṟaraiyum iṅku viṭṭuk kuṟicci-

yuṭaiyāṉ periyāṉ paṉantiṭaikku meṟkun teṉ[pā*]ṟkellai veṭkuṉṟūrt tiruttāṉ
toṉṟiccuramuṭaiyār tevatāṉam [ū]ṟ kiḻiṟaiyili pūvāṅkuḻikku vaṭak-

ku melpāṟkellai ku[ṟi]c[ci] yuṭaiyāṉ arai[yaṉ] periyāṉ nilattukkuk kiḻakkum
vaṭapāṟkellai meṟpaṭiyāṉ maṉaic ceykkuṅ kuṭiyirupput toṭṭattuk-

kut teṟkum āka icainta perunāṉkellaiyuḷ naṭuvupaṭṭa māntoṭṭamuṅ kuḷamum
meṭṭucceyyum nilaṅkāle araimā iṅku viṭṭu vāyalūr-

ellaikku teṟkuk kāṭṭup peruvaḻi oḻukkaikkuk kiḻakkut toṭṭamuṅ kuḷattukkuṅ
kiḻpāṟkellai teṉṉavaṉ muventaveḷāṉ puḻaikkaṭai

cceykku meṟkun teṉpāṟ kellai meṟpaṭiyāṉ toṭṭattukku vaṭakkum melpāṟ
kellai peruvaḻikkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai vāyalūr nilattukkut te-

ṟkum āka ic[ait]ta perunāṉkellaiyuḷ naṭuvupaṭṭa nilan nāṉmā varai āka nilam
iruveliyum innilam iruveliyum paṉmarkeccura irakṣai [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 543.
(A.R. No. 501 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 29+1: 1245-46 A.D.

This inscription refers to the invation by Siṅgaṇa Daṇḍanā āka of the Chōḷa
country in the 25th year (1240-41 A.D.) of Rājarāja's reign.

It records the order (tāṉa-niyōgam) of the trustees (sthānattār) of the temple of
Tirukkōḍikkuḻagar in Kuṉṟūr-nāḍu, a division of Umbaḷa-nāḍu conferring certain
honours on Śāttaṉ Bhaṭṭanambi alias Uttamaṉ-Nambi of Atulaparākkiramapuram in
Kāṉattūr-nāḍu, who besides providing a capital of 50,000 kāśu for repairs, undertook
to provide for the re-consecration, daily worship and festivals in the temple, which
had been disturbed and neglected due to the invasion of Siṅgaṇa Daṇḍanāyaka.

l.ta:
vaṟku yāṇṭu irupatton
patāvatin etirāmāṇṭu umpaḷa nāṭṭu kunṟūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirukkoṭikkuḻaka[r]
koyilttāṉattom innāṭṭuk kāṉattūr nāṭṭu atulaparākkiramapu[ra]ttu [cāttaṉ]-

[paṭ]ṭaṉ nampiyāṉa uttamaṉ nampi••••ti•• ku••••tāṉaniyokam paṇṇik kuṭutta
paricāvatu innāyaṉār tirukkoyilil muṉpu irupattaiñcāvatu nāḷil ciṅkaṇṇa
teṇṭunāya[kka]ṉ paṭai naṭantu tecaṅkaḷile cila[kai] taṭṭuṇṭākaiyil piṉpu
āḷuṭaiyaṉāyaṉā-

raiyum nācciyāraiyum eḻuntaruḷuvikka[ppāṟ][t*][ta] viṭattu śrī paṇṭārattu uṭa
lillātapaṭiyāle ittanmattai cilavaṟkku kuṭuttākilum nāyaṉārai eḻuntaruḷu
vittup pūcai [koṇṭa]ruḷap paṇṇaveṇumeṉṟu pāṟ[t*]tu inta utta[ma]
nampi•pakkale tāṉamāka[c ceytu] nī-

rum ummuṭaiya vaṟkattārum inta taṉmattaik kaiykoṇṭu eḻuntaruḷappaṇṇaveṇu
meṉṟu colla ivaṉum ippaṭikku icaintu eḻuntaruḷap paṇṇukaikku veṇṭum
[poru]ḷukku ivar pakkal nāṅkaḷ kaikkoṇṭa kācu

ntamākat tiruppaṇik
kuṟaivaṟac ceytu [kuṭu]ttamaiyil ittirukkoyilile iṉṉāyaṉāraiyum ṉācciyāraiyum
inta [uttama](ma)ṉṉampi tiruppiritaṭṭaiyum paṇṇat tirukkāppu paṇṇi [tiruveḻucci]
uḷḷiṭṭa tirunāḻkaḷum e-

ḻuntaruḷap paṇṇavum pūcaikaḷum tiruvamutuka ḷuḷḷiṭṭaṉavaiyiṟṟukkum tāṉe ceytu
koḷkaiyāle [ti]ru ulāc ceyta[ruḷu]miṭattum pūcaikoṇṭaruḷumiṭattum [pula]var
talaivar•• ṉṟum•• uttamapi••••• tāṉeṉṟumparicciṉṉam pa[ṇi] māṟavum ippaṭi cantirāti-

[ttavaṟ] ivaṉukkum ivaṉuṭai[ya] vaṟkattāṟkum ittiruppaṇi kaṭavatāṉapaṭi[kku]
ippaṭikku icaintu tāṉaṉiyokam paṇṇi[kkuṭutt]om ikkoyil tāṉattom
ippaṭikku••••• vai tevarkaṉmi māteva•• eḻuttu

rakkaṇkāṇi periya tirumaṟaik
kāṭan eḻuttu eṉṟum ippaṭikku tirukkoṭikkuḻakar eḻutteṉṟum tirumaṟaikkāṭu
ṭaiyār koyil tevarkaṉmi kuṉṟur nāṭṭu paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu eṉṟum ikkoyil
kaṇakku ārāṟṟūruṭaiyāṉ e-

rakkaṇkāṇi periyatirumaṟaikkāṭṭup pirāṉ
[eḻuttu] eṉṟum ippaṭi[kku] ivai virikkuḻalaḻakiya mārāyan eḻuttu eṉṟum
[||*]

inscription.head:
No. 544.
(A.R. No. 502 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 24: 1239-40 A.D.

This records the sale and dedication of two slaves Kaḻani and Kuḍiyāḷ by Ariyan
Pichchaṉ alias Gaṅgaināḍāḻvāṉ to god Tirumaṟaikkāḍ-uḍaiyār.

l.ta: yār tirumaṟaikkāṭuṭaiyār koyil āticaṇṭecura tevar kaṉmikaḷukku i[n]nā-
ṭṭu meṟpp[āṭi]kāval a[ri]y[ā*]ṉ piccaṉāṉa kaṅkaināṭāḻvāṉeṉ [e]ṉṉaṭiyā[ril pe]-
ṟṟamakaḷ kaḻaṉiyaiyum kuṭiyāḷaiyum vilaikku viṟṟu cipaṇṭā[rattil]•••
kāṇṭu iva[r*]kaḷai uṭaiyarkku aṭimaiyāka kalveṭṭik kuṭutt[eṉ] [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 545.
(A.R. No. 503 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
Rājēndrachōḷa III. Year 12: 1257-58 A.D.

This seems to record the ūr-niyōgam, given by the residents of Rājēndraśiṅga-
paṭṭiṉam, regarding a gift of land for certain services and offerings and the contribution
of a fish as kārttigai-ppachchai, in the temple of the Tirumaṟaikkāḍu-uḍaiyār by the
members of a body called the Paramamāmuṉi Eḻunūṟṟuvar, who claim to be the
descendants of the sage born of the fire that burnt Kāma (i.e., Manmatha).

l.ta: runtavattoṉ yokaḻippa vekuṇṭeḻunta karuppu villi kāma-

ṉ viḻat tiruneṟṟiyil [tiruk]kāppu nikki vi[ḻi]tta taḻal orumuṉiyā yuyarmuṉivaṉ
pa[ṉi]ccaṭaimuṭip paramamāmuṉi maṭiyiṟ t[o]ṟṟiya parama-

māmuṉi eḻunūṟṟuvarom eṅkaḷ nāyaṉār uṭaiyār tirumaṟaikkāṭuṭaiyār tirumaṭai
viḷākattu vaṭa-

pāṟkellai kaṇṭaṉ periyāṉ vāykkāl niroṭiṉa [ner] kiḻak••••luṟa itaṉ
teṉpāṟkellai toṇṭa[mā]-

ṉ pūṇ[ṭikku] vaṭakkum itaṉ merpāṟkellai appaṉ kuḷamum araiyaṉ aṟu•• kku
ner meṟkuṅ kiḻa•• ṟṟu eṅkaḷ kaṭaṟkāṇiyāṉa teva[r]

uṭaiyār tirumaṟaikkāṭuṭaiyār cipātaṉ tāṅkun tirukkaṇ [uṭai]yar pala vilai koṇ••••
ttuttaṭ•• pattukku mutal āṭṭaikku

[i*]rupatu kācum tirukkāttikaipaccaik koru miṉum ippaṭiyāka
•••• ṉṟu i••••
paṭi cammatittu ūr niyokam paṇṇik kuṭuttom ivvūṟku-

raiyaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai ūrkkaṇakku nūṇūruṭaiyāṉ aravāparaṇaṉ tiru
maṟaikkāṭuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu:—

inscription.head:
No. 546.
(A.R. No. 504 of 1904.)
AGATTIYĀṈPAḶḶI, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Agastyēśvara temple.
Māṟavarmaṉ Vīrapāṇḍya, Year 15.

On palaeographical considerations, this inscription may be assigned to the 13th
century.

This seems to record the gift of 2,000 kuḻi of land by purchase for service and
offerings early in the morning to god Tiruvagattiyāṉpaḷḷi-uḍaiyār in Kuṉṟūr-nāḍu
in Umbar-vaḷanāḍu alias Taraṇimuḻudum-uḍaiya-vaḷanāḍu by Arayaṉ Pōyaṉ Viḻup-
paraiyaṉ, the kiḻavaṉ of Eḻumūr, who is also stated to have reclaimed the land which
was lying fallow. The donor is stated to have deposited in the temple 45 paṇam for
his purpose, out of which 5 paṇam was provided towards the payment of taxes such as
kuḍimai, viṉivōgam, etc.

l.ta: m eṉṟum ivvarici irunāḻi uḻakkum āṇṭu [varai]
vākkum paṇam añcu ippaṇam añcum inta oṭuk-

inscription.head:
No. 547.
(A.R. No. 504-A of 1904.)
AGATTIYĀṈPAḶḶI, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Agastyēśvara temple.
Vikramapāṇḍya.
The date is lost in the damaged portion.

This seems to record the sale of land to the maṭha belonging to the Tiruch-
cheṟṟimuṟṟattu-santānam made free of taxes for 60 paṇams by the Ūravar. Other
details are lost.

l.ta: [pa]ḷa nā(ṭ)āṉa taraṇi[muḻu]tuu•••• ḷanāṭṭu ku[ṉ*]ṟūr nāṭṭu kuṉ-
•••••••• ppa uṭaiyār ti••••• ḷi uṭaiyār tiru-
m maṭattirukku iṉṉāṭṭu•••• ru•• lattu ūravarom nilavi
ippaṭi irācar[ā*]camu••••• eḻu[t]tu i[p*]paṭikku tirumaṟai tāvuṭaiya••••••
ttu ittaṟmi•• ttāṉ eḻuttu [ku]•ṟu•veḷār [e*]ḻuttu caliyāl vetaṇa•• ṉ

inscription.head:
No. 548.
(A.R. No. 505 of 1904.)
AGATTIYĀṈPAḶḶI, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south base of the central shrine in Agastyēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 2: 1218 A.D.

The details of date given, viz., 2nd year, Kumbha, Monday, śu. 2, Śadaiyam,
correspond to 1218 A.D., January 20, Monday, f.d.t., ·13; ·40.

This records the gift of 1,500 aṉṟāḍu-naṟkāśu entrusted into the hands of the
śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple of god Tiruvagattiyāṉpaḷḷi-uḍaiyār in Kuṉṟūr-nāḍu in
Umbaḷa-nāḍu, for a perpetual lamp, by Tiruppaḻaṉam-uḍaiyār alias Virāṭakularāyar
Pākkai Kuḍapulamuḍaiyāṉ Araiyaṉ Ādittadēvaṉ Āditta••••ḻvār of Koṟṟamaṅgalam
in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rajēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
tiri[pu]vaṉaccakkaravart[ti]kaḷ śrī rājarājateva[ṟ]ku yāṇṭu iraṇ[ṭ]āvatu
kumpa nāyaṟṟup pūrva pakkṣa tutikaiyum tiṅkaḷ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa cataiyattu nāḷ
rā[j*]entiracoḻavaḷanāṭṭup puṟaṅkarampai nāṭṭuk koṟṟamaṅkalamuṭaiya•••• yaṉ
tiruppaḻaṉamuṭaiyārāṉa virāṭakularāyar pā[k]kai

kuṭapulamuṭaiyāṉ araiyaṉ ātittatevaṉ ātitta••• ḻa[vaṟ]kku umpaḷa nāṭṭuk-
ku[ṉṟū]r nāṭṭut tiruvakattiyāṉpaḷḷi uṭaiyār koyil muppatu vaṭṭattuk kāṇiyuṭaiya
civappirāmmaṇarom tirunontāviḷakku upaiyaṅkoṇṭa paricāvatu ivvāṭṭai māci
mātattu ittirunantāviḷakku oṉṟukkum upaiyamākap policai
cekuttut tiruviḷakku erikkakkaṭavomāka nāṅkaḷ kaikkoṇṭa aṉṟāṭu naṟkācu

ikkācu āyirattu ainnūṟum kaikkoṇṭu tirunontā viḷakku oṉṟum cantirātittai
varai erikka kaṭavomāka upaiyaṅkoṇṭom ikkoyil muppatuvaṭṭat-

inscription.head:
No. 549.
(A.R. No. 506 of 1904.)
AGATTIYĀṈPAḶḶI, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in Agastyēśvara temple.
Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkhara I. Year 31: 1299 A.D.

The details of date given, viz., 31st year, Ṛishabha, Sunday śu. [9], Uttiram,
correspond to 1299 A.D., May 10, ·18; ·89.

This epigraph records the grant of 5 vēli of land, made free of taxes to god
Tiruvagattiyāṉpaḷḷi-uḍaiyār in Kuṉṟūr-nāḍu, in Umbar-vaḷanāḍu alias Taraṇimuḻud
uḍaiya-vaḷanāḍu, for the celebration of a festival in the month of Vaikāśi, on the last
day of which occurs the star Hasta, for the health of the king by the Nāṭṭavar of Kuṉ-
ṟūr-nāḍu. The land was called Bhāgampiriyāda-nallūr after the name of the god.

l.ta: ṟu vaikāci mātattu a[t]tam tittamāka nāṅkaḷ ka-
ka iṟaiyili ceytu kuṭutta nilamāvatu iṉṉāṭ-
nāṅkaḷ mutalaṭaṅka iṟaiyili ceytu kuṭutta nila-

[ṭṭu] vari ūr vari uḷḷiṭṭu varum kaṭamai kuṭimai uḷḷiṭṭa
eppeṟpaṭṭaṉavum eṅkaḷ nāṭṭile eṟṟi iṟuttu iṉ-
liyāka cantira ātittavaṟ celvatāka iṟaiyili ceytu kuṭu-

yaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai•••••• raiyaṉ e[ḻu*]ttu

inscription.head:
No. 550.
(A.R. No. 507 of 1904.)
AGATTIYĀṈPAḶḶI, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the central shrine in Agastyēśvara temple.
Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkhara I. Year 5: 1273 A.D.

This inscription is dated, year 5, Tai 21. Palaeographically this inscription
may be assigned to the 13th century.

This records the gift of 150 paṇam by Gautaman Āṇḍāṉ Kuṉṟameṟindār, for
Śrī Māhēśvarapūjai in Agattiyadēvar tirumaḍam in the same place, out of the income
accruing from the gift-money either by investing it in tax-free land or by lending
it out for interest. The money was entrusted to the tāṉattār in the temple of
Tiruvagattiyāṉpaḷḷi-uḍaiyār.

l.ta:
tiyati nāḷ uṭaiyār tiruvakattiyāṉpaḷḷi uṭaiyār koyil tāṉattār
vacam ivūr akattiyatevar tirumaṭattil śrī māho

pūcaikku kāṇikoṇṭu iṟaiyili ceytu koṇṭātal mutal niṉṟu po[n*]ta palicaiyāluḷḷa
mutal koṇṭātal cantirātittavar naṭatta tirupper oṉṟukku amutukku

inscription.head:
No. 551.
(A.R. No. 508 of 1904.)
KŌḌIYAKKĀḌU, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Amṛitaghaṭēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 14: 1229-30 A.D.

On palaeographical considerations this epigraph may be assigned to Rājarāja
III.

This seems to record a gift for a perpetual lamp for god Tirukkōḍi-kkuḻagar
in Kuṉṟūr-nāḍu in Umbaḷa-nāḍu by a native of Nandinallūr in Tirunaṟaiyūr-
nāḍu. Other details are lost as the inscription is partly built in.

l.ta: ṟṟu umpaḷanāṭṭukkuṉṟūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirukkoṭikkuḻakaṟku i••••

inscription.head:
No. 552.
(A.R. No. 509 of 1904.)
KŌḌIYAKKĀḌU, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Amṛitaghaṭēśvara temple.
Sundarapāṇḍya.

The title of the king and the date have been lost in the beginning of the
inscription which is built in.

This seems to record some provision made for the excavation of a veḍi (?) in the
temple by the Paradavar of kaḍalvaṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 553.
(A.R. No. 510 of 1904.)
KŌḌIYAKKĀḌU, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Amṛitaghaṭēśvara temple.
Jaṭāvarmaṉ-Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ. Year 18.
Palaeographically this inscription may be assigned to the 14th century.

This inscription records an order of the king arranging for the surplus of 55 poṉ,
left after spending for the services till the 17th year in the temples of Tirukkōḍik-
kuḻagar in Umbaḷa-nāḍu and Tiruvagattiyāṉpaḷḷi-uḍaiyār and another 63 poṉ, on
which there were some charges, to be utilised as capital for the services, repairs and
other services in those temples from the 18th year.

l.ta: koneriṉmai koṇṭāṉ umpaḷa nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirukkoṭikkuḻaka[r]
koyil tāṉattāru[m*] tiruva[ka]ttiyāṉpaḷḷi uṭaiyār koyil tāṉattāru-
taṅkaḷ koyil patineḻāvatu varai[p]pūcai mitti iṟuttuppon[ta]
kkum tiruppaṇikkun pala nima[n]taṅkaḷukkum uṭalāka mutalaṭaṅ[ka] kaḻitto[m]

inscription.head:
No. 554.
(A.R. No. 511 of 1904.)
KŌḌIYAKKĀḌU, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Amṛitaghaṭēśvara temple.
Jaṭāvarmaṉ Parākramapāṇḍya. Year 5
On grounds of palaeography the inscription may be assigned to the 13th century.

This inscription records a gift of 150 varāhaṉ for a perpetual lamp for god
Tirukkōḍikkuḻagar in Umbaḷa-nāḍu alias Taraṇimuḻudumuḍaiya-vaḷanāḍu by
Gamudavaṉ Āṇḍāṉ Peṟṟā[ṉ*].

edition.l: panma[ rāṉa tribhu[va*][na]cca[k*]karava-

inscription.head:
No. 555.
(A.R. No. 512 of 1904.)
KŌḌIYAKKĀḌU, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Amṛitaghaṭēśvara temple.
Jaṭā (Māṟavarmaṉ) Kulaśēkhara. Year 39: 1307 A.D.

The high regnal year seems to indicate that the surname Jaṭāvarmaṉ
is a mistake for Māṟavarmaṉ. The details of date given viz. 39th year,
Kumbha, śu are insufficient for verification.

This inscription seems to record a sale of land. Other details are
lost as the record is built in at the right end and is incomplete.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] koccaṭapaṉmar tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulacekaratevarkku yāṇṭu 39-
āvatu kumbhanāya[ṟṟu] pūrvva[pa*]kṣattu catu•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 556.
(A.R. No. 513 of 1904.)
KŌḌIYAKKĀḌU, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Amṛitaghaṭēśvara temple.
Rājēndra III. Year 3[2]: 1276 A.D.
The details of date, viz., Kumbha śu. are insufficient for verification.

This inscription seems to record a sale of land to Ediroppilādār alias Sōmanātha-
dēvarA.R. Ep., 1911, pārt ii, para. 31.of the lineage of Mudaliyār of [Tiruchchatti]l-muṟṟam by the authorities of
the temple of god Tirukkōḍikkuḻagar for the raising of a garden called Tiruvāy-
pāḍi.

l.ta:
karaikkaṭaippu uṭaiyār tirukkoṭikkuḻakar koyil āticaṇṭecuratevarkaṉmika[ḷ]
ḷom•••••

eti[r*] oppillātār āṉa comaṉā[ta*]tevar
muta(l)liyārkku na•••••••

iṟṟaināḷā•••••

ātit•••••

••veli•••• teṟ

inscription.head:
No. 557.
(A.R. No. 514 of 1904.)
KŌḌIYAKKĀḌU, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Amṛitaghaṭēśvara temple.
Rājēndra III. Year 32: 1276 A.D.

The details of date given, viz., 32nd year, Makara ba. Thursday, Rēvati corres-
ponds to 1276 A.D. January 23.

This inscription records that in exchange for the land formerly granted for the
maintenance of the garden called Tiruvāypāḍi (cf. No. 556 above) which was
given over to Sēnāpatigaḷ, another plot of land called maiyārtaḍaṅgaṇṇi-mayakkal
was granted by Nambikuḍiyaṉ alias Gaṅgaināṭṭukkōṉ for his own merit.

edition.l:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājentiracoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 32- vatu [maka]ranāyaṟṟu apa
rapakṣa[ttu] viyāḻakkiḻa[m]ai peṟṟa revati nāḷ umpaḷa nāṭṭuk kuṉṟur nāṭṭu uṭai
yār tirukkoṭik[ku]ḻakar

ratevar kaṉmikaḷom iṉṉāyaṉār koyil muppatu vaṭṭattuk
kāṇiyuṭaiya [ci]vappirāmaṇarom upaiyat[tiṭṭu kuṭu]tta paricāvatu iṉṉāyaṉār teva
tāṉam uḷḷur tiru-

nāmattukkāṇiyil muṉṉāḷ irupatteḻāvatu nāḷil ivūrilirukkum nampi kuṭiyaṉāṉa
kaṅkai nāṭ[ṭu]k koṉ [ta]ṉakku taṉmamāka paṇṭa••••• vāypāṭiyāṉa tirunantavaṉat
tukku tiruttoṇ[ṭu] ceyki-

kiḻakku ivaṉ kācu oṭukkikkoṇṭu kalveṭṭiṉa ••••••• ṉilam veliyum ceṉāpati

ḷakkum aricikku i viḻukkāṭ[ṭil]•••• [kayi]•••••••••
[mattaṟu]•[ṭaiya]•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 558.
(A.R. No. 515 of 1904.)
KĪVAḶŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Akshayaliṅgēśvara temple.
Rājarāja II. Year 18: 1163 A.D.

The details of date given, viz., 18th year, Dhanus ba. 8, Sunday, Hastam,
correspond to 1163 A.D., December 20, Friday (not Sunday) ·79; ·20.

This inscription records the grant by purchase of land called Śendāmaraik-
kaṇṇaṉ-kollai situated in Śāttaṅguḍi, a hamlet of Periyālattūr alias Kshatriyaśikhā-
maṇi-chchaturvēdimaṅgalam for rearing a garden for god Kēḍili-nāyaṉār by Śrīmūlat-
tāṉam-uḍaiyāṉ-bhaṭṭaṉ of Kuravaśēri worshipping god Tiruvārūr-uḍaiyār in Tiruvārūr-
kūṟṟam in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu and Āvuḍiyāḷchchāṉi, wife of Perumbaṟṟap-
puliyūr Viṉāyaka-bhaṭṭaṉ, a devotee of this same god. The latter is stated to have
had the former as her guardian (mudukaṇ). Provision was also made for the resi-
dence of the āṇḍār tending the garden.

l.ta:
ṟṟukkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa atta[t*]tu nāḷ keyamāṇikkavaḷa[ṉā]ṭṭu tiruvārūr kūṟṟattu
uṭaiyār tiruvāruruṭaiyārai pūcik[ku]m kuravaceri cimula-

ttāṉa muṭaiyāṉ paṭṭaṉum ivaṉaiye mutukaṇṇāka uṭaiya ināyaṉārai pūcikkum
perumpaṟṟappuliyūr viṉāyakapaṭṭaṉ pirāma-

[ṇi] āvuṭaiyāḷaccāṉiyum iviruvo[m*] uṭaiyār keṭilināyaṉārkku nāṅkaḷ tirunanta
vaṉam ceyya viṭṭa puṉce[y*]kkellai ā-

ḷai
yil māttūr kiḻavar kāṇi[y]ai i(ru)pperuvilai k[o]-

ṇṭa kuravaceri civalokanāyakapaṭṭaṉum tū[ya]vāmaṉapaṭṭaṉ makaṉ koṉaippaṭṭaṉ pakkalum
vilaikoṇṭu viṭṭa centāmaraikkaṇṇaṉ kollai[yu*]m ittiruna[n*]tavaṉam
ce[y*]yum āṇ-

ṭārkaḷukkuk kuṭi irukka [ūṭum] ivūr•••• ṟkkaṭai[ya] koṭṭuruṭaiyāṉ āḷum
araiyar [ū]ṟṟukkeṇikku vaṭakku uttecapaṭi maṉai kuḻi i[rupa]tta[ñ]cum ippaṭi
uḷḷapaṭi kol[lai]

inscription.head:
No. 559.
(A.R. No. 516 of 1904.)
KĪVAḶŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the prākāra of the Akshayaliṅgēśvara temple.
Tuljārājirājē Bhōsle. Ś. 1697-1775 A.D.

This record in Marāṭhī, written in Nāgarī, is dated in Śaka 1697, Manmatha,
Vaiśākha 1 and states that the prākara was built by Rājēśrī Tuljā-rājirājē Bhōslē
Sāhēb.

inscription.head:
No. 560.
(A.R. No. 517 of 1904.)
KĪVAḶŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the Sundarakuchāmbā shrine in the Akshayaliṅgēśvara
temple.
Rājarāja II. Year 10: 1155 A.D.

On palaeographical considerations, this inscription may be assigned to Rāja-
rāja II. The details of date, viz., 10th year, Mēsha, ba. 4, Friday, Mūlam, regularly
correspond to 1155 A.D., April 22, Friday, ·85, ·64.

This inscription records some arrangements made for laying a sannadhi-tiruvīdi
in front of the temple of god Kēḍili-nāyaṉār of Kīḻvēḷūr, by Arayaṉ Śiṅgapirāṉ of
Kīḻakkuḍi and Nārāyaṇadēvaṉ Śavuri-perumāḷ.

l.ta:
vaṟkku yāṇṭu pa[t]tāvatu meṣa
nāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu

yum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa mulattunāḷ keyamāṇikka
vaḷanāṭṭu•••••
yam kiḻveḷur uṭaiyār keṭi[li]nāyaṉār koyil tiripuvaṉa ā

vakaṉmi[ka]ḷom iṉṉāṭṭu kiḻakkuṭaiyāṉ arayaṉ ciṅkapirāṉārkkum n[ārā]yaṇa
tevaṉ cavuriperumāḷukkum tāṉa••••

ta paricāvatu nāyaṉārkku muṉpu caṉṉati ti[ru]vitiyilāmaiyil [ca]ṉṉatitiruviti kiḻakke
[vi]ṭṭu kiḻaittiruvitiyum teṟkku nokki muṉpu eḻuṉtaruḷi aruḷukiṟa tiruviti
yile uṟaviṭa veṇṭukaiyāle taṅkaḷ kāṇiyāṉa akamaṉaippuḻa

inscription.head:
No. 561.
(A.R. No. 518 of 1904.)
TĒVŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Dēvapurīśvara temple.
Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya II. Year 1[1]: 1287 A.D.

On grounds of palaeography the king Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya may be identified
with the second king of that name and the details of date given, viz., 1[1]th year, Vṛiś-
chika, ba. 6, Wednesday, Pūśam, correspond to 1287 A.D., October 29, Wednesday.

This inscription records the grant of lands after purchasing the same from the
temple for 150 paṇam, to god Tiru-Ādittīśvaram-uḍaiyār in Tiruttēvūr in Tēvūr-
nāḍu in Arumoḻidēva-vaḷanāḍu, as capital for performing the Āṭkoṇḍanāyakaṉ-sandhi
named after the donor, just after the early morning service, by Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷaṉ,
Āṭkoṇḍanāyakaṉ of Teṉguḍi, a lease-holder in Mēnilappāḍi alias Vīrarājēndrapuram
in Tiruvārūr-kūṟṟam in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu. The temple authorities undertook
to conduct the service even in times of floods and draught.

This money received as price for the land was utilised by the temple authorities
to construct in stone the structure above the kumudaga-ppaḍai and kōvaḷam in the
tiru-agra-maṇḍapam.

l.ta: cantiyāka āṭkoṇṭa nāyaka[ṉ] cantiyeṉṟu āṭi aruḷi amutu
viḷakkeṇṇai amutupaṭi arici patakku kaṟi amutuḷḷiṭṭa viñ-
ṇṭu ippaṭi celvatāka kalveṭṭik kuṭutta piṭāriko[yi]lp-
peruvaḻikku kiḻakku cāṭkaiy[e]ṉṟum māmpaṭukai eṉṟum per kū-
ṉāl āka upa innilam iraṇṭe orumāvukkum
ippaṇam nūṟṟa aimpatum iṉāyaṉār koyil tirua-
kkiṟa maṇṭapam muṉpu tirukkaṟceytu niṉṟa ku[mu]takappaṭaikku mel
tirukkaṟccātta vā[ṅ]ki kovaḷattu kallaṭippikkavum paṭa[vu] kūlikkum
[kaḷom] innilaṅkaḷ peruveḷḷam peruñcāvi pokilum [i ni]vanta muṭ
ṭāmal celuttakkaṭavomākavum ippaṭikku ivai tevarkaṉmi īc[ā]ṉapa-

inscription.head:
No. 562.
(A.R. No. 519 of 1904.)
TĒVŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Dēva-
purīśvara temple.
Dēvarāya II, Ś. 1347: 1426 A.D.

The details of date given, viz., Śaka 1347, Viśvāvasu, Tai 15, correspond to
1426 A.D., January 10, Thursday.

This inscription seems to record the settlement made by Periya Śirupparaśar
fixing the amount of the levy called magamai, payable by those, to whom the taxes
on the classes of villages such as paṉḍāravāḍai, iṟai-ilippaṟṟu, amaram, and likhita-
jīvitam were leased out. It appears that the taxes were being collected without
reference to the prevailing prices. Some settlements seem to have been made
previously by Dēvargaḷnāyaṉ alias Bukkaṇa Uḍaiyar. It also mentions one
Aṇṇappayyaṉ, who seems to have come to Tiruvārūr, to conduct an enquiry and
Kaṇṇimandanāyaṉ, who bid a high rate for being given the right to collect these taxes.
It is also stated that the Chikka Dēvar laid down that the members of the Valaṅgai
and Iḍaṅgai could collect I panam per mavaṉ (son) in each community.

edition.l: aruḷāniṉṟa cakā[btam] 1347- ṉ melc

l.ta: tu periya cirupparacar tiruvārur ucāvaṭi akappaṟ[ṟu]
ḷiṭṭa nāṭu nāṭṭavaṟku [ni]rupam [|*] vūrkku naṉtaṉa va-
ruṣam mallāyi avvaiyār perāle makamai āka
kku kā[cu] iraṇṭum āka iṟu[k]kumpaṭikkuk kaṭṭaḷai paṇ
ṟukku nellu [mu]kkuṟuṇi vi[laiy]āka viṟṟapaṭiyāle inta vilai-
yile irekai viḻuntu piṉpu varuṣantoṟum paṇam oṉṟuk[ku]
nellu irukalamum [mu]kkalamum āka viṟkaiyileyum na[n*]taṉa varuṣa
vaiy[ā*]r atikārikaḷukku mutal varuṣattukku irunūṟupoṉ kuttokai
koṇṭu vantā[r]kaḷ [|*] itukku kuttokai koṇṭa atikārikaḷ
nāṭṭile aṉṉiyāyattile iraṭṭikka ilakkam eṭuttu nāṭu
nalaṅkitteṉṟum anta maṉmantiram poṉapiṉpu cāṟuvari varuṣa[tti]-
kaṇṇimantanāyaṉ intak kuttokai veḷḷai eṇṇūṟu p[o]-
li veḷḷai iraṇṭāyiram poṉṉākat tāṉ kuttokai ākak-
varuṣavaraikku iṟuttapa[ṭi]āle perukka nalaṅkiṉom eṉṟum i[tu]
oḻintum muṉpe tevarkaḷ nāyeṉāṉa pukkaṇa uṭaiyar kālatti
[ru]r ṉataṅketi eṉṟu itukku[ccikka]tevar i[ṭ]ṭuk kuṭukkaveṇum e-
ṉac coṉṉapaṭiāle valaṅkai iṭaṅkaivarkku cā[ti]toṟum mavaṉ oṉṟukkuk kāl paṇam

ākak koḷḷumpaṭikku kaṭṭaḷai paṇṇikkuṭuttu ammariyātiyile avaṉukku [naṭa]nta varaiyum
[naṭantu]••••

inscription.head:
No. 563.
(A.R. No. 520 of 1904.)
TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the central shrine in the Āmalakēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 21: 1236-37 A.D.

On palaeographical considerations this inscription can be assigned to
Rājarāja III.

It records a grant of land, made tax-free by the sthānattār of
the temple for burning a lamp at the Kulachchiṟai maThaThe maTha and the guhai had been evidently named after the Pāṇḍya minister Kulachchiṟai.
and the saint TiruñAṉasambandhar, both of whom lived in about the 7th century A.D.situated in
the tirumaḍaiviḷAgam to the north of the temple of Tirunellikkā-uḍaiyār.
It mentions TiruñAṉasambandan guhai, The maTha and the guhai had been evidently named after the Pāṇḍya minister Kulachchiṟai.
and the saint TiruñAṉasambandhar, both of whom lived in about the 7th century A.D.which was probably a monastery
(See No. 564 below).

vatu uṭaiyār tiru
nellikkā uṭaiyār koyil vaṭaciṟakiṟ tirumaṭaiviḷākattu kiḻ[talai]yi[l] kūttāṭu(m)
māṇṭār irukkiṟa kulacciṟai maṭam tiripuvanaviricirattu

viḷakku tarmamāka veṇum me[ṉ*]ṟu ū[r*]potuvāna nilat[tu] iṭṭa••••

vatikku kiḻakku uṭaiyār śrīkayilā[ca]muṭaiyār tirumaṭaiviḷākattu tiruñ[ā]nacampantan [kukai]
kku talai[m]māṟu viṭ[ṭa nilattu]kku kiḻa-
kkaṭaiy[ā]y ni[ṅka] viṭṭu nāyanā[r] iṟai[yi*]liyāka anupavittu varukiṟa viḷākamum kuḻi

inscription.head:
No. 564.
(A.R. No. 521 of 1904.)
TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Āmalakēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 20: 1235-36 A.D.

This inscription records the grant of land for a garden to god Tirunellikkā-
uḍaiyār, by Maṟaiñāṉasambandar and Āḻittērvittagar, residing at the Tiruñāṉa-
sambandan-tiruguhai. It mentions Kuṟukkai Purandaraguru-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

l.ta:
patāvatu [u]ṭaiyār
tirunellikkā [āḷu](u)ṭaiyār tevartāṉattuk kāṇi uṭaiya tiṭṭaicceriuṭai-

yāṉ tillaiperumāṉum ivar tampi kaṇṭarātitta teva[nu]m ivaṉtampi uyya[van]tapiḷḷaiyum
pakkal ikkoyil tāṉattār tiru nāmattu[k*]kāṇiyāka koṇṭu tiruñāṉacampantaṉ

tirukkukaiyil irukku[m*] maṟaiñāṉacampa[nta*][ṉu]m āḻitter vittakarum [ta]ṅkaḷ piyaril tiri
viṭuvittu koṇṭu tirunantavaṉamāka viṭukiṟa nattam uvaccaṉkālukku teṟkku tirunāmat

tu kāṇikku naṭuvupaṭṭa oḻukkaikkum vaṭanattam kuḻi aṟupatteṭṭukkum [piramāṇa]vilaippaṭi
kācu āyirattinūṟum cipaṇṭārattu tāṉattārvacam oṭukki i[ṉ]ṉilaṅkaḷum kuḻi

inscription.head:
No. 565.
(A.R. No. 522 of 1904.)
TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine, Āmalakēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 20: 1235-36 A.D.

This inscription seems to refer to some grant of land for food-offerings to god Iḷaiya-
piḷḷaiyār (Subrahmaṇya) by a certain Maṟaiñāṉasambandar.

inscription.head:
No. 566.
(A.R. No. 523 of 1904.)
TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Āmalakēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 8+1: 1225 A.D.

The details of date given viz, year, 8+1 Kumbha śu. 14, Sunday, Pūram correspond
to 1225 A.D., February 23, Sunday, ·75, f.d.n. ·30. The Kumbha month seems to
have ended that day as the details correspond to the 1st of Mīna. It is also stated
in the body of the inscription that the gift was taken over in Paṅgūṉi month.

This epigraph records a gift of 400 kāśus for a lamp for Iraṇavīra sandi for god
Tirunellikkāvuḍaiyār in Ārvala-kkūṟṟam in Irājēndra••••-vaḷanāḍu, by
Āṇḍavaṉ Śelkalāḻvāṉ of Ārūr. The money was taken over by Śivaṉaṉ Tiru-
nellikkāvuḍaiyāṉ alias Vīraśōḻa Bhaṭṭan, a śivabrāhmaṇa of the temple for execut-
ing the wishes of the donor.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīirājarājateva[r]kku yāṇṭu eṭṭāvatiṉ
etirāmāṇṭu kumpanāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu caturteciyum nāyaṟṟukkiḻamai-

yum peṟṟa pūrattunāḷ irācentira[coḻa]vaḷanāṭṭu ārvalakkūṟṟattu uṭaiyār tiru
nellikkāvuṭaiyār koyil kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmmaṇaṉ pāra-

ttuvāci civaṉaṉ tirunellikkāvuṭaiyāṉāṉa vi[racoḻapaṭṭa]neṉ ivvūril irukkum ārūr
uṭaiyāṉ āṇṭavaṉ [celkal]āḻvāṉukku upaiyattiṭṭuk kuṭutta paricāvatu iva-
ṉ pakkal ivvāṇṭaip paṅkūṉi mā[tatti]l nāṉ koṇṭa kācu

nūṟuṅ kaikkoṇṭu ikkācāl vanta policai[kkācu]kku iraṇaviracantikku oru tiruviḷakku
iṉṉāyanārka

inscription.head:
No. 567.
(A.R. No. 524 of 1904.)
TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north and east walls of the central shrine in the Āmalakēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III (?) Year [·3]+1.

The portion containing the date is damaged. It is stated that the grant was
made on some day in Paṅgūṉi.

This inscription seems to record a sale of land as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple
of god Tirunellikkā-uḍaiyār, receiving 1000 kāśus, by Śokki, wife of Tamiḻavēlāṉ of
•••• ṭṭaiyanallūr who had Tillaināyakaṉ as her guardian and another woman
whose identity is not clear.

[kkāvu]ṭaiyār teva•••• ṭṭaiyanallū[ru]ṭaiyān tamiḻaveḷāṉ pāriyai cok[ki]
tillaināyakaṉai mutukaṇṇāka koṇṭu ivanu-

••• peruntiruvāṭṭi••••••••• yum nā[ṅ]kaḷ iṉṉāya
nāṟkku tirunāmattukāṇiyāka viṟṟukuṭutta••• ca[ṉa]karanilam pa

inscription.head:
No. 568.
(A.R. No. 524-A of 1904.)
TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north and east walls of the central shrine in the Āmalakēśvara temple.

This fragmentary record seems to register a grant of land, for repairs and food
offerings to goddess Tirukkāmakōṭṭam-uḍaiya Periyanāchchiyār whose image i
stated to have been set up by••••nallūr-uḍaiyā[ṉ*] Tirunellikkāvudaivān
Tamiḻavēḷāṉ (See No. 567 above).

l.ta: tiruppaṇikkum tiruvamutukku[m*] uṭal ivu[ri]l en kāṇiyāna tevatāṉattu ivan
kāṇiyāna nilattu viṭṭa uvaccaṉkāl [ka]ṇṭattu nilam mu[ṉ*]ṟumāvum eṇṇaice[y*]
nilam orumāvaraiyum tiruvaṟttacāmapuṟam iraṇṭu mākāṇiyum teṟkku ceruvil te

inscription.head:
No. 569.
(A.R. No. 525 of 1904.)
TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north and east walls of the central shrine in the Āmalakēśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 7: 1184-85 A.D.

This inscription is dated in the 7th year of Tribhuvaṉachakravartin Vīrarājēndra-
dēva. As this title Tribhuvanachakravartin is not found with the name of the Chōḷa
kings earlier than Kulottuṅga I and as Kulōttuṅga III himself had the title of Vīrarājēn-
dra, the king may be identified as Kulōttuṅga III.

This records the gift of 160 kāśu for a perpetual lamp to the god in the temple by
Tirunaṭṭamāḍi Āṭkoṇḍanāyakaṉ alias Ediriliśōḻa Iḍaiyaḷanāṭṭu-kkōṉ, the head man
of Kuṉṟalūr.

l.ta:
vaṟku yāṇṭu eḻāva••••
•••• maiyum peṟṟa [pūcattunāḷ] irāje

coḻavaḷanāṭṭu ārva[lak kūṟṟattu]

nāyakaṉāṉa etirilicoḻa iṭaiyaḷanāṭṭukkoṉ vaitta tiruṉuntāviḷakku oṉṟukku nāḷ
oṉṟukku eṇṇai uḻakkāka [nāḷ] muṉṉūṟṟu aṟupattaiñcukku eṇṇai toṇṇūṟṟu
orunāḻi uḻa[k]kukku ivar upaiyamākat tanta kācu

ikkācu nūṟṟaṟupatukkup
policai cekuttu ikkoyilār muppatuvaṭṭattukkāṇiyuṭaiya civa

neti tirunaṭṭa[m]āṭiyāṉa atik[ai]nāyakapaṭṭaṉuḷḷiṭṭomum kavicikaṉ
mātavan uyyavantāṉāṉa aḻakaviṭaṅkapaṭṭaṉum

paṭṭaṉuḷḷiṭṭomum ikkuṭiyil [ci]vannelliyāṉa tevar kaṇṭapaṭṭaṉuḷḷuṭṭomum

ikkuṭiyil kamalākaraṉ paḷḷikoṇṭāṉuḷḷiṭṭomum āka ivaṉaiv[o]m••••

yā[ru]m ippaṭi [kaṭav]omāka

veṭṭivittom ivaṉaivom i[ppa]ṭikku ivai

paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu:—itu

kūttaṉ[īca]ṉāṉa puravari paṭṭaṉ taṉamaikku iṉṉāyaṉār
koyilil māṭāpattiyañ ciṟṟāmū[ru*]ṭaiyarn tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ tirunellikkāvuṭai

ikkoyilil nimantakkāṇiyuṭaiya kūttāṭuvāṉ tiru

inscription.head:
No. 570.
(A.R. No. 526 of 1904.)
TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Āmalakēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 31: 1246-47 A.D.

This damaged and incomplete inscription seems to record a grant of land to god
Tirunellikkāvuḍaiyār by Arayaṉ Tillaiperumāṉ of Tiṭṭaichchēri and his brothers Ga[ṇḍarā
dittadē]vaṉ and Uyyavandāṉ. It also mentions the land of a certain Tamiḻavēḷāṉ
(see No. 567 above) as the western boundary of apparently the granted land.
Read saṅjñaiyāṉamaikku.

l.ta: vatu tiṭṭaicceri uṭaiyāṉ arayaṉ ti[l]lai perumāṉum ivan tampi ka•••• vanu-

m ivan tampi uyyavantāṉum uṭaiyā[r] tiru[nellikkāvu]ṭai[yārk]ku nāṅka[ḷ]•••
••• kaḷ kāṇi••••[ru]m putuvūr pā•ti[run]elli[k*] kāvil vaṭak[kiṟ] teru
vil vaṭa••••• ṭiiru[ppu]

••••• m ā[ḷu]ṭaiyā[r] ku[ṭi] i[ruppu maṉai kuḻi]••
••••• kiṟa tevankuṭi iruppu maṉai kuḻi

[tevar]tāṉam putuvūr uḷḷūrkaḷ iruvariyil nilatta•••ḻ
•••• ttuk[ku] i•••• ṇiyāṉa•••• •• kaṇ•••

i pa••••[tami]ḻaveḷāṉ nilattukku kiḻakku [putuvūr]••••ku•
••kuṟumpur kiḻavaṉ pakkal••••
l.note: This inscription is incomplete and much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 571.
(A.R. No. 527 of 1904.)
TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Āmalakēśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 4: 1219 A.D.

The details of date given viz., 4th year, Makara ba. 7••• Hasta, corres-
ponds to 1219 A.D., December 29, Sunday, f.d.t. ·02, ·62.

This inscription seems to record a gift of 500 kāśu for a sandhi lamp in the night
to the temple, by Anantan Ki(Kṛi)shṇaṉ alias••• gaināṭṭu-vēḷār, the headman
of Kalaiyūr. This amount was lent out for interest at the annual rate of 24 per cent
to 3 śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple of god Tirunellikkāvuḍaiya-nāyaṉār in Ārvala-
kkūṟṟam in Rājēndraśōḻā-vaḷanāḍu. Arayan Tillaipperumān, Arayan Gaṇḍarādit-
tadēvaṉ, and Arayan Uyyavandā[ṉ*] (mentioned as brothers in No. 570) figure as
signatories.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattika[ḷ śrīrāja]rājatevaṟku yāṇṭu nālāvatu makara
nāyaṟṟu apara••••• ḻamaiyu[m]

miyum peṟ[ṟa*] atta[t]tu nāḷ irāje[ntirac]oḻa vaḷanāṭṭu ār[va*]lak kūṟ
ṟattu uṭaiyār tirunelli[k]kā[vuṭaiyār] kovil kāṇi u-

maṇaṉ pā[rattu]vāci civaṉaṉ tirunellikkāvuṭaiyāṉāṉa viracoḻappa
[ṭ*]ṭaṉum (uyya) uy[ya] vantāṉ tirunel-

lik[kā]vu[ṭaiyā]ṉāṉa [n]e[lli]paṭṭaṉum ivaṉ tampi tirunaṭṭamāṭi paṭṭaṉum imuvom
ivūril irukkum kalaiyūr kiḻavaṉ aṉantaṉ ki-

[ṣṇaṉā]ṉa•• kaināṭṭu ve[ḷār]kku nāṅkaḷ canti viḷakku upaiyat tiṭṭuk kuṭutta paricāvatu
iṟṟai nāḷāl nāṅkaḷ ivaṉ pakkal ko-
ka vanta kācu policai po[li]vatākavum intap policaiyāl kācukku iṉṉāyaṉār koyilil
nāḷ [oṉṟu]kku oru tiruviḷakku iravaic cantiyile iṭakkaṭavomākavum inta mutal [k]ā-

cukke policaik kācukku cantirāti[t*]tavaraiyum inta oru tiruviḷakkum iravaiccantiyil[e*]
•••••
kuṭuttom imuvom [|*] ippaṭikku ivai tiṭṭaicceri uṭaiyā
n arayan tillaipperumān eḻuttu:—ippaṭikku ivai tiṭṭai-
cceri uṭaiyān arayan kaṇṭarātitta tevaṉ eḻuttu:—ippaṭi-
kku ivai tiṭṭaicceri uṭaiyān arayan uyyavantā
l.note: A few letters are lost at the end.

inscription.head:
No. 572.
(A.R. No. 527-A of 1904.)
TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Āmalakēśvara temple.

This fragmentary inscription seems to record a grant of land. Other details are
lost.

l.ta: uṉṉatattu arumo[ḻi]tevaṉ tiruvitikai vākkālukku meṟku kuḻi
teṟkil teruvil teṉ ciṟakil taramilikku kiḻakku kuḻi
