kiḻavaṉ pa[kkal vi]lai ko••••
ita[ṉ*] mekku taramilikku meṟku taramilikku kiḻakku kuḻi

inscription.head:
No. 573.
(A.R. No. 528 of 1904.)
TIRUTTEṄGŪR, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Rajatagirīśvara temple.
Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkhara (I). Year 31: 1298-99 A.D.

This inscription is dated in the 31st year of Tribhuvanachakravartin Māṟavarman
Kulaśēkhara. The high regnal year points to the identity of the king with
Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara I.

It records the gift of paddy and money to god Tiruveḷḷiyaṅguṉṟam-uḍaiya-
nāyaṉār of Tirutteṅgūr for a service called Māḷavaṉ-sandi by Alaivāyuganda Perumāḷ
alias Māḷa[va*]chchakravattigaḷ, son of Nārasiṅgadēvar.

l.ta: ṉār koyil pūcaimikutikku koḷḷukiṟa nellu muṉ-
ṉa māḷavaṉ cantikku kaḻitta nellu irunūṟṟu ka-
lamum paṇam muppatum nellum paṇamum peṟṟu

inscription.head:
No. 574.
(A.R. No. 529 of 1904.)
TIRUTTEṄGŪR, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Rajatagirīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 20: 1235-36 A.D.

This inscription refers to a grant made in the 30th year (1208 A.D.) of Periya-
dēvar Tribhuvaṉavīradēvar i.e., Kulōttuṅga III.

This epigraph records the undertaking given by the śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple
of Tiruveḷḷiyaṅguṉṟam-uḍaiyār to continue to burn the lamp for which they received
4000 kāśu from Kuruppuḻāṉ Tiruvēkambamuḍaiyāṉ Tiruchchōṟṟuttuṟai-uḍaiyāṉ
in the 30th year of the reign of Tribhuvanavīradēva. The lamp given by the
donor is said to have weighed 200 palam. The donor is also mentioned in No. 575
below.

l.ta:
ṟuttuṟai uṭaiyāṉ uṭaiyār tiruveḷḷiyaṅkuṉṟa muṭaiyār koyi[lil] peri
[ya*]tevar tiripuvaṉavīratevarkku muppatāvatu vait-

ta tirunantāviḷakku oṉṟukku ikkoyil muppatu vaṭṭamum kāṇi uṭaiya [ci]vappirā
maṇarom annāḷil upaiyamākak kaikko-

ikkācu nālāyiramum annāḷil kaikkoṇṭu tirunantāviḷakku [e]rit
tuvarukiṟapaṭiye cantirātittavarai erikka-

kkaṭavomākac cammatittom muppatu vaṭṭamum kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmaṇar[o]m
ittiruviḷakku [e]riya ivaṉ ceyvittu iṭ

inscription.head:
No. 575.
(A.R. No. 530 of 1904.)
TIRUTTEṄGŪR, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Rajatagirīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 38: 1215-16 A.D.

This inscription is dated in the 38th year of Tribhuvanachchakravarttigaḷ Tribhu-
vanavīradēva, who took Madurai, Karuvūr, Īḻam and the Pāṇḍya's crowned head and
who performed the vijaiyābhishēkam, and vīrābhishēkam, who is evidently identical
with Kulōttuṅga III.

It records an exchange of the land called Tirumeykāval in Śivapādaśēkhara-
nallūr, a dēvadāṉa of god Tiruveḷḷiyaṅguṉṟamuḍaiyār and 40 kaḻañju for two other
pieces of land belonging to Karuppuḻāṉ Tiruchchōṟṟuttuṟai uḍaiyāṉ (see No. 574
above)

inscription.head:
No. 576.
(A.R. No. 531 of 1904.)
TIRUTTEṄGŪR, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Rajatagirīśvara temple.
Rājēndra III. Year 11+1: 1257 A.D.

The details of date given viz., 11+1 year, Kaṟkaṭaka ba, Monday, Rōhiṇī, cor
responds to 1257 A.D., July 9, Monday. The tithi was 11.

This epigraph seems to state that the temple authorities exempted the merchant
of Tirutteṅgūr, a tiruṉundāviḷakkuppuṟam in the dēvadāṉa of god Tiruveḷḷiyaṅguṉṟam-
uḍaiya nāyaṉār, from the payment of the tax in oil in consideration of the return
of the land bought from the temple, by them sometime back in lieu of 50 kāśu
received by them. They also arranged to give to Tirutteṅgūr Pōyaṉ some other
land as vēṭṭai-ppēṟu in return for the land which was included in the tirunundā-
viḷakku-ppuṟam.

l.ta:
vatiṉ etirā
māṇṭu kaṟkaṭaka nā[ya]ṟṟu aparapakṣattu ti-

ṅkaṭkiḻam[ai]yum urocaṇiyum peṟṟa nāḷ muṉṉāḷil uṭaiyār tiruveḷḷiyaṅkuṉṟa

inscription.head:
No. 577.
(A.R. No. 532 of 1904.)
TIRUTTEṄGŪR, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Rajatagirīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 24+1: 1241 A.D.

The details of date given viz., 24+1st year, Makara ba. 14 Saturday, Uttirāḍam
regularly correspond to 1241 A.D., January 12, Saturday.

This inscription records the gift of one mā of land, as araśupēṟu entrusted to the
paḷḷigaḷ of the land endowed as tirunundāviḷakkuppuṟam, for supplying oil for a
perpetual lamp for god Tiruveḷḷiyaṅguṉṟamuḍaiyār, by the Ūravar of Tirutteṅgūr
Veḷḷiyiḍunagaram in Rājarājaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 578.
(A.R. No. 533 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Tyāgarāja shrine in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 23: 1092-93 A.D.

This inscription contains portions of the praśasti, Pugaḻśūḻnda puṇari etc., of
Kulōttuṅga I.

It seems to record some sale of land to the temple by the nagarattār of Tiruvārūr
in Tiruvārūr-kūṟṟam in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu. Other details are lost as the record
is built in and incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 579.
(A.R. No. 534 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Tyāgarājasvāmin shrine in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Rājēndra II. Year 9: 1060-61 A.D.

This inscription contains portions of the praśasti, Tirumādu puviyeṉum etc.,
of Rājēndra II.

This records the gift of land by purchase from the peruṅguṟi sabhā of Vēlaṅguḍi,
a brahmadēya in Tiruvārūr-kūṟṟam in Adhirājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, who made it tax-free
in lieu of 140 kāśu received by them from Vēḷāṉ Māṟaṉ alias Śembiyan Ten•••
of Paravai-chchuṟṟu-ppūṇḍi in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, for certain services including
Na[ḍu]vil Rājēndran sandi to god Mūlasthānam-uḍaiyār at Tiruvārūr.

l.ta:
ta[vi]yarkaḷāka nitiyuḷ nikaḻunāḷiṉu[ḷ] ikalveṭṭeḻuntu ceṉṟarumuṉaik koppat
tākavamallaṉoṭu porucayappuriyuṅkālai yarinikartta

jaṉmunti naṟpāṭu etittu nerāmaraicarai neṭuvicum peṟṟi antaravāṉattarampaiyar etir

inscription.head:
No. 580.
(A.R. No. 535 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM, TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab built into the floor of the first prākāra, north of the Tyāgarāja shrine
in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Sarfōjī-mahārāja. Śaka 1639: 1717 A.D.

This inscription mentions the king's predecessor Sahajī-mahārāja and his pre-
decessor Ēkōji-mahārāja. The details of date given viz. Śaka 1639, Kali 4818,
Hēvilambi, Chittirai 15, śu. 13, Friday, Hasta, regularly correspond to 1717 A.D.,
April 12, Friday.

It records the renovation (ashṭābandhaṉam) of the shrine of Mahāśivaliṅgam-
within the temple of god Tyāgarāja-svāmin Valmīkaṉāthasvāmin, by Mahāmaṇḍa,
lēśvara Sarapōśi (Sarfōji)-mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 581.
(A.R. No. 536 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On another slab built into the floor of the first prakāra, north of the central shrine
in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Achyutappa-Nāyaka. 1564 A.D. (?)

The inscription is dated in Ś. 1482, Rudhirōdgārin Paṅguni 29. The śaka and the
cyclic years do not tally. Rudhirōdgārin falls in Śaka 1485.

This seems to record the grant of income from levies such as araimaṉai śōḍi,
adhikāri [kaḍaikkūṭṭu], and śōḍi-puṟavagai, as sarvamānya, for the merit of his
father Śevvappa Nāyaka of Neḍuṅguṉṟam by Achyutappa Nāyaka. Other details
are lost as the inscription is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 582.
(A.R. No. 537 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM, TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple, right of
entrance.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 8: 1184-85 A.D.

This inscription commences with the praśasti Puyal vāyttu etc., of Kulōttuṅga III.
The month Tulā is mentioned as the date of the grant. It seems to refer to
a transaction made in the reign of Śuṅgantavirttaruḷiṉa Kulōttuṅgachōḷa. The donor
of this record is probably the same as Vēdavaṉam-uḍaiyāṉ Ammaiyappaṉ Pallava-
rāyaṉ, who figures as an officer of the king's predecessor Rājādhirāja II (Vide Ep.
Ind. Vol. XXI, p. 191).

This records a grant of land to the temple for burning 3 perpetual lamps each for
the deities Tiruvārūr Śrī Mūlasthāṉamuḍaiyār, Vīdiviḍaṅga-pperumāḷ and goddess
Tirukkāmakkōṭṭamuḍaiya Periyanāchchiyār, by Vēdavaṉam-u••• of Paḻai-
yaṉūr in Mēlmalai-ppaḻaiyaṉūr-nāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. The land was
entrusted in pieces to two persons who agreed to contribute the ghee required for
the lamps.

tuñ celanaṭappak kaṟpakālam puvikāppa•••••••••—•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 583.
(A.R. No. 538 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
right of entrance.
Rājādhirāja II. Year 2: 1164 or 1168 A.D. (?).

This inscription commences with the praśasti Kaḍal śūḻnda pārmagaḷum, etc.,
of Rājādhirāja II. The details of date given, viz., Year 2, Mēsha śu. 6, Monday, Puṉar-
pūśam, regularly correspond to 1168 A.D., April 15, Monday, ·30; ·28. Dr. Kielhorn
however has equated these details to 1164 A.D., March 30, ·85. The star, however,
was current throughout Tuesday and continued on Wednesday till ·10 of that day
according to the Indian Ephemeris.

This records a gift of two pieces of land by purchase as Rājādhirājapperuvilai
from Tōṉṟi Mūvāyirattoruvaṉ, one of the tiruvīdi āṇḍār and a headman of Peruñār,
tiruppudiyidūṭṭu viḷāgam (i.e., land whose fresh produce is entirely allotted for
food offerings) by Paḻaiyaṉūr-uḍaiyāṉ Vēdavanam-uḍaiyān Ammai-Appan alias
Rājarāja-viḻupparaiyan of Mēṉmalai-ppaḻaiyaṉūr-nāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.
1. The inscription is built in at the right end and incomplete.

l.ta:

colaruñ ciṅka[ṇa]rum mutalāya maṉṉavarkaḷ tiṟai cumantu vantiṇṭic cevippa ūḻiyūḻi

ñār kiḻavaṉṟ[o]ṉṟi muvāyirattoruvanperil

tirunāmattile kāṇiyākat tiruppūtiyu[tū]ṭṭu viḷākamāyk koyiluḻavu muṟṟūṭṭāk-

ttu nirvārttuk kuṭutteṉ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu

rājarājaviḻupparaiyaneṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 584.
(A.R. No. 539 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple, right of
entrance.
Māṟavarmaṉ Śrīvalla[bha*]dēva. Year 35: 1342 A.D.

Palaeographically the inscription may be assigned to the 14th century and the
details of date given, viz., Year 35, Siṁha śu. 5, Wednesday, Chittirai regularly cor-
respond to 1342 A.D., August 7, ·44; ·30.

This seems to record a gift of land by purchase, after making it tax free, for the
recitation of Vēdic hymns (adhyayana) once daily in the temple, by Tōḷāṉ Irājarāja
Akaḷaṅka-ṉāḍā[ḷvāṉ] of Tiruttoṇḍīśvaram in Paṉaiyūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrīḥ [||*] ko[m]āṟupaṉmar tirupuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ [śrī]civalla[pa*]t[e]
vaṟku [y]āṇṭu 35 āvatu muppattañcāvatu [ siṁ] ha nāyaṟṟu [pū]rvvapakṣattu
pañcamiyum putaṉ[kiḻa][mai*]yu[m pe]ṟṟa [ci]t[ti]r[ai]•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 585.
(A.R. No. 540 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Rājādhirāja II. Year 10: 1173 A.D.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Kaḍal śūḻnda pārēḻum etc., of
Rājādhirāja II. The details of date given, viz. Year 10, Mīṉa śu. 13, Tuesday, Maghā.
regularly correspond to 1173 A.D., February 27, Tuesday, ·48; ·65. These details
regularly correspond also to 1176 A.D., February 24, Tuesday, ·41; ·85.

This records a grant of land, bought as Irājādhirāja-pperuvilai in Śōḻakēraḷa-
nallūr in Tiruvārūr-kkūṟṟam in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu as kāṇi to goddess Paralōka
••• meṉmoḻi-nāchchiyār in the shrine of the goddess Tirukkāmakkōṭṭam-uḍaiya
Periyanāchchiyār in the temple of god Śrī-mūlasthānamuḍaiyār by Paḻaiyaṉūruḍaiyāṉ
Vēdavaṉamuḍaiyāṉ Ammai Appaṉ alias Pallavārājan of Mēṉmalai-paḻaiyaṉūr-
nāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam (vide No. 583 above).

edition.l:
[svasti]śrīḥ ||— kaṭal cūḻnta pāreḻun ticaiyeṭṭuṅ kāttu niṉṟu taṭamāmatiyeṉa [vi]
ḷaṅkit taraḷaveṇkuṭaini[l]ā veṟippa āḻvar[ai] varappāka aṭaṟkaliyaip
pilattotukki ūḻitoṟum pukaḻoṅka orāḻi veyiṟparappak kayal cilaiyil cāl [viḷai]
ya kaḷiṟāreyiṟ karaṅkuvippap puyalā[ḻi]yiṟ poṟṟiceyya pulimeruvil viṟṟiruppa
tiruvāṇaiyuñ ceṅkolun c[ai]yeṭṭuṅ kāval koḷḷapperu[vā]ḻvu peṟṟu
yiraṉaittumpiḻai•tta•• kkavarac camaiyamāṟun talaiyeṭuppat tarumamu marumaṟai-

l.ta:
[yu] moṅka amaivillā maṉuvoḻukka mātiyāmpaṭi nilainiṟka orppiṉun tammuṟukaṉavi
ṉum oṉṟoṭoṉṟu pakaiyiṉṟip porppuliyum pulvāyum puk[k]orutuṟai
niruṇṇap poṉṉinatiyum poyyātu [pu]yalum puṉalkaravātu maṉṉiya netivaḷam
perukka viḷaivayaliṉ vaḷañ curakkap pokapūmi yituveṉṉap pokamellām van-
tiṇṭi ekavāṇaiaracurimai eḻupoḻilkaḷum peṟṟateṉṉap pārkkaṭaliṟ kārkkaṭa[l]ppaṭi
vāḻamuṭicūṭi ārtta vamararapiṣekac[ce]-

tilaracar tiḷaittāṭa ev[vu]lakamu[m*] yiruṇīṅka vanta komāṉivaṉeṉṟu kavvai
tirak kalikālat tātikālaṅ kāṭṭiṉaṉeṉ ivaṉ kākkum puviyaṉaittum yāvaiyum
niṉai[n]tiṉṟaruḷi yavaṉ kākkumeṉa akilalokaṅkaḷum aṭiyaṭaiyak kulaipa[ṭu]
vaṉa teṅkukaḷe koṭpaṭuvaṉa iḷaṅkamuke alaipaṭuvaṉa nirnilaiye atirpaṭuvaṉa
iṭiyeṟe taḷampaṭuvaṉa porvarampe taṭaipaṭuvaṉa kopurame ūmpaṭuvaṉa cūṉmakaḷire
yoṟuppuṇpaṉa matakaḷiṟe taṭuppuṇpaṉa koṭṭakame taḷḷuṇpaṉa

[pū]ṅkoṭiye kaṟaipaṭuvatu niṟaimatiye paṭṭuṇpaṉa aṇiyalkule parintāṟṟuva-
pariyākame kuṟaipaṭuvaṉa kuḻa[ṟ]curuḷe kuḻaivillaṉa maṇikkoṅkaiye maṟaipaṭuvaṉa
cūḻkāñciye mālkoḷvaṉa vaṇaṅkiṭaiye eṉṟu pāṭip pārventa riḷaṅkaḷiṟṟiṉ meṟ
pokacceṉṟiṟaiñcip pārventar tiruvācaṟpuṟani[ṟ]pacceralarun teṉṉavaruñ ci[ṅ*]kaḷaruṅ
koṅkaṇarum peraracupeṟaveṇṭip piriyātu cevippat tam maracu tām peṟṟut ta[m*]
muṭaṉe••

[cū]ṭi[y] ammaruṅkun teviyarkaḷ aṇimaṅkalam peṟṟaruḷa vāḷaracar talaikākka yātavakula
marapil vanta coḷa[kula]maṇiviḷakkuc coḷakulamāṇikkam maṭanāṇmutaṟkaṇa nāṉkiṉ
vaṭivukoṇṭu vaḷavace[kara]ṉ uṭaṉāṇaiyun tiruvāṇaiyu muṭaṉcel[la] muṭikavittāḷ
maṇṇaracar vanti[ṟ*]aiñca makuṭam puṉaivaḷa[va*]ṉuṭaṉ peṇṇaracum peruntāyamum
peṟṟaruḷum peṇ[p]erumāḷ uṟaiyūrum perurakaiyū mutakaiyu maturāpuriyu muṟaimuṟai

[y]āṇṭaruḷi muḷarinakar tuṟantavaṉṉam poṉṉācaṉañ ciṅkavaṇaipolaṅ kaṟpakappūñ
colai muṉṉeval nittal muṟaimuṟaiye peṟumulaipperumāḷ aḷakanutaṟkayal
nayaṉat tampoṟ[ku]ḻaikkompeṉṉa uḷamakiḻa vantaruḷiya ulakuṭaiya mukkokkiḻā
ṉaṭiyuṭaṉ vāḻivāḻi maṇampuṇarntūḻiyūḻi pala voṅkic cempoṉ viraciṅkā

yuñ cevvāykkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa maka
ttunāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu meṉmalaippaḻaiyaṉūrnāṭṭup paḻaiyaṉūruṭai

kāṇiyāka ikkoyil āticaṇṭe

inscription.head:
No. 586.
(A.R. No. 541 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple, right of
entrance.
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 44: 1113-14 A.D.
This record commences with the praśasti pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc., of Kulōttuṅga I.

It states that the sabhā of Pudukkuḍi, a brahmadēya in Tirūvārūr-kkūṟṟam in
Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu, having assembled at the Dēvāśriyan sold and made tax-free
various pieces of lands with all rights of irrigation to Tribhuvanamuḍaiyān Irājēndra-
śōḻan alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Malaiyarājapanmar, a paṇimagan, resident in the place,
who paid 24(1/4) kaḻañju of gold and donated the land for daily worship and offerings
to the deity Umai Naṅgai set up at the Ulaguyyakkoṇḍa-kāmakkōṭṭam. The treasury
is called Tyāgavinōdaṉ and the standard weight stone is stated to be equivalent to
Tyāgavinōdaṉ-māḍai.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātuviḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa ṉilamakaḷ ṉilava malarmakaḷ puṇara urimai
yiṟ ciṟanta maṇimuṭicūṭi minavar[ni]laikeṭavi[l]lavaṟ[kulaitara eṉai]ma[ṉ]na[var i]ḻi[ya]
luṟṟa[ḻita]ra tikkaṉaittum [taṉ]cakkaranaṭātti vira

yam putukkuṭiyil u[ṟup]puṭṭur vāmanapaṭṭa
num u[ṟu]ppuṭ[ṭu]r nārāyaṇanum uṟuppuṭ(ṭa)ṭur kaṉtaiya paṭṭanum iḻakkantiṟattu

yom tiruvārur t[evāśrī]••••••• [va]ṇattu kūṭṭaṅkuṟai[va]
ṟa kūṭiyi[ru]ṉtu āticaṇṭecuvarate[va][r*]kku nilavilaiyāvaṇam ivvūr irukkum [paṇi]
makan tiripuvanamuṭaiyān irāje

ruḷiyirukkum umainaṅk[ai]•••• māṟṟukkum nilai
niman[tattu]kkum nilaniman[tattu]kkum [nila]ṅkoḷḷa tiyāka[vi]notaṉ paṇṭārattu
meṟpaṭiyārmāṭaiyokka kuṭiṉaikkallāl oṭukkinapoṉ irupattu nāṟkka[ḻa*]ñcu araiyum

inscription.head:
No. 587.
(A.R. No. 542 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple, right of
entrance.
Rājādhirāja II. Year 9: 1172 A.D. (?)

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Kaḍalśūḻnda pārmagaḻum, etc.,
of Rājādhirāja II. The details of the date given, viz., year 9, Tulā ba. are insufficient
for verification. Other details are lost in the portion built in.

This records a grant of land in Rājarājan-Pōttūr alias Ammaiappanallūr, formerly
a portion of Śiṟukūṟṟanallūr, in Vēḷā-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, purchased
for 1500 kāśu, to god Śrīmūlasthānam-uḍaiyār of Tiruvārūr, by Paḻaiyaṉūruḍaiyāṉ
Vēdavaṉamuḍaiyāṉ Ammai Appaṉ alias Pallavarājaṉ of Mēnmalai-ppaḻaivanūr (Vide
Nos. 582 and 583 above).

l.ta:
kaṭal cūḻnta pārumakaḷum pūmakaḷuṅ kalaimakaḷu maṭal cūḻnta porma[ka]ḷuñ cīr
makaḷu mamarntu vāḻa nāṟkaṭal cūḻ puviyeḻum pāṟkaṭal pol pukaḻ parappa ātiyuka
māmeṉṉac coti muṭi puṉaintaruḷi aṟucamaiyamum aimpūtamum neṟiyil vantu pā[ri]
kkat teṉ[ṉa]•••••••
cevippa ūḻiyūḻi yoru ceṅkol eḻupārum initaḷippac cempoṉ vira

meṉmalaip paḻaiyaṉūr nāṭṭup paḻaiyaṉūruṭaiyāṉ vetavaṉa muṭaiyāṉ ammai appaṉāṉa
pallavarājaṉeṉ uṭaiyār tiruvārur

ṅkacoḻap pallavarayarum puravuvariśrīkaraṇanāyakam tattainallūruṭaiyārum tirumantira
olai rājentiraciṅkamuventa veḷārum eḻuttiṭṭa [pi]ramāṇappaṭi eṉ kāṇiyāṉa
kulottuṅkacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu veḷā nāṭṭu [ci]ṟukūṟṟanallūrp piṟinta

matitta kācu āyirattaiñūṟu ikkācu āyirattaiñūṟum oṭukki iṉṉīlam nāṟ
taraiye orumāmukkāṇi araikkāṇi muntirikaik kiḻ oṉpatumāvaraiyum nāṉ koṇṭa
piramāṇappaṭiye nāyaṉār

ttile nirv[ā]rttuk kāṇiyākak kuṭuttu inta
rājā
l.note: The inscription is built in at the right end.

inscription.head:
No. 588.
(A.R. No. 543 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple, right of
entrance.
Rājādhirāja II. Year 5: 1167 A.D. (?).

This inscription is dated in the 19th day of the 5th year of Tribhuvanachakra-
vartin Rājādhirājadēva, evidently identical with Rājādhirāja II on account of the
mention of an order of Vikrama-chōḷa.

This records an order of the king, restoring to the servants of the temple of Śrī-
mūlasthānamuḍaiyār, house-sites previously made tax-free and set apart for them after
making allotments to the cheṭṭis, chekkārs, their followers and Chōḷēndiraśiṅgaṉ vēḷai-
kkāṟar in the 9th year of Vikramachōḷa. The sites are stated to have
been later on encroached upon by the cheṭṭis for their dwellings and shops. It is
also stated that the servants of the temple were to receive 100 kāśu in compensa-
tion.

l.ta:
muṭaiyār koyilil patipātamulap
paṭṭuṭaip pañcācāriyat tevarkaṉmikkum

ḷukkum śrīkāriyañ ceyvāṉukkum tiruvārur nakarattu iṟaiyili nattattile ivvūrc ceṭṭi
kaḷukku nūṟṟumuppatu maṉaiyum cekkārkkum ivarkaḷ

tuḷḷārkkum irupatu maṉaiyuñ coḷentiraciṅkaṉ veḷaikkāṟarkku aimpatu maṉai[yu]ṅ kuṭiyirup
pākavum nikkiniṉṟa nattam uṭaiyār

tirumaṭaiviḷākamāy nivantakkāṟarkkuk kuṭiyiruppākavuṅ kaṭavatāka vikkiramacoḻatevarkku
oṉpatāvatu nāḷile olai ceṉṟu ippaṭiye cilanāḷ aṉupavittu varacce-

yte nivantakkāṟarkkuk kuṭi iruppāṉa maṉaikaḷaiyuñ ceṭṭikaḷe kaikkoṇṭārkaḷeṉṟum
ivvūr iṟaiyilinattattut tiruvārur nakarattuc ceṭṭikaḷukku ivarkaḷ kuṭiyiruppāṉa
teṟkiṟ puṟattiruvi-

ttuḷḷāruṅ kuṭiyiruntu kaṭai kaṭṭip
parimāṟi varukiṟa avaṉinārāyaṇap perunateruviṟ cekkār oḻukkaikku meṟkum a[ka]p
piṇavar teruvuk-

ku vaṭakkum kuṭiyiruppākavum cekkārkku avaṉinārāyaṇap perunteruviṟ ce[kkār] oḻukkai
yeṉkiṟa itukkuk kiḻakkuk kuṭiyiruppākavum coḷentiraciṅkaṉveḷaikkāṟarkkut te-

ṟkiṟpuṟattiruvitikku vaṭakku aimpatu maṉai kuṭiyiruppākavum nīkki niṉṟa•••••••
[mu]ṭaiyārkkut tirumaṭaiviḷākamāy nivantakkāṟar pala iṭaṅkaḷiluṅ kuṭi irukkiṟār tiru-

vitiyile cera maṉai yeṭuttu irukkavum tiruvārur nakarattukku•••••••••
ṭu varukiṟa kācile tirumaṭai viḷākamāy nivantakkāṟark kuṭiyiruppāṉa nattattukku [nū]-

ṟu kācu nālāvatu mutal tavirntu nikki niṉṟa kācu innakarattukku••••••••• ḷavum
ippaṭikkuk k[oyi]lile kalveṭṭavum peṟaveṇumeṉṟu pallava

ṉṉamaiyil ippaṭi ce[yyak] kaṭavatākac collik kaṇakkilum iṭṭu•••••••••
••••• kaḷukku[c*] [co]ṉṉom tiruvārur iṟaiyili natta[t*]tiṟ [teṟ]kiṟ puṟattiruvitiyu
•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 589.
(A.R. No. 544 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 34: 1103-04 A.D.
This epigraph commences with the praśasti, pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc., of the king.

It seems to record a list of the lands belonging to god Ulagīśvaramuḍaiyār of
Tiruvārūr, in Tiruvārūr-kkūṟṟam in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu. The dēvadāṉam Paḻa-
ṉakkuḍi is mentioned. The details of the gift are lost as the inscription is damaged
and incomplete.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ vaḷara malar makaḷ puṇara urimai
yiṟciṟanta maṇimuṭi cūṭi miṉavar nilai [k*]eṭa villavar kulaitara eṉai maṉnavar [i]ḻiyaluṟ
ṟuḻitarat tikkaṉaittuntan cakkarannaṭātti vira

itu cuppiramaṅkalamuṭaiyāṉ mātavaṉiravi
yāṉa mānālaiya [muve]nta veḷān kolottuṅka coḻavaḷanāṭṭu v(e)•••••••••

coḻaccaruppe[timaṅ]kalattuk kuravacari cirāma paṭṭarum uḷḷaḷavaḷantu
ci• raṅkaṭa•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 590.
(A.R. No. 545 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Vikramachōḷa. Year [6]: 1123 A.D.

The details of date given viz. Year 6, Āvaṇi, śu. Āyilyam are insufficient to calculate
the equivalent.

This inscription records the sale of land, made tax-free, to god Śrīmūlasthāna-
muḍaiyār by Ponmalai-chchēri Uḍaiyāṉ. Vēḷāṉ. Śūriyaṉ alias Virudarājabhayaṅ-
kara Aḻundūr-Vēḷāṉ, receiving 4 aṉṟāḍu naṟkāśu granted to the temple for maintain-
ing a garden, by Pālaiyūruḍaiyān Chandraśēkharan Ādiviḍaṅgaṉ. alias Mahābalivā-
ṇarāyar. Of the 4 kāśu, 1 kāśu is stated to be the cost of the land and 3 kāśu, the lump
sum provided for making it tax-free.

l.ta:
kṣattu āyileyattu [n]āḷ keya
māṇikka vaḷanāṭṭut tiruvārurk kūṟṟattu teva[t]ānam••••[k]kuṭi kāṇi uṭaiya [poṉ
malaic]eri uṭaiyāṉ veḷān cūriyanāṉa virutarāya payaṅkara aḻuntur •••••

inscription.head:
No. 591.
(A.R. No. 546 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Year 2.

This inscription does not give the name of the king but mentions the 9th day of the
second year. On palaeographical considerations this may be assigned to the 12th
century.

It records a grant of land as kāṇi and jīvitam to Pirāṇa-upakāri-Sāraiku••••alias
Śōḻēndraśiṅga••••••• [Vīrati]yāgataraiyaṉ by the deity at Tiruvārūr made at the
instance of Śōḻatuṅga-kkaḍigamārāyaṉ, (Ghaṭika-mahārājā).

l.gr:
svastiśrī [||*] yatjaga[t*]traya nirmmā[ṇa]trāṇa ni[rvvā]ṇa kāraṇam [|*] tadidaṅ
śāsanam [śambh]ōḥ śrīpū[rvv]ā[rū]ravāsina[ḥ] [||*]
l.ta:
pirāṇa upa[kāri] cāraiku
••••••• ṉāṉa coḻentira[ciṅka][vira] tiyākataraiyaṉukku
nam [a]kampa[ṭi]yāril maruttuvaṟku

••• [y]āṉa kuṭiyil kāṇiyum jīvitamum[ā]ṉa nilamāy maruttuva[ṉ*] pakkal tāṉ
vilai koṇṭuṭaiya piramāṇappaṭi nila [mu]k[kā]lum taṉakkun taṉ vaṟkattāṟkum kāṇi
[yum] jīvitamumākat tantom iṉṉila miraṇṭāvatu mutal

••• [k]oṇṭu aṉupavikkaveṉṟu coḻatuṅkak kaṭikamārāyaṉ viṇṇappattāl
iraṇṭāvatu nāḷ oṉpatiṉāl

ñceytaruḷat tirumantira volai toḻuvūruṭai
yā[ṉ] eḻutiṉa tirumukappaṭi [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 592.
(A.R. No. 547 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
[Rājarāja II].

This inscription commences with the Praśasti pūmaruviya poruppēḻum, etc., and
stops in the middle.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya (poḻileḻum) p[o]ruppeḻum puṉainittalattāma neṭuṅkuṭai poḻinta tavaḷa veṇ
ṇilākku•ra potiyac cuṭaṟ cakkaraveṟpiṟ ṟaṉanaṭaic cakkaram veyileṟippac ciṉappuliyuñ
ceṅkolu manaittuyiṟkkuṅ kā[val] pūṇap [pa]•[ya]•ṇamicaip parañcoti
pāṟkaṭal niṉṟeḻuntaruḷi maṇineṭu muṭi vittā[neṉa] maṇmaṭantaiyai[k]kaip
piṭittu malarmaṭantai maṇiyāra varaimārviṟ kuṭivāḻa [pula] maṭantai koḻunaṉā[ki]p
por maṭantaiyai maṇam puṇarntu paruti mutaṟ kulam viḷaṅkac curutikaḷ••••

[ṉav]e raruḷ vāyppa viḻunta aṟi camaiyattai a•ḷeṭut tātiyukaṅ ko[ḻu]ntu
viṭṭut taḻaittoṅkak komātaṟaṅ kuḷir[tū]ṅka māri vāyt[tu] vaḷañ curakkat tāraṇi
yor piṇi niṅka nallor taṅkaṟpuyara nāṉmaṟaiyor tuṟai vaḷara ellārun tanitta
[ṉi]yey [v]āḻntaṉam yāmeṉa makiḻṉtu oruvaruṭaṉ oruvaṟkum oṉṟiṉuṭaṉ oṉṟukku
varupakai [yaka]ttiṉṟi viḻaintu [kātaluṭaṉ]cera intiraṉ mutaṟticāpālar eṇmaru moru
vaṭivāki vantapaṭiyeṉa niṉ[ṟu] maṉuvāṇai taṉi naṭāttiya [pāṭiyāṉaiye] piṇippuṇpaṉa
vaṭimaṇic[ci]lampe yaraṟṟuvaṉa celoṭaiyey kalakku•••••••••

[ṇpaṉa]•māvey vaṭuppaṭuvaṉa māmalarey kaṭīyavā[yi]ṉa varupuṉaley ciṟaippa[ṭu]vaṉa
kāvukaḷey koṭiyavāyiṉa kaḷḷuṇpaṉa vaṇṭukaḷey poy[yu]ṭaiyaṉa va[rai]veyey
pormalaivaṉa eḻukaḻaṉiye maiyyuṭaiyaṉa neṭuvaraiye maruḷuṭaiyaṉa iṉamāṉkaḷey
kayaṟkulamey [pi]ṟantoḻukuṅ kaittāyarey kaṭintoṟuppār iyaṟpulavarey poruḷ
vaippār icai[ppāṇa]rey kūṭañ c[ey]vār e[ṉṟu] kūṟi ivaṉ kākkun tirunā
ṭṭiṉiyal vituveṉa ṉiṉṟu kāval neṟipūṇ[ṭu] neṟiyallatu niṉaiyātu tantaiyil
lor tantaiyākiyun tāyarillor tāyarākiyu maintaril lorumaintarākiyum

[maṉ]ṉuyirkaṭ kuyirākiyum viḻipeṟṟa payaṉe[ṉ]ṉavu m[e]y peṟṟa aruḷeṉṉavum[o]
ḻi [po]ru[ḷe]ṉṉavu mukam peṟṟa [paṉu]valeṉṉavum ettuṟaikkum iṟaivaṉeṉṉa
[vu yā]ñ cey

inscription.head:
No. 593.
(A.R. No. 548 of 1904).
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākara in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Year 13.

This inscription does not give the name of the king. It is dated in the 202nd day
of the 13th year. Palaeographically it may be assigned to the 12th century A.D.

It records a grant of land in Vāyaṟṟūr, a brahmadēya to one Pūṅgōyilnambi
who composed the Vīraṇukka-vijayam in honour of Vīraśōḻa-aṇukkar patronised as
our sons (nammakkaḷ) by the god Tiruvārūruḍaiyār Vīdiviḍaṅgadēvar, when he was
witnessing a dance by Pūṅgōyil Nāyaka-ttalaikkōli at Dēvāśriyan. The grant is
stated to have been made by the deity at the instance of Tyāgavinōda-kkaḍigaimārāyan
who acted as the kēḻvi. Muttiṟattān figures as the signatory.

edition.l:
svastiśrī ||— yāṇṭu 13 āvatu nāḷ 202 || tiruvāruruṭaiyār vitiviṭaṅkadēvar dēvā
śrīyan [ti]rukk[āvaṇatte] eḻuntaruḷiyiruntu pūṅkoyil nāyakattalaikkoliyai āṭṭaṅ
kaṇ[ṭaruḷa] nam makkaḷ viracoḻa aṇukkarai viraṇukka vijayam

l.ta:
yam vāyaṟṟūril iṟaiyilinilamāṉa kanakarāyan nila
menṟu perkūvappaṭṭa nilattile nilaṅkalantapaṭi nilam araiyum te[n]kuḷaṅkaraiyil
kūttan maṉaikaḷil kiḻakkaṭaiya maṉai

ttārkkum kāṇiyāka kuṭuttomenṟu tinruvāymoḻintaruḷit tiyākavinotak
kaṭikaimārāyan keḻviyāl keḻp[pi]ttapaṭikku aruḷāl ivai muttiṟattā
neḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 594.
(A.R. No. 549 of 1904).
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Kulōttuṅga II. Year 15: 1147-48 A.D.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Pūmaṉṉupadumam, etc., of Kulōt-
tuṅga II and stops after mentioning the date.

l.ta:
patumam pūtta veḻulakun tāmuṉ ceytavattāl parutivaḻit
tonṟi neṭumāliva neṉac cuṭarmuṭi cūṭi yirunilamakaḷai u[rim]aiyiṟ puṇarntu tiru
makaḷ paṇaimulaic ce(ya)ñcān taḷaintu parumaṇimārvam paṉivarai nikaṟppa ceyamakaḷ
[ceñcan]taṉa (cantana)[c] cuvaṭṭāl puyamiru ka[yilaip] poruppeṉat toṉṟi tiru
makaḷ tāṉumeṅkomakaḷ cevvāyp pavaḻac ceyoḷi paṭaittaṉan niyāneṉat tavaḷanaṉ
niṟa[ṉ] taṉittoṭaiye[neṉap] pukaḻ makaḷ cintai makuḻu

naṉ[nāḷiṉum] oru kuṭai
nilavum porupaṭaittikiri veyililuṅ karuṅ-

kaliyiruḷiṉait tu[ṟa]ppa niṭupallūḻi yeḻkaṭaṟ puṟattiṉuṅ koṭāc centaṉik koli
[ṉi] tulāva mi[ṉamu]ñ cilaiyuñ citaittu vāṉuyar poṉneṭumeruviṟ puli viṟṟiruppa
umparyāṉai oreṭṭiṉukkun tampameṉṉat taṉittaṉi ticaitoṟum vicaiya

(ta)mpa niṟpa
pacipakaiyā[ṉiti•• tu niṅ]ki maṉṉuyir taḻaippa maṉuvāṟu viḷaṅka mātavar tavamu maṅ
kaiyar kaṟpum ātiyantaṇarākutikkaṉalu miteḻukoṇṭal vicutaṇpuṉalu metiṉivaḷamu
cātiyoḻukkamum nītiyaṟamum piṟaḻātu nikaḻp pāvum paḻaṉapparappum paṉaikkai māvu
mallatu vanḷaiṟappaṭutal kaṉaviluṅ kāṇṭaṟ kariteṉa varuntic cu-

ṉaiyilum palaveṟu puḷḷiṉumallatu ciṟaiyeṉappaṭutalaṉṟi niṟaiperuñ celvamoṭavani
vāḻap pallavar teluṅkar vāḷuvar kaliṅkar kocalar kannara kaṭārar ciṅkaṇar teṉ
nar cetikar tirikattar vaṅka raṅkar

ttivar mattirar kaṅkar coṉakar kaiykayār jiṉa
reṉ ṟaṟaikaḻalventaru mellā araicaru muṟaimaiyi[l] varinta tiṟaikuṇarn tiṟaiñca
kimaiyappoṟkoṭṭimaiyap pāvaiyuñ civanaruḷ peṟṟup puvanamuḻutuṭaiyā ḷivaṉ
tiruviḷḷattaruḷu muḻutuṭaiyāḷeṉa uṭaṉiruppa ampoṉ malaṟkoṭi cempiyaṉ kiḻā
naṭi [yuṭa]ṉ tiru[ma]ṇimārvat taruḷuṭaṉiruppa cempoṉ vira

ṉamuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kovirājakecaripaṟmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravart
tikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkacoḻa

inscription.head:
No. 595.
(A.R. No. 550 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājāsvāmin temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 13: 1190-91 A.D.

This inscription is dated in the reign of the Kulōttuṅga-chōḻadēva, who took
Madurai and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya, i.e. Kulōttuṅga III.

It records the grant of lands by purchase as antarāyam koḷḷā ūrkkīḻ iṟaiyili by the
Śrīkāryam Pūṅguḍaiyāṉ Vīra-Vichchādira(vidyādhara)—ppallavaraiyaṉ, from the
villagers of Pudukkuḍi and Paṉaṅguḍi, for worship and daily food offerings to god
Dikku-niṟainda Vināyaka-ppiḷḷaiyār set up to the west of tīrthakkuḷam by the donor.
The lands along with another piece of land granted for the same purpose by the resi-
dents of Tyāgavinōda-chchaturvēdimaṅgalam, were entrusted to the care of Mahāvrati
Bāradāyaṉ Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ Tirumutti-yāvāṉ who had the parichāra-
kkāṇi in the temple. The god Vīdiviḍaṅga-pperumāḷ is stated to have approved
this arrangement made at the instance of Tiruvāykkēḻvi Tamiḻadaraiyaṉ, when
he was taken out in procession in the āṭṭattu-veḷi on the occasion of the Pūśam festival
in the month of Tai.

l.ta:
śrī[ti]ru[pu]va[ṉa]ccakkaravarttikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇ[ṭiyaṉ] muṭittalaiyum
koṇṭaruḷiṉa śrī kulottuṅkacoḻatevarkku yāṇṭu

vatu uṭaiyār vitiviṭaṅkap peru
māḷ taippūcat tirunāḷ āṭṭattu veḷiyil tiru ulāc ceytaruḷā niṟka śrīmāyecurar
viṇṇappañ ceytapaṭi pūṅkuṭaiyāṉ vīraviccāttirappallava[raiyar] tirttakkuḷattuk
ku melkaraiyil eḻuṉtaru[ḷuvi]tta tikkuniṟainta viṉāyakappiḷḷaiyārkku aṟca
ṉaikkum tiruamutupaṭikkum ṉāḷ-

oṉṟukkuk kuṟuṇi nā[ṉā]ḻi arici peṟaveṇumeṉṟu viṇṇappañ ceyyat tiruvāk
keḻvi tamiḻatarayaṉ viṇṇappattāl cimāyecurak kaṇkāṇi māneṉtukaiyyaṉaiyum
cikāriya viraviccātirap pallavaraiyaṉaiyum nivaṉtam niccayittu kuṭuṅkaḷeṉṟu [tiru]u
ḷamā[y] aruḷa pūṅkuṭaiyār viraviccātirappallavaraiyar tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka vilai
koṇṭeṉ keyamāṇikka vaḷanāṭṭut tiruvārur kūṟṟattup piramatecam putukkuṭi
ūravar-

pakkale nālumā nilamum iṉnāṭṭu paṉaṅkuṭi ūravar pakkale nālumānilamum āka nilam
e[ṭṭumāvu]m antarāyaṅ koḷḷā ūrkiḻ iṟaiyiliyāka vilai koṇṭe neṉṟum
tiyākaviṉo(ta)taccaruppetimaṅkalattār antarāyaṅ koḷḷā ūrkiḻ iṟaii(l)li
yākap piḷḷaiyār cipāta[t*]tile nirvāṟṟatu viṭṭa nilam iraṇṭumā āka nilam
araiyum ikkoyil paricārakkāṇi uṭaiya makāvirati pāratāyaṉ tiru-

cciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ tirumuttiyāḻvāṉ pe[ṟi]le antarāyaṅ koḷḷāt taramili ūr
kiḻ iṟaiyiliyākap piramāṇam paṇṇik kuṭuttārkaḷeṉṟu viṇṇappañ ceyya ippaṭi
ivvūravar pakkale ūrkiḻ iṟaiyili peṟṟapaṭikku[m] ippaṭi nivantamuṅ kaṭṭi[na]
paṭikkum nam piḷḷaikku iraṇṭu tirupparicāram nāṭṭina per iraṇṭukkum ṉāḷ oṉ
ṟukku coṟu kuṟuṇi nāṉāḻiyāka vaṉta coṟu niṟpatākavum nam piḷ-

ḷai iruppum cikāramum kuḻi eḻupatilum ikkoyil pa[ri]kāri ākkiṉa teṅku uḷ
ḷiṭṭa māvirukṣam tiruviḷakkukku uṭalākak koṭuttom ippaṭi kalveṭṭik kuṭukka
veṉṟu cimāyecura kaṇkāṇi māneṉtukaiyyaṉaiyum cikāriyam viraviccātirap pallava
raiyaṉaiyum tiruviḷamāy aruḷinār; ||

inscription.head:
No. 596.
(A.R. No. 551 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Māṟavarmaṉ Kuḻaśēkhara I. Year 40: 1308 A.D.

The details of date given viz. Year 40, Ś. 1229, Mīṉa ba. 10, Monday, Tiruvōṇam,
regularly correspond to 1308 A.D., March 18, Monday. The Śaka date 1229 is given
at the end.

This epigraph records a grant of land in Śiṟunallūr a brahmadēya in Tēvūr-nāḍu in
Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu as tirunāmattukkāṉi to god Tiruvārūruḍaiyār by Vēḷāṉ
Koṅgil-piḷḷaiyāṉ of Kaḻaṉivāyil in Eyil-nāḍu in Pāṇḍikulapati-vaḷanāḍu. The
lands are stated to have been purchased for this purpose even in the 32nd year of the
king but were in the enjoyment of the donor.

l.ta:
yum tiṅkaḷkkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattu
nāḷ uṭaiyār tiruvāruruṭaiy[ār] koyilkaṇkāṇiceyvā[r]kaḷukkum koyil
mutalk karaṇatt[ā]rkkum pāṇṭikulapativaḷanāṭṭu eyilnāṭṭu kaḻaṉivāyiluṭaiyāṉ
veḷāṉ koṅkilppiḷḷaiyāṉeṉ iṉṉāyanā[r*]kkut tirunāmattukkāṇiyākak kuṭutta
ittevaṟkku

vatu[mutal iṉṉāḷ]va[rai] nāṉ tirunāmattukkāṇiyākak
kuṭu••••••••• vatu keyam[āṇikkavaḷa]nāṭṭu tevūrnāṭṭu

m
ciṟunallūril irācākkaḷ tampirāṉāṟṟukku vaṭakaraippaṭṭa nilattu nāṉ palar
pakkalilum eṉperālum eṉ makaṉ tevarkaḷtevaṉ perālum ivaṉtampi

ṉ pakkal iṉṉāyaṉārkku tirunāmattuk
kāṇiyāka peci

vilaikoṇṭa nilamumāy eṉṉutāy nāṉ innāḷvaraiyum aṉupavittu ponta
viḷainilaṅkaḷum paṭukaikaḷum kollaikaḷum ūrnatta[t*]tu maṉaikaḷumāy

vatu
ṉāḷilum inta nāṟpatāvatu nāḷilum nāṉ tirunāmattukkāṇiyākak kuṭuttu
inilaṅkaḷukku vilaikoṇṭa

ṇaṅkaḷum kuṭuttu itukku iṭṭukkoṭutta taṭi
uḷvarippaṭi viḷai pattaraiye mukkāṇiyum paṭukai nilam iraṇṭe irumāvaraiyum
kollaikaḷum teṟkil nattamum nilam veli āka nilam patimuṉṟaraiye muṉṟu
mākka

••••••••• vaṭakkil ūr natta[t]tu māṭalaṉ tirucciṟṟampala muṭaiyāṉ
paṭṭaṉ maṉai oṉṟum

tamaṉ kecavapaṭṭaṉ maṉaiyum iṟṟaināḷāl tiruṉāmat
tukkāṇiyākak kuṭutteṉ kaḻaṉivāyiluṭaiyāṉ veḷāṉ koṅkilppiḷḷaiyāṉeṉ
ivai eṉ eḻuttu iṉṉāḷāvatu

inscription.head:
No. 597.
(A.R. No. 552 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Kulōttuṅga II. Year 12: 1143-44 A.D.

This inscription mentions a land survey made in the 16th year of Śuṅgandavirtta-
ruḷiṉadva i.e. Kulōttuṅga I.

It records a gift to Śri-Mūlasthāṉam-uḍaiyār, of land made tax-free, purchased
from Brāhmaṇas in Naḍappūr alias Uttamaśōḻamaṅgalam in Paṉaiyūr-nāḍu in
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, in Ediriliśōḻanallūr, a tax free dēvadāna, by Āṇḍapiḷḷai
Āṇḍār. The produce from the land was to be utilised to maintain (1) three perpetual
lamps, (2) servants bringing water from Kaḍuvāy for the holy bath (3) servants in the
garden called Puṟṟiḍaṅgoṇḍāṉ in Nandipura alias Karuṇākaranallūr, a dēvadāṉa
(4) a servant working at the śeṅgaḻunīr garden in Naḍappūr alias Uttamaśōḻamaṅ-
galam in Paṉaiyūr-nāḍu and (5) the servant supplying hay for the cows (gōśālai-
surabhigaḷ).

l.ta:
ṉamuṭaiy[ā*]rkku tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyāy ik[koyil ā]ṇṭapiḷḷai
āṇṭār tirunu[n]tāviḷakkukku[m] kaṭuvāyttirumañcaṉanir cumappārkkum tevatāṉam
nantipuramāṉa karuṇākaranallū(r)ril ivar ceyvikkiṟa puṟṟiṭaṅkoṇṭāṉ tirunantaṉa
vaṉakku-

ṭikaḷukkum ceṅkaḻunir tiruppaḷḷittāmam ākkukaikkum paṉaiyūrnāṭṭu naṭappūrāṉa
uttamacoḻamaṅkalattu ivarkoṇṭu viṭṭa ku••• ṅakaḻuni[r*] tiruppaḷittāmam
ākkum kuvaḷaikiḻa[vaṉu*]kkum tirukkocālaiccuravikaḷukku vaikkol koṇṭiṭukaikkum
iṟuppatāka muṉṉuṭaiyārum paḻamperum

āvatu pacāṉamutal tevatāṉa
iṟaiyili iṭṭa kulottuṅkacoḻavaḷanāṭṭup paṉaiyūrnāṭṭu naṭappūrāṉa [ut]tama
coḻamaṅkala[t]tu

ṇa[rila] [pā]rattuvāci mārāyaṉ iṭarnikkiṉā[ṉu]m kavutavaṉ
cirāḷaṉ āḷuṭaiyāṉum uḷḷiṭṭār pakkal āṇṭa piḷḷaiyāṇṭā[r] vilai-
āvatu pacāṉamutal tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyāy veṟupiṟinta etirilicoḻanallūr

ḷam ittevarkku ceṅkaḻunir
tiruppaḷḷittāmam ākkukaikku kulottuṅkacoḻavaḷanāṭṭup paṉaiyūrnāṭṭu naṭappūrāṉa
uttamaco-

āvatu aḷavil niṅkalukkuṭalāka
nikki[na] ūruṇikuḷa[m]māy i[vvūr]

ṇar pakkal āṇṭa piḷḷaiyāṇṭār
koṇṭuṭaiya piramāṇappaṭi nilamāy muṉpuṭaiyārum paḻamperun tavittu yāṇṭu

āvatu aḷavil niṅkalukkuṭalāy nikkiṉa
ūruṇiku[ḷa]māy yāṇṭu

āvatu pacāṉa mutal ceṅkaḻunir tiruppa[ḷ*]ḷittāmam
[ākkuk]aikku niṅkiṉa kuḷamuṅkaraiyum kiḻakku

ḷa itil uṭaiyār śrīmulastāṉa muṭaiyār
kōyilil eriyuṉ tiruṉu-

ḷam kuvaḷaikiḻavaṉ oru
vanukku aṭai[tta] nilam va li

ḷa
•••• kulottuṅka coḻavaḷanāṭṭu ceṟṟūrkkūṟṟattu kuṭavāyilāṉa coḻakulavallic
caruppetimaṅ—
kalattu koḻuntākkum nilattup paḻam peyar tavirntu yāṇṭu

āvatu pacānamutal
antarāyam pāṭṭam [uṭ]••••••• iṟaiyiliyāy veṟu [piṟitta naraciṅka] maṅkalam nilam

uṭpaṭa tevatāṉa iṟaiyili iṭṭamaikku innilattukku taram eḻutip pukutāmaiyil
tarameḻutip pukuntāl taravopātiyāl vanta nel[lu] ūriṟ curukki innellu
nilavopātikku eṟumākil iṟaikkaṭṭākkik koḷḷuvatu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 598.
(A.R. No. 553 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Kulōttuṅga II. Year 10: 1142 A.D.

This inscription commences with the praśasti Pūmaṉṉu padumam of Kulōttuṅga.
The details of date given viz. Year 10 Dhaṉus śu. 11, Monday, Aśvati, regularly corres-
pond to 1142 A.D., November 30, ·98, ·68.

It records the sale of land made tax free, as kuḍinīkkākkāṉi to god Tiruvārūruḍai
yār Śrīmūlasthānamuḍaiyār for the koṟṟu of the 12 tavasiyar who were tending the
gardens and supplying Śeṅgaḻunīr-ttiruppaḷḷittāmam from the garden at Vāyaṟṟūr
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻaṉ Peruṅguḷam by the sabhā of Rājarājabrahmamaṅgalam, a brahma-
dēya in Tiruvārūr-kūṟṟam in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu, having met at Dēvāśriyan
Tirukkāvaṇam receiving from the temple 157(1/2) aṉṟāḍu naṟkāśu.

The sale of another piece of land similar to this, for supply of the damanaka flowers
is also recorded. The Sabhā reserved for itself the supervisory functions and the respon-
sibility to make over the produce of these lands to the temple.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 599.
(A.R. No. 554 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year. 24: 1201 A.D.

This inscription mentions the 57th day of the 24th year of Tribhuvaṉavīradēva
who is referred to as nam tōḻaṉ by the god.

It records the redistribution of tax-free lands made according to the order of the
deity to the 100 āṇḍārs, serving in the temple at the request of Vīrarājēndrappallavarai-
yaṉ and Tirumuttiṉ-śivigai-peṟṟāṉ, by god Śrī Pūrvvārūrvāsi, as the said āṇḍārs failed
to distribute among themselves properly the lands previously allotted to them and
quarelling left their land uncultivated. The lands lay in Śōḻa-Kēraḷanallūr in Gēya-
māṇikka-vaḷanāḍu, Virudarājabhayaṅkarachcharuppēdimaṅgalam and Kaḷachchāt-
taṅguḍi in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. Tiruvāykkēḻvi Aṇukkanambi, tirumandira
ōlai Śrīnandikēśvara-mūvēndavēḷāṉ and tirumandira ōlai Tyāgavinōda-mūvēnda-
veḷāṉ figure as signatories in the record.

l.gr:
svastiśrī % yajjagat[tra*]yanirmmāṇatrāṇa nirvvāṇakāraṇam [|*] tadidam śāsanam
śambhōḥ śrīpūrvvārūravāsinaḥ
l.ta:
nammai sevaippaṇṇi kumpiṭṭu irukku
āṇṭārkaḷ per nūṟṟuvarkkum nam aṭukkaḷaip puṟaṅkaḷāṉa ūrkaḷil keyamāṇikka
vaḷanāṭṭu coḻakeraḷanallūril aṭaitta nilam muppate[mu]kkāle muṉṟumāviṉ
kiḻ muk[kā]le oru māmukkāṇiyum aruvala-

māṉa virutarājapayaṅkarac caruppetima[ṅ*]kalattu ūrkiḻ iṟaiyili p[o]ttakappaṭi nilam
kālum kulottuṅkacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu kaḷaccāttaṅkuṭiyi[l] ūrkiḻ iṟaiyiliyil aṭaitta
nilam mukkāle orumāvarai muntirikai kiḻ araiyum āka nilam muppattoṉ[ṟe ki]ḻ
l.note: This empression kIḻ must be ignored. Otherwise the total cannot be accounted for.
l.ta:
mukkāle nāṉmāvaraiye araikkāṇi kiḻāṟumā mukkāṇiyil nām ulāppotumiṭattu
muṉpu kai-

ytarum [ku]vaḷaiveliuṭaiyāṉ tiruttūḷi aḻakiyāṉukku aruvalattu ūrkiḻ iṟai-
yiliyil pottakappaṭi aṭaitta nilam kālum coḻakeraḷanallūril aṭaitta nilam
[i]rumāvaraiyum vayirākikaḷ iruvarkku kaḷaccāttaṅkuṭiyil aṭaitta nilam araiye
irumāvarai muntirikaiyum nikki per toṇṇūṟṟeḻuvarkkum coḻakeraḷanallūr
nilam mu-

ppate mukkāle araimāviṉ kiḻ[muk*]kāle oru māmukkāṇiyum kaḷaccāttaṅkuṭiyil nilam
nālumāviṉ kiḻaraiyum ivarkaḷukku nāmaṭaitta nāḷmutal nellum nilamum taṅka
ḷil o[k]ka kūṟiṭāmaiyāle āṇṭukaḷtoṟum aḻittu kūṟiṭṭuvarukaiyāle innilaṅkaḷ
oruvarum uṟcākam paṇṇāmaiyāle taṭṭuppaṭṭuvārāni[ṉṟa] teṉṟum taṅkaḷil
nellum nilamum

rappallavaraiyaṉum
tirumut[ti]ṉ civikai peṟṟāṉum namakkuc coṉṉamaiyil nam toḻaṉ tiripuvaṉaviraṉukku
irupattu nālāvatu mutal taṅkaḷil nellum nilamum okkaccerttu eḻuta virarājentirap
pallavaraiyaṉum tirumuttiṉ civikai peṟṟāṉum eḻuttiṭṭa kaiyyaṭaiypa[ṭiye] ivarkaḷuk
kum ivar-

kaḷ vaṟkattāṟkum kāṇiyum iṟaiyiliyum ākac coṉṉom tiruvāykkeḻvi aṇukkanampi
viṇṇappattāl aruḷāl eḻutiṉāṉ tirumantira olai śrīnantike

muventa veḷā
ṉeṉṟum ivai virarājentirap pallavaraiyaṉ eḻutteṉṟum ivai tirumuttiṉ civikai
peṟṟāṉ eḻutteṉṟum ivai tirumantira olai tiyāka viṉota muventaveḷāṉ eḻut
teṉṟum yāṇṭu irupattuṉālā-

ceytaruḷittu || ip pe[r*] ṉūṟṟuvaril arivalattu
nilattāl per oṉṟum kaḷaccāttaṅ kuṭiyil per iraṇṭum nikkip per toṇṉūṟ
ṟeḻuvarkku coḻakeraḷanallūril nilamum nellum okkac certtu per iruvarkku kaiyyaṭai
oṉ[ṟā]ka ivvū[r*]ka kaṇakku eyiṉū[ru*]ṭaiyāṉ eḻuttāl eḻuti ivaṉum virarājentirap
pal[la]varaiyaṉum tirumuttiṉci[vi]-

kai peṟṟā[ṉu]m eḻuttiṭṭa kaiyyaṭaippaṭi kaiyyaṭai oṉṟukku cetta kār maṟu nilam irumā
varaikku māttāl nellu eṇkalaṉe iru tūṇippatakkāka irupattirukalane kuṟuṇiyum
oru pūnilam oṉpatumā mukkāṇikkum māttāl nellu aiṅkalaṉepatakku n[ā*]ṉāḻiyā
nellu aimpatiṉ kalaṉe iru tūṇi mukkuṟuṇi aṟunāḻi āka nilam araiye iraṇṭu
māk[kā]ṇiyāl varicaippa[ṭi ne]-

llu eḻupattu mukkalaṉe aṟunāḻi kuṟuṇivāci nellu aṟukalaṉe kuṟuṇi āka nellu
eḻupattoṉpatiṉ kalaṉe kuṟuṇi aṟuṉāḻi celavaḷavu vā[ci]nellu eḻukuṟuṇi iruṉāḻi
neṭṭāḷukku nellu irutūṇik kuṟuṇi paya[ṟu] kalaṉe iruṉāḻikku nellu iru kalaṉe
nāṉāḻi [ūrk]kaṇakkaṉukku uṟaināḻikku viṭṭa nilattāl nellu eḻukuṟuṇi muṉṉāḻi

taccaṉukku viṭṭa nilattāl nellu patakku nāvicaṉukku viṭṭa ṉilattāl nellu mukkuṟuṇi
oruṉāḻi āka nellu eḻupattoṉpatiṉ kalaṉe eḻukuṟuṇi kaḷaccāttaṅkuṭiyil opāti
nellu aiṅku[ṟu*]ṇi āka per iruvarkku nellu toṇṇūṟṟuk kalattukku kaiyyaṭai
oṉṟāka eḻuti eḻuttiṭṭatu

inscription.head:
No. 600.
(A.R. No. 555 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Rājēndra III. Year 20: 1266 A.D.

The details of date given viz. year 20, Makara śu. 13, Wednesday, Punarpūśam
regularly correspond to 1266 A.D., January 20, ·77, ·82.

This inscription records the grant of two pieces of land by Pirambar Viśaiyapālar
of Tiruviḍaimarudūr to the maṭha built for the tapasyar, who were tending the gardens of
the temple, doing other services in the shrines of gods Aḻagiya Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-
uḍaiyār and Poṟpatikku-nāyakīśvaramuḍaiyār, set up by the donor and Aḻagapperumāl
alias Kaḷappāḷarāyar of Śiṟunāṅgūr. One of the pieces of the land is
stated to have been purchased from Umaiyāḻvi alias Aḻagilumaḻagiyadēva-
ttalaikkōli, one of the dancing girls (padiyilār) living in the northern row of Irāśākkaḷ
tambirāṉ-tiruvīdi Sannadhi-ttiruvīdi. The mahāsabhā of Aḍiyakkai-chcharuppēdi-
maṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Tiruvārūr-kkūṟṟam in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu remitted
the taxes on the lands.

edition.l:
svastiśrīḥ tiripuvaṉaccakkaravarttikaḷ śrīrājendracoḻadēvarkku yāṇṭu 20
vatu makara nāyaṟṟu pūrvvapakṣattu trayodaśiyum budhaṉ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa
puṇarpūcattu nāḷ keyamāṇikka vaḷanāṭṭut tiruvārurk kūṟṟattu brahmadēśam
aṭiyakkaic caruppeti [ma]ṅkalattu mahāsabhaiyom vyavasthai [||*] celvat tiruvāruril
irācākkaḷ tampirāṉ tiruviti caṉṉatit tiruviti teṉ ciṟakil ciṟunāṅkūruṭaiyār aḻakap
perumāḷāṉa kaḷappāḷarāyarum

l.ta:
pirampar tiruviṭaimarutū[ru*]ṭaiyārāṉa [vi]caiyapālarum eḻuntaruḷivitta uṭaiyār aḻakiya
tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyārum uṭaiyār poṟ[pa]tikku nāyaki

yum celuttit tiruppaṇiyum ceyvittu tirunantavaṉamum ceytu tiruppaḷḷittāmamum
paṟittu sevittu irukkum ta

rkku ittiruviti vaṭaciṟakil patiyilāril umaiyāḻviyāṉa
aḻakilumaḻakiya tevat talaikkoli pakkal ivarkaḷ vilaikoṇṭu ka[ṟ]pitta maṭattukku
iṟaiyiliy[ā]kakkuṭu-

tta inta vicaiyapālar muṉṉāḷil irāyūr ārā amutupaṭṭaṉ pakkal vilaikoṇṭa irācavic
cātira vāykkālukkut teṉ pakkattu āyapperai oṭaiyeṉṟu per kūvappaṭṭa nilattukku
kiḻpārkkellai kārampicceṭṭu nampi muttapirāṉ paṭṭaṉ nilattukku meṟkum veṟpuṟattu
poṉṉampipaṭṭaṉuḷḷiṭṭār nilattukku vaṭakkum nam[pū]rk kāṭṭukkai cantirapūṣa[ṇa]paṭṭaṉ
uḷḷiṭṭār nilattukkuk kiḻakkum••••• [y]kkā-

inscription.head:
No. 601.
(A.R. No. 556 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Vikramachōḷa. Year 8: 1125 A.D.

This inscription commences with the praśasti Pūmādu puṉara, etc., of Vikrama-
chōḷa. The details of date given viz. year 8, Simha ba. 3, Tuesday, Rēvatī correspond
to 1125 A.D., August 18, f.d.t. ·09, f.d.n. ·27.

It records the gift of 17 kāśu for a perpetual lamp to god TiruvārUruḍaiyār ŚrImU
lasthānam-uḍaiyār by Gaggaicheṭṭi, son of Kudiraicheṭṭi (horse-dealer) Siddha
Cheṭṭi, a member of Vīrarājēndradēvamaṅgala-chchāttuThis Śāttu is perhaps a reference to a caravan of horse-dealers belonging to or camping at
Vīrarājēndradēvamaṅgalam. cf. MAśāttuvān and Sanskrit sārtha.in Mēlai-Mārāyapāḍi alias
Kōyiṟukkuṟu-nāḍu. This amount was received by the representatives of the body
called Śivabrāhmaṇar Nāṟpatteṇmar, holding leases of the temple, who agreed to
contribute oil, measured by tuḍaiyuḷakku, equivalent to Puṟṟiḍañgoṇḍāṉ nāḻi, out of
the interest from the endowed amount.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu [viḷa]ṅka jayamātu nilavat tannirupatu
mala[r] maṉṉavarcūṭa manniya urimaiyil maṇimuṭicūṭic ceṅkol cenṟu ticaitoṟum
vaḷarppa veṅkali niṅki meyyaṟa

ḷāḻi varaiyāḻi naṭātti irucuṭaraḷavum orukuṭainiḻaṟṟa cempon vira

inscription.head:
No. 602.
(A.R. No. 557 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.

Rājēndra III. Year 13: 1259 A.D.
Year 20: 1265-66 A.D.
Year 23: 1268-69 A.D.

This inscription records three grants on different dates in the reign of the same
king. The details of date given for the first grant viz. year 13, Mīṉa ba. 3, Monday,
Tiruvōṇam, are irregular as the combination is impossible.

The first grant is of three pieces of land, one in Paḷḷi and Mārāyamaṅgalam alias
Rājādhirāja-chcharuppēdimaṅgalam, a dēvadāna in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu and ano
ther in Perumpōr, a dēvadāna and another in Rājanārāyaṇa-chcharuppēdimaṅgalam, a
tiṟappu-vāḻ-kuḍi, the last made tax-free by the sabhā, of the same place for worship and
other services to gods Aḻagiya Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyār and Poṟpatikkunāyaka-
Īśvaramuḍaiyār, set up by them on the southern side of Rājākkaḷtambirāṉ-tiṟuvīdi
Sannadhittiruvīdi in Śelva-ttiruvārūr by Śiṟunāṅgūruḍaiyāṉ Araiyaṉ Aḻaga-pperumāḷ
alias Kaḷappāḷarāyaṉ of Pāmbuṇi-kkūṟṟam in Śuttamali-vaḷanāḍu and Pirambaṉ
Poṟpatikkunāyakaṉ Tiruviḍaimaruduḍaiyāṉ alias Vijayapālan of Ārkāṭṭukkūṟṟam
in Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu (See No. 600 above). The latter made two grants in the
20th and 23rd regnal year of the same king, of two fields in his own land in Perumpōr, a
dēvadāna in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu, respectively for worship and other services to
god TirumāḷigaiViṉāyaka-ppiḷḷaiyār and to god Subrahmaṇya-ppiḷḷaiyār, whose images
were set up by himself.

l.ta:
tiyaiyum tiṅkaḷkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattu nāḷ cutta
malivaḷanāṭṭu pāmpuṇikkūṟṟattu ciṟu nāṅkūruṭai-

inscription.head:
No. 603.
(A.R. No. 558 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra of the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.

This inscription engraved in characters of about the 12th century defines the
social status, rights, and duties of Rathakāras or Kammāḷar based on the scriptures,
āgamas and purāṇas which were examined in detail by the Brāhmaṇa residents of
Pāṇḍikulāntakachaturvēdimaṅgalam.

vāstu vidyākṛtābhyāsaśśalyōddhāraviśāradaḥ || lakṣaṇapramāṇē | sarvva-
lakṣaṇasampannō nīrōgaḥ kōpavarjjitaḥ | upāyajñassuśīlajñaḥ kṛtahastō
vicakṣaṇaḥ [||*] takṣakaśśāstrasampannō vinītō vāstuvit sadā [|*] pōstakaṁ
hastadhārī ca kṛtakṛtyassujātakaḥ [||*] prapañcataṅtrē lakṣaṇapramāṇē ||
prasiddhadēśē saṅkīrnnajātijōbhīṣṭa la•••••••

ṇaḥ [|*] vāstuvidyāvidhānajñastūhāpōha samanvitaḥ [|*] nimittaśśakuna
jyōtijñānē samyak prabō[dhakaḥ] [||*] vēdaviddharmmaviddhīmānācāryyaḥ prōkṣita-
[ssudhī]ḥ [|*] prāsādaliṅgakāryyēṣu mānōsura kāyakarmmabhiḥ ||
l.note: The passage here is faulty or contains an omission as the case may be.
l.gr:
—brahaiva
sthapatissākṣāt yajamānastu kēśavaḥ [|*] gurussarvasya kāryyasya mahādēvassa
kāraṇam—śrīpañcarātram kāpiṅ[jalam] [|*] bhuvam khātvā śilām paśyēdā
cāryyaśśilpibhissaha [|*] śilpiham pūjayēt kālē dhanadhānyagajādibhiḥ [||*]
paramapuruṣa saṅhitāyām ||••••• sārēṇa kārayēt••••
ṇam 2 śrīvaikhānasamahāstavē | śilpaśāstrāktavidhinā śilpinassamyagācarēt
[|*] tasmāt sarvvaprayatnēna ācāryyaśśilpibhissaha [||*] atriprōktē || vidhivat
sthāpayitvaivam śilpibhiśśāstrakōvidai[ḥ*] || āgastyavāstuśāstrē || svatantraḥ
paratantrajña[ḥ*] kṛtakṛtyassumānasaḥ [|*]

[dha]rmmajñassatyasampannassarvēndriyajitēndriyaḥ [|*] itihāsapurāṇajñaḥ smṛtivit
vāstuvit suthīḥ [|*] ata•• dha bhaktaśca alubdha[ścā]malātmakaḥ [|*]
nimittātām kramajñaśca [ci*]trakarmmaviśāradaḥ [||*] sarvāvayava niṣpannē vimānē
cōttarāyaṇē [|*] pra[śa]sta[pa]kṣanakṣatrē yajamānō janārddanaḥ•••••
••••••••• patiśca mahātmanaḥ••••śarīrāṅgam pācē
bhautikasaṅjñi(ta)ta[m |*] vimānam vidhinā samyak jalasaṅprōkṣaṇañcarē[t] [||*]
vāstuvidyāyām ||—viśvakarmmā [ca ā]cāryō gurutvāt viśvakarmmaṇām [|]
sthapatiḥ sthāpanam kurvan iti śilpi••••••••• sthapati-
ssa[tya]śāstrakarmmavicakṣaṇaḥ [|*] kṛtakṛtyaḥ kulīnaśca ahīnādhikalakṣaṇaḥ [||*]
dhārmmikaḥ satyavādī ca gaṇitajñaḥ purāṇavit [|*] citravit sarvvadēśajña
ssunāmāyamanāmayaḥ [||*] dṛḍhayōnasū-
l.note: The passage here is faulty or contains an omission as the case may be.

l.gr:
hāpōhavicakṣaṇaḥ || sārasvatīyē || viśvakarmmā ṛṣērnnāmnā
gurutvā[t] viśvakarmmaṇaḥ [|*] sthapatiḥ sthāpanam kurvvan iti śilpirvvidhīyatē |
tatvajñaḥ sthapatissarvaśāstrāṇāñca viśēṣataḥ [||*] dhāmarmikassatyavādī ca kṛta
kṛtya [ḥ*] kulōtbhavaḥ [||*]

svastiśrī |||—| vaiśyataḥ śūdrakannyāyām sañjātaḥ karaṇastriyām—asmādambaṣṭhatō
jā[t*]ō rathakāra iti smṛta[ḥ||*]

rathakāraḥ prajāyatē
[|*] nāsyōpanaya[na*]nnējyā nādhānañca niṣiddhyatē [||*] sūtrasya vājinō
vāstuśilpayōssyandanasya ca [|*] vidyayā[bhya*]dhika[ḥ*] svasya śarīram vartta-
yēdayam [||*] māhiṣya

lomarāna jātikaḷil iḻitoḻil ceyyomākavum eṉṟu coṉṉār•••

[yā*]dhānōpanayana kriyāścā pratiṣi[ddhatvā]t [||*] tasyāśvaratha sūtraśilpavāstu
vidyā dhanurvvi[dyā*]nvitāścēti [|*]

inscription.head:
No. 604.
(A.R. No. 559 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 39: 1216-17 A.D.

This inscription is dated in the 39th year of Tribhuvanavīradēva, a title assumed by
Kulōttuṅga III.

It records a grant of land to god Śrīmūlasthānamuḍaiyār of Tiruvārūr, by Tiru-
vambalanallūruḍaiyār Araiyaṉ Piḷḷaiyāḻvi for the merit of her son Aḻagiya Tiruchchi-
ṟṟambalamuḍaiyār. The former is stated to have had as her guardian, Kaṇṇandai
Araiyaṉ Rājarājadēvaṉ alias Aṇukkappallavaraiyaṉ of Vāḻaippandal in Palkuṉṟa-
kkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. The donor is also mentioned as Śōṟuḍai-
nāyakaṉ Piḷḷaiyāḻvi in line 13.

The inscription gives in details the situation of the land and the donor's title to it.
Śiṟukūṟṟanallūr in Vēḷāṉāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷaṉāḍu, Śiṟukaṇpūr in Muttūr-
kkūṟṟam in Rājarāja-Pāṇḍināḍu and Ammai-appanallūr are mentioned.

l.ta:
ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu palkuṉṟak koṭṭattu vāḻaippantal
kaṇṇantai araiyaṉ irā[ja*]rā[ja*]tevaṉāṉa aṇukkappallavarayaṉai mutukaṇṇāka uṭaiya
tiruvampalanallūruṭaiyān araiyaṉ piḷḷaiyāḻviyen uṭaiyār tiruvārur cimulattāṉa-

muṭaiyār koyil ātitaṇṭecuratevar tiru muṉpu eṉ makaṉ aḻakiya tirucciṟṟampala
muṭaiyārkku naṉṟāka ṉān nīr vār[t*]tukkuṭutta nilamāvatu kulottuṅkacoḻa
vaḷaṉāṭṭu veḷ[ā]ṉā[ṭ]ṭuc ciṟukūṟṟanallūril tiruvam[pa]lanallūruṭaiyāṉāṉa a-

raiyaṉ kaṅkaikoṇṭacoḻaṉ pakkal cītaṉam peṟṟuṭaiya potta[ka]paṭi nilam eṇ
veliyum nilam kalantavāṟṟāl eṉṟu eḻutiṉamayil iṉ nilam piṟittu taraveṇum
eṉṟu tiruvampalamuṭaiyāṉ araiyan kaṅkaikoṇṭacoḻaṉai-

ccoṉṉamaiyil ivar piṟittu tantapaṭiyāy kaiykoṇṭa kācu pāti yiṟukkap paṟṟāy uṭai
yār tiruvāruruṭaiyār[k*]kut tiruviḷakkuppuṟamāy oṭṭukkaṭaittu koyilttāṉattāṟ uṭal
koṇṭu varukiṟa pottakappaṭi nilam patiṟṟuveliyil eṉ opāti nilam a••-

raiye mukkāṇiyum aṉupavikkumiṭattu ūr nattattu vaṭakkaṭaiyavum kaṭuvāyāṟṟaṅkaraiyil
vaṭapakkatti[lu]m teṉpa[k*]kattilum kamuku niṉṟa nilattilum akayāṟṟupaṭukaiyilum
teṉpakkattu kiḻakkaṭaiya nikkavum viḷainilaṅkaḷ patiṟṟuveli vāykkālu-

kkukkiḻakku kiḻkaṇṭamāṉa nilaṅkaḷ kaṇṭam toṟum kiḻpakkatte nikkavum niṉṟa nilat
tukkutirai vaṭṭattu kulaikku ki[ḻa]kku teṉkaṇṭattu nilamum kiḻa[k*]kaṭaiya niṟka kaṭavatā
kavum tiruveṇkāṭṭu viḷākattuk kollai kiḻakkaṭaiya [ni]ṟka kaṭavatākavum iṉattoṟ
paṭṭi iṟai-

yili pottakappaṭi nilam veliyum cimākecuranallūruṭaiyāṟ vikkiramacoḷīcuramuṭaiyār
tevatāṉattu iṟukkum nilam onṟey [on]patu mākkāṇiyum ākap pottakappaṭi nilam
i[ra*]ṇṭe onpatu m[ā]kkāṇiyum payiṟ piṟittu koṇṭa ātittatevaṉ mayakkalil
meṟkaṭaiya nilam-

iraṇṭu māmukkāṇi civatavaṉamuṭaiyān ṉilam iraṇṭumā melaippiccatevan mayakkalil meṟ
kaṭaiya nilam eḻumāvaraiyil teṟkaṭaiya nilam iraṇṭu māvaraikkāṇiyum kūttapirān
mayakkalil kiḻkkaṭaiya nilam āṟumākkāṇi araikkāṇiyum kutiraiva[ṭ*]ṭattu vaṭa kaṇ-

kaṇṭattu arumoḻinaṅkai kiḻakkaṭaiya nilam mākāṇiyum tenkaṇṭattu meṟkkaṭaiya nilam
kaṭaiy nilam araiye orumāmukkāṇiyum itaṉ kiḻakku nilam āṟumāmukkāṇi nīkki kiḻak
kaṭaiy nilam mūṉṟu māmukkāṇiyum nāṟṟaṅkāl nilam kālum [pi]cca-

[n] tevaṉ toṭṭattu nilam ṉāṉmāvaraiyum āka nilam oṉṟe mukkāle nāṉmā
varai [i]n oṉṟe mukkāleṉā[ṉ]māvaraiyum kiḻai māḷikaiyil teṟkaṭaiyakkuḻi [e]ḻu
pattañcaraiyum••••ampalattukku vaṭakkup paḻaiyāṟu [kiḻaivaṉ]kuṭiiruppumaṉaiyāy
ci[ṟu]kaṇpūruṭaiyāṉ etirilico[ḻa*]nukku

vicattaṭaip parivattaṉai paṇṇi[ṉa] māḷikaiyu[m] taṉatu teṟkaṭaiyakkuḻi muppatum
ākakkuḻi nūṟṟañcaraiyum kuṭutta [nila]ttiṉ maṉaikuḻi muppattiraṇṭum tiruveṇkāṭṭu
viḷākattukkollaiyil kiḻakkaṭaiya nilam orumāmukkāṇi araikkāṇi muṉtirikaiyum
āka kollaivaṭṭa nilam iraṇṭe mākāṇi araikkāṇi muṉti-

rikaiyum nattam kuḻi nūṟṟumuppattu eḻaraiyum ākap pottakappaṭi nilam eṇveliyum
[u]ṭaiyāṟ tiruvara(r)rūr cī mulattāṉa muṭaiyār koyi[l*] ātitaṇṭecuratevaṟ tiru
muṉpu tiripuvaṉavirat[e]vaṟkku muppattoṉpatāvatu

kārmutal nir vāṟttu
kuṭutteṉ [eṉ]p pattar vāḻaippantalk kaṇṇantai araiyaṉ irā[ja*]rā[ja*]teva
ṉāṉa

aṇukkap pallavaraiyaṉai mutukaṇṇāka uṭai[ya] coṟuṭai nāyakaṉ piḷḷaiyāḻviyeṉ
iṉṉilattu tevatāṉa āṇṭu mutal ti[ru]nāmattukkāṇiyāṉa ammaiyappaṉallū[r*]
uṭaṉ kūṭṭi [u]ṭal koṇṭuvarukaiyil kamuku niṉṟa taṟai uḷḷiṭṭa nila[ttu me]l
[e]ṟṟamuṇṭākilu[m*] uṭal tuṇṭamākilum nāyaṉārkku āvatu iṉṉilam eṇveliyum
nāya-

ṉār[k*]ku nāyāraca paṭikku ivai aṇukkappallavaraiyaṉ eḻuttu || ippaṭi ippiramāṇattil
tiri[vi]ṭṭapaṭi aṟiveṉ (a) aṇukkappallavaraiyaṉ kaṇa[kku••••kiyaluṭaiy]āṉ eḻuttu:—
[ippaṭi] aṟiveṉ pūṇṭi uṭaiyāṉ veḷāṉa āḷa[va]ntāṉ eḻuttu:—ippaṭi aṟiveṉ
cāttampāṭi uṭai[yā*][n] eḻuttu:—ivarkaḷ veṇṭa ippiramāṇam eḻutineṉ
irā[ja*]rā[ja*] pāṇṭi nā-

ṭṭu muttūrk kūṟṟattu [ci]ṟukaṇpūruṭaiyāṉ ulakapi[maṉ]ṉāṉa etirili coḻaneṉ eḻuttu ||—
mulappiramāṇattil ippaṭikku ivai tiruvampalamuṭaiyāṉa••••ṇattā•• ṉ kaṅkai
koṇṭāṉ eḻuttu :—ippaṭikku ivai ciṟukaṇpūruṭaiyāṉ tirunaṭṭap perumāḷ
eḻuttu ||—ippaṭikku ivai ciṟukaṇpūruṭaiyāṉ tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu:—
ippaṭi aṟi-

veṉ tiruvampalamuṭaiyāṉ tiru[v]ā[cci]rā[ma]muṭaiyāṉ irā[ja*]rā[ja*]tevaṉāṉa
vikkiramacoḻa [aṅ]karāyaṉ eḻuttu ||

inscription.head:
No. 605.
(A.R. No. 560 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the second prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Vikramachōḷa. Year 3: 1120-21 A.D.

This epigraph commences with the praśasti, Pūmālai miḍaindu etc., of Vikrama-
chōḷa.

It records the gift of a piece of land, called Kaṟpūrakkuḍukkai, on the northern
banks of Kaḍuvāy alias Rājanārāyaṇa-ppērāṟu after making it tax-free to god Śrī-
mūlasthānamuḍaiyār of Tiruvārūr, by the Peruṅguṟi-mahāsabhai of Pulivalam,
brahmadēya in Puliyūr-nāḍu in Arumoḻidēva-vaḷanāḍu, when it was assembled at
Rajēndraśōḻa-atti (for attāṉi).

l.ta:
pūmālai miṭaintu ponmālai tikaḻtarap pāmālai miṭainta parumaṇittiraḷ
puyat tirunilamaṭan[t]aiyoṭu jayamakaḷiruppa kanavaraimārva[n] tanatenap peṟṟu tirumakaḷo
[ru ta][ni*]yiruppa ka[lai]ma[ka]ḷ coṟṟiṟam puṇar[ntaka]ṟ[pi]na[ḷāki virup]poṭu nāvaka
ttiruppa [ti]caitoṟum tikiriyoṭu ceṅkol naṭappa akila puvanamuṅ kavippa[to]r
putumatipola [v]eṇ[ku]ṭai melmicai ni[ṟ*]ppa karuṅkali oḷittu va-

ṉpilattiṭaik kiṭappa teliṅka(l) viman [vila]ṅka micaiyeṟa ka[liṅ]ka maṇ[ṭa*]laṅ kanaleri
parappi veṅkai maṇṭalaṅ kaṭa

tu tāṅkalar kaliṅkameḻum kanaleri parappi aimpaṭaip
paruvattu vempaṭai tāṅki vaṭa[ticai] aṭippaṭuttaruḷi tenṟicai[ttaru]mamum tavamum
tānamum taḻaippa vetamum me[y]mm[ai]yum ātiyukampol talaittalai ciṟappa [va]

l maṇimuṭi cūṭi mannuyiṟkell[ā] ni[n*]nuyirpola tannoḷi tankoyil koṟṟavācal
maṇināvoṭuṅka muraicukaḷ muḻaṅka vicaiyamu[m] pukaḻum me[l*] meloṅki ceḻiyar
veñcuram puka ceralar kaṭalpuka aḻita[ruci]ṅkaṇa[r a]ñ[ci n]eñca[la]ma[ra kaṅ]kar tiṟaiyiṭak
kanniṭar venniṭa koṅkar otuṅ[ka*] koṅkaṇar cāya maṟṟum etticaippaṭṭa mannarum
ta
ma[k*] karaṇena cempoṟ cevaṭi mimicai yi[ṟ*]aiñ-

ci tollai eḻum toḻuteḻa tonṟi mullaivāṇakai mukkokiḻānaṭi umaiyoṭu caṅka[ra]n
imai[ya]ttiruntenap porunti uṭaṉiruppa avaḷuṭan kaṅkai viṟṟirunta maṅkaiyar
tilatam ti[y]ākapatākaip purikuḻal maṭappiṭi [pu]ni[ta]kuṇavanitai tiripuvanamuṭaiyāḷ
avan tiru[vi]ḷa[ttu] aruḷumuḻutuṭaiyāḷena mukkokkiḻānaṭikaḷoṭum cem[p]on
vira

coḻa attiyile kūṭṭakkuṟai[va]ṟak kūṭiyi[ru]ntu paṇip
paṇiyāl paṇikka tiruvārur u[ṭ]ai[yār] śrī mula

ce[ytu] kuṭutta nilamāvatu e[ṅ]kaḷu[r] ka[ṭu]vāyāna rā[ja]nārā[ya]ṇap perāṟṟu vaṭakarai
tuṇṭappaṭṭa kaṟpū[ra]kkuṭuk[kaiye]n[ṟu] perkūvappaṭṭa nilattukku kiḻpāṟkellai
it

vatānam karuṇā[kara]nallūr ellaikku meṟkum ten[pāṟ]kellai kaṭuvāyāna rāja
nārāyaṇap perāṟṟu niroṭukālukku vaṭakkum melpār-

inscription.head:
No. 606.
(A.R. No. 561 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the second prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 49: 1118-19 A.D.
This record begins with the praśasti, Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc., of Kulōttuṅga I.

It seems to record some favour granted by god Vīdiviḍaṅgar of Tiruvārūr, in
Tiruvārūr-kūṟṟam in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu, when the god was seated in the hall
called Dēvāśriyaṉ, witnessing a dance performance by Pukkatuṟai-vallavattalaik-
kōli. As the inscription is incomplete other details are lost.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ puṇara
urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta maṇimuṭicūṭi mīṉavar nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara eṉai maṉnavar
[tiri]taluṟṟaḻitarat tikkaṉaittun taṉ cakka-

yaṉil eḻuntaruḷiyiruntu pukkatuṟai[va]
llavat talaikkoliyai āṭṭaṅkaṇṭaruḷā niṟka naṅkollaril toṇṭaimāṉ peruṅ
kollaṉ••••••••• periṭṭu na

inscription.head:
No. 607.
(A.R. No. 562 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the second prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Vikramachōḷa. Year 3: 1120-21 A.D.

This epigraph commences with the praśasti Pūmālai miḍaindu of Vikramachōḷa.
His surname, Rājakēsari, given here, is probably a mistake for Parakēsari.

It records the sale of land made tax-free by the sabhā of Vāyaṟṟūr, a brahmadēya in
Tiruvārūr-kūṟṟam in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu receiving 26(1/2) kāśu out of the amount
endowed to the temple by Arāvaṇai Pandakkūttaṉ Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa Amattiyarāyan,
a member of the regiment called Tyāgavalli Vēḷaikkāṟar. A Tyāgavinōdan-śālai maḍam
is also mentioned.

l.note: This inscription is very indifferently engraved.
l.ta:
pāmālai malinta paruma[ṇi]t tiraḷ
pu[ya]t tirunilamaṭan[t]aiyo[ṭu*] jeyamakaḷirup[pa]k kaṉavarai mārvan ta[ṉat]eṉap peṟṟu[t]
tirumakaḷoru[ta]niyiruppak kalaima[ka]ḷ coṟṟiṟa[m pu]ṇarnta kaṟpiṉaḷā[ki] viruppoṭu
nāvaka[t*]ti[ru*]ppa a[ki*]la puvaṉamuṅ kalippatorputuma[ti]pol veṇkuṭaimic[ai] [ni]ṟpak
karuṅkaliyoḷittu vaṉpilattiṭaikkiṭappak kuḷattiṭaitte[li*][ṅ]ka viman vi[la]ṅkal[mi]cai
y[e]ṟavum kaliṅkapumiyaik kaṉaleri parukavum ai(y)m[pa*]ṭaipparuvattu ve[mpa]ṭaitāṅki
veṅkai maṇṭalat tāṅkini[ti]runtacaippap pa[ṭu]ttaruḷitteṉṟicait tarumamun[tavamun]

inscription.head:
No. 608.
(A.R. No. 563 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the second prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Vikramachōḷa. Year 4: 1122 A.D.

This record commences with the praśasti, Pūmādu puṇara etc., of Vikramachōḷa.
The details of date mentioned viz., year, 4, Ṛishabha, śu. 3, Wednesday, Tiruvādirai,
regularly correspond to 1122 A.D., May 10, ·66 ·53.

It records the price-deed (poruḷmāvaṟudi) pertaining to the gift of land by purchase
for a tank to be called Neḍuṅguḷam, to the temple, from the Peruṅguṛi mahāsabhā of
Tirunallūr, a brahmadēya in Vēḷā-nāḍu, in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu when they were
assembled in Rājanārāyaṇan-tirumaṇḍapam, by Madhurāntakan Ponnambalakkūttan
alias Poṟkōyilnambi Toṇḍaimānār of Arumbākkam in Maṇavil-kōṭṭam. The land was
purchased for 100 kāśu given in gold currency of 32(1/2) kaḻañju equal in value to Rāja-
rājan-māḍai was endowed in order to supply Śeṅkaḻunīr flower for the god.

inscription.head:
No. 609.
(A.R. No. 564 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the second prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Vikramachōḷa. Year 4: 1121-22 A.D.

This record commences with the praśasti Pūmālai miḍaindu etc., of Vikramachōḷa.
The details of date given are the same as in the foregoing number.

This records the sale-deed (vilaiyāvaṇam) pertaining to the transaction recorded
in No. 608 above.

inscription.head:
No. 610.
(A.R. No. 565 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the second prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Vikramachōḷa.

This inscription stops after a few lines of the praśasti Pūmālai miḍaindu etc., of
Vikramachōḷa (1118-1135 A.D.).

l.ta: [pūmālai] miṭaintu [po]ṉ[mālai ti]kaḻtarap pāmālai ma[li]nta parumaṇittiraḷpuyat ti[ru]nilama-
[ṭa]ntaiyoṭu jayamaka[ḷi]ruppak kaṉavarai mārvan taṉateṉap peṟṟut tirumakaḷoruta[ṉiyi]
[ru]ppak kalaimakaḷ coṟṟiṟam puṇarnta kaṟpiṉaḷāki viruppoṭu nāvakattiruppa-
toṟu tikiriyoṭu ceṅkol naṭappa akila puvaṉamuṅ [ka*]vippator putuma-
veṇkuṭai mimīcai niṟpak karuṅkali yoḷittu vaṉpilattiṭaik kiṭappak kuḷattiṭai-
ṅka vīmaṉ vilaṅkal micai yeṟavuṅ kaliṅka pūmiyaik kaṉaleri parukavum aimpaṭaip-
paruvattu vempaṭai tāṅki veṅkai maṇṭalat tāṅkiṉitiruntu vaṭaticai aṭippaṭuttaru
l.note: The inscription ends here. The engraving has been done very indifferently.

inscription.head:
No. 611.
(A.R. No. 566 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
At the right side of the entrance into the western gōpura of the second prākāra in
Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
[Dēvarāya II]. Ś 1362: 1440 A.D.

This record mentions Mahāprādhāna Lakkaṇṇa Daṇṇāyaka Uḍaiyar. The details
of date given viz., Ś. 1362, Raudra, Bhādrapada ba. 1, Monday, Rēvati, regularly
correspond to 1440 A.D., September 12, ·48; ·83.

It records the erection of the gōpura as a service to god Valmīkanātha by Nāga-
raśar, son of Siddaraśar, of Vasishṭha gōtra and Āśavlāyana sūtra, for the merit of
Lakkaṇṇa-Daṇṇāyaka.

edition.l: prathamaiyu[ m so] mavāramum rē[va]-

inscription.p:
At the left side of the entrance into the western gōpura of the second prākāra of the
Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
[Dēvarāya II]. 1440 A.D.
This is a Kannaḍa version of No. 611 above.

l.ka: śubhamastu [||*] śakādba 176[2] ṟamēle saluva ra·udra saṁva-
tsarada bhādrapada ba 1 nōmavāra rēvatī nakṣatradalū

inscription.head:
No. 613.
(A.R. No. 568 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the Achalēśvara shrine in the south-east corner of the second
prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Rājēndra I. Year [8]: 1020 A.D. (?)

The praśasti Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara etc., of Rājēndra I is given at the beginning of this
epigraph.

It seems to record an undertaking given by the temple officials at Tiruvārūr in
Tiruvārūrkkūṟṟam in Kshatriyaśikhāmaṇi-vaḷanāḍu to arrange for the midday
offerings to god Tiruvaraneṟi Dhanma-viḍaṅgadēvar. The Śrīkāryam of this
temple was Nambaṉ Irāmaṉ alias Śōḻavijyā(vidyā)dhara-mūvēndavēḷāṉ of Tīya
ṅguḍi in Iṅgaṇāḍu in Kshatriya-śikhāmaṇi-vaḷanāḍu. Other details are lost
as the inscription is incomplete.

edition.l:
svastiśrī— tirumaṉṉi[va]ḷara irunilamaṭantai[yu]m poṟca[ya]ppāvaiyum cirttaṉic celviyun
taṉ A portion in the middle is lost which may be restored as perunteviya in line 1 and
kopparakecari in line 2. •••••rāki iṉpuṟaneṭu[tiyaluḻiuḷ] iṭaituṟ[ai]nāṭun tuṭarvaṉaveli
paṭarvaṉavāciyuñ cuḷḷic cūḻmatiḷ k[oḷ]ḷippākkaiyum [naṇṇa]ṟ karumuraṇ maṇṇaik

maṇṭala muḻuvatum eṟipaṭaik keraḷaṉ muṟaimaiyiṟ [cūṭuṅ kula]taṉamākiya palar pukaḻ
muṭiyu[ñ] ceṅkatir mālaiyuñ caṅkatirvelait tol peruṅkāvaṟ palpaḻantivum
ceruvi•••••

inscription.head:
No. 614.
(A.R. No. 569 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the Achalēśvara shrine in the south-east corner of the second
prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Rājarāja I. Year 23: 1007-08 A.D.

This inscription begins with the praśasti Tirumagaḷ pōla of Rājarājakēsari
Rājarāja I.

This seems to record the exemption from some taxes and poliyūṭṭu-kaḍamai due to
god Śrīyārūr dēvar on some pieces of land in lieu of 40 kāśu received from the servants
of the god Vishṇu••••••• who was pleased to be in the standing posture at maṉṟakam
by the peruṅguṟi sabhā of Vēlaṅguḍi, a brahmadēyam of Tiruvārūr-kkūṟṟam in
Kshatriyaśīkhāmaṇi-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
tirumaka[ḷ] polap perunilaccelviyun taṉak[ke] urimai pūṇṭamai maṉak
koḷak kāntaḷurccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅ[ki]nāṭuṅ kaṅkapāṭiyum nuḷampāṭiyun taṭikai
pāṭiyuṅ kuṭamalai nāṭuṅ kollamuṅ kaliṅkamum eṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻa maṇṭalamum iraṭṭa
pāṭi eḻarai ila•••••••••

yom innāṭṭu ikkūṟṟattu tiruvārur maṉṟa

ellai kiḻpāṟkellai ti[runāra]ṇa vāykkālu[k]ku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai uṭciṟu
vāykkālukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai uṟuppuṭṭūr

varite ivvūr

inscription.head:
No. 615.
(A.R. No. 569-A of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the Achalēśvara shrine in the south-east corner of the second
prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.

The beginning of this inscription is lost. On palaeographical considerations this
is assignable to 11-12th centuries A.D.

It records the sale of land, that was lying fallow, to the temple for reclamation
and utilisation of the produce for the flower-garlands and other services to god Tiru-
vārūruḍaiyār, by the sabhā of Vāyaṟṟūr, for a consideration of 60 kāśu.

l.ta:
aiyom iṉṉilam mu••••• [ḷa]ṟṟa••••m ku••••tiṭalu[m i]k kuḻiyumāy
kiṭantamaiyil iṉṉilan tirutti•••••••••

nālu tiruppaḷḷittāmattukkuṭpaṭa veṇṭu•ti•••••••

[t]oṟu[ñ]ceyyakkaṭavārkaḷākavum ikkācu aṟupatuṅkoṇṭu i[ṉṉi]lam•[raiye]
mākāṇiyum viṟṟukkuṭuttom uṭaiyār ti•••••••••

neṉ•ippaṭi [a]ṟiveṉ
kaucikan cirāmaṉ taṇṭiyeṉ ippaṭi aṟi[ve]•••••••••

••• [nu]•riya•• taṉmacivaneṉ••• itu•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 616.
(A.R. No. 570 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the Achalēśvara shrine in the south-east corner of the second
prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Rājarāja I. Year 2: 986-87 A.D.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 1.

inscription.head:
No. 617.
(A.R. No. 571 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the Achalēśvara shrine in the south-east corner of the second
prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Rājarāja I. Year 7: 992 A.D.

This inscription is dated in the 303rd day of the 7th regnal year of Rājarājakēsari-
varman, i.e., Rājarāja I.

This records an endowment of 234 kāśu equivalent to 200 kaḻañju as pudukku-
ppuṟam (capital funds for renovation) of the shrine for god Amarasundaradēva and
goddess Umā Bhaṭṭārakī whose images were set up by Uḍaiya-pirāṭṭiyār Śembiaṉ-
mahādēviyār in the temple of Tiruvaraṉeṟi Āḷvār at Tiruvārūr raised in stone by her.
Provision was also made from these funds for worship and other offerings for the deities.
The sabhā of Aḍiyappiya-chaturvēdimaṅgalam undertook to be in charge of the endow-
ment. Śembiyaṉ Ambar-nāṭṭukkōṉār was the Śrīkāryam officer.

l.ta:
ṉā[l*]
coḻanāṭṭu kāviri teṉkaraiy tiruvārur[k kū]ṟṟattu

ya ṉampar nāṭṭuk koṉā[r]meṉāyakattu tiruv[ā]ṉeṟi āḷvār koyil tirukkaṟṟaḷi
eḻu

kkum tiruvamutukkum tiruviḷakkeṇṇaikkum tirukkaṟṟaḷikkum cuṟṟumāḷikaikkum tiruppaḷḷi
kkaṭṭil maṇṭapattukkum putukku puṟamākavum pali[cai]kkiṭṭukkoḷkaveṉṟu aruḷicceytu
[vara]k kāṭṭiṉa kācu

ṉukku poṉ mukkāle iraṇṭu mañcāṭiyu mukkāṇiyāka ivvūr
ūr kallāl māṟi mutalā eṭu[t]tu paṇṭ[ā]ra[ttu] iru

ippoṉ
irunūṟṟu kaḻañcum yāṅkaḷ [ko]ṇṭu kaṭavom koṇ-

ṭa paricāvatu ippoṉ [iru]nūṟṟu [kaḻa]ñ[ci]lu[m mu]ppatiṉ ka[ḻañcu poliyi]ṉāl
nicatam uḻakkeṇṇai [tiru]nilakaṇṭattāl ko[yi]lu[k]ke koṭu ceṉṟu
aṭṭakkaṭavomākavum [ni]ṉṟa poṉ nūṟṟueḻupatiṉ kaḻañcālum āṇṭu
var[ai]
l.note: The rest of the inscription is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 618.
(A.R. No. 572 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
To the right of entrance on the west wall of maṇḍapa in front of the Achalēśvara
shrine in the south-east corner of the second prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 20.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 291.

inscription.head:
No. 619.
(A.R. No. 573 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Achalēśvara shrine in the south-
east corner of the second prākara in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple, right of entrance.
Parāntaka I. Year 32: 939 A.D.

This record is dated in the 232nd day of the 32nd regnal year of Maduraikoṇḍa
Parakēsarivarman, i.e., Parāntaka I. The inscription appears to be a later copy
as the characters are assignable only to the 12th century.

This records an undertaking given by the sabhā of Pandaṇainallūr, a brahmadēya
in Tiruvārūr-kūṟṟam to provide oil for a perpetual lamp for god Śrī Ārūr-ttiru-aṟaneṟi
Mahādēva Bhaṭṭāra[ka*]r out of the interest from 30 kaḻañju endowed for that purpose
by Śrīkōyil Dēvaṉār•••••

inscription.head:
No. 620.
(A.R. No. 574 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Achalēśvara shrine in the south-
east corner of the second prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 6.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 158.

inscription.head:
No. 621.
(A.R. No. 575 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Achalēśvara shrine in the south-
east corner of the second prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.
Vīra-Bhūpati-Uḍaiyar. 1429 A.D.

This epigraph is dated in the reign of Mahāmaṇḍalīśvaran Śrī Vīra-Bhūpati-
Uḍaiyar. The date given Kīlaka, Māśi 28, may correspond to 1429 A.D., February 22.
This seems to record a royal decree for the merchants (vāṇikar).

l.ta:
tiyati uṭaiyar aruḷic ce[y]tapaṭi•• kal v[e]••••• [coḻa]maṇṭa[la]
ttil vāṇi[ka]r•• m
iṭṭu iṟukkakkaṭava[tāka]•••••• kkarai paṭikkattā••••• [al]la•• [l]laracar maṉaikku kiḻakku

••••• [ko]yilukkum tiruvitikkum vaṭakku tirumaṇimuttāṟṟukku teṟku vāṇiyar kaṭai
yum

l.note: This inscription is very badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 622.
(A.R. No. 576 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the third prākāra in the Tyāgarājasvāmin temple.

This inscription in Sanskrit engraved in late characters, mentions Ānandēśvara
and Dēvatīrtha. Records a certain pious act done by Brāhmaṇas.

l.gr: sya pūrvvataḥ | ā[na]ndēśvaranāmnā tat sarvva pa••
l.note: Built in at the right end.

inscription.head:
No. 623.
(A.R. No. 577 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Satyavāchakēśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga I.

This inscription commences with the praśasti Pugaḻ śūḻnda puṉari, etc., of the
king. Probably this is the original grant mentioned in No. 625 below and if so,
though the date portion is lost here, it may be restored as the 26th year of the king's
reign.

It seems to record the sale of land made tax-free to god Tirumaṇḍaḷi uḍaiya
Mahādēva by the sabhā of Pandaṇainallūr, a brahmadēya in Tiruvārūr-kkūṟṟam in
Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu, receiving 4 kaḻañju and 8 mañjāḍi of gold equivalent to 8
kāśu from [Paṭ]ṭaśālichch[i] Śellūriṟ-Kāri Pavaḻakkuṉṟu alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira
An[ṉad]āna Naṅgai, for the feeding of śivayōgis and tapasyar on New moon days.

l.ta:

••••••••• [ka]rattuvāri a••••• t toḷvali kāṭṭip poṟpari naṭāttik kirtti[yai]

raṇenap pukka kāṭaṟattuṭaittu nāṭṭip paṭuttu maṟṟavar tammai vanaca[ra*]r tiriyum poṟṟa veñcura

inscription.head:
No. 624.
(A.R. No. 578 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Satyavāchakēśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 48: 1117-18 A.D.
This inscription commences with the praśasti Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc., of the king.

It records an allotment of fallow lands belonging to the temple to various persons,
for reclamation and contribution of paddy for śrībali and other offerings to God
Ālaviḍaṅgadēvar, the deity taken out on procession in the temple, his consort Paḷḷi-
aṟai Nambirāṭṭiyār of the inner apartment and Chandraśēkharadēva. Puliyan
Arumoḻidēvaṉ, the kiḻavaṉ of Pālaiyūr who appears as the signatory of the record
was one of the persons to whom land was allotted.

inscription.head:
No. 625.
(A.R. No. 579 of 1904.)
TIRUVĀRŪR, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Satyavāchakēśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 27: 1096-97 A.D.

This inscription commences with the praśasti Pugaḻ śūlnda puṇari of KulōttuṅgaI.
Though the portion containing the date is lost in the body of the grant it is stated that
this grant issued in the 27th year became necessary as the endowment already made
in the 26th year was found insufficient.

It records the sale of land, made tax-free, to god Tirumaṇḍaḷi-uḍaiya Mahādēva
probably by the same sabhā as in No. 623 above, receiving 6(3/4) and mākāṇi kāśu
endowed for offerings on specified occasions and for feeding Śivayōgis and Māhēśvaras,
by Paṭṭaśā••••• [Śe]llūr-kkāri Pavaḻa-kkuṉṟu alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-vannadānanangai
Tāḻi-Nindi and Śellūr-kkāri Pāṟpati.

inscription.head:
No. 626.
(A.R. No. 580 of 1904.)
KAḶḶA PERUMBŪR, TANJORE TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Kailāsanātha temple.
Rājēndra III. Year 12: 1257-58 A.D.

In this inscription in verse the king is described as one who has captured the crown
and city of the Pāṇḍya king and as Vīrarākshasa and Rājākkaḷ-tambirāṉ.cf., S.I.I. Vol. IV. No. 511.

It seems to record a grant of land to god Śiva by Mallīśaṉ, probably a chief of
Ambāḷūr.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] veṉṉikaḻuma rājākkaḷ tampirāṉeṉ-

inscription.head:
No. 627.
(A.R. No. 581 of 1904.)
KAḶḶA PERUMBŪR, TANJORE TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the central shrine in the Kailāsanātha temple.
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 49: 1118-1119 A.D.

In this inscription only the date, year 49, is mentioned. The palaeography,
the high regnal year and the name Rājasundari-chaturvēdimaṅgalam suggest that
this inscription belongs to the reign of Kulōttuṅga I.

It records some injunctions regarding the eligibility of candidates for selection
framed by the Peruṅguṟi Mahāsabhā of Rājasundari-chcharuppēdimaṅgalam.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] rājacuntaric caruppetimaṅkalam yāṇṭu 49 āvatu sabhā vivastai pa-

inscription.head:
No. 628.
(A.R. No. 582 of 1904.)
KAḶḶA-PERUMBŪR, TANJORE TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the central shrine in the Kailāsanātha temple.
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 49: 1118-19 A.D.
This record commences with the praśasti Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc., of Kulōttuṅga

It records the grant of land as maḍappuṟam for feeding the devotees of the god
by••••Tillaiyār Kulā Araṉ Teṟpiḍāraṉ Iraṇḍāirappichchan, a tapasi resident in
the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of god Mahādēva at Śrī Kayillāsam (Kailāsam) alias Kulōt-
tuṅgaśōḻa-Īśvaram in Rājasundari-chcharuppēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 629.
(A.R. No. 583 of 1904.)
KAḶḶA-PERUMBŪR, TANJORE TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Kailāsanātha temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 19: 1234-35 A.D.

This inscription records the dismissal of the accountant named Śeñalūruḍaiyāṉ
Madaḷai Uttamapiriyan, who held a lease in the village, from his post for grāmadrōha
and divesting the right of his progeny to that post, by the Peruṅguṟi mahāsabhā of
Rājasundari-charuppēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Tañjāvūr-kūṟṟam, in Pāṇḍya
kulāśani-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
vaṟku yāṇṭu pattonpatāvatu
nāḷ pāṇṭikulācani vaḷanāṭṭu tañcāvūṟ kūṟṟat-

inscription.head:
No. 630.
(A.R. No. 584 of 1904.)
KAḶḶA-PERUMBŪR, TANJORE TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the gōpura of Kailāsanātha temple, right of entrance.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 25: 1202 A.D.

This inscription describes the king as Kulōttuṅga who conquered Madurai and
Īḻam and captured the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya king. The details of date given
viz., Year 25, Kaṟkaṭaka śu. 5, Wednesday, Uttiram correspond to 1202 A.D., July
24, f.d.t. ·96; ·52.

It records a gift of land by purchase to the god in the temple at Śrīkayilāyam
alias Kulōttuṅgachōḷīśvaram by Vikra[ma]chōḻa Pāṇḍiya[Śe]•kuḻa•• li Śrī Rāma
Bhaṭṭan of Rājasundari-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Tañjāvūr-kūṟṟam
in Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 631.
(A.R. No. 585 of 1904.)
KAḶḶA-PERUMBŪR, TANJORE TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the gōpura of Kailāsanātha temple, left of entrance.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 23: 1200 A.D.

The king who is described in this inscription as Kulōttuṅga who conquered Madurai,
Īḻam and Karuvūr and captured the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya is evidently identical
with Kulōttuṅga III. The details of date given, viz., year 23, Vṛiśchika ba. 13, Monday,
Viśākham, correspond to 1200 A.D., November 6, ·52; f.d.n. ·84.

It records the gift of land in Amaṇguḍi in Śrī Rājasundari-chchaturvēdimaṅgalam
a brahmadēya in Tañjāvūr-kkūṟṟam in Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu to god Uḍaiyār at Śrī-
kayilai alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Īśvaram by Āṇḍāṉpiḷḷai alias Ādivarāhabhaṭṭaṉ alias Śīkā-
ḻippiḷḷai of Virudarājabhayaṅkarachchēri in Āmbalūr. It is stated that the gift land
was acquired by the donor partly by inheritance from his father on his death and partly
by purchase from his brother.

edition.l: [ha]ra svastiśrī [||*] ti[ri]puvaṉaccakkaravattika[ḷ ma]-
