m mākāṇi araikkāṇik kiḻaraiyum iṟṟai nāḷāl

inscription.head:
No. 632.
(A.R. No. 586 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 2.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 2.

inscription.head:
No. 633.
(A.R. No. 586-A of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the walls of the Gaṇēśa shrine, in the Rāmanātha temple.
On palaeographical grounds this inscription may be assigned to the 10th century.

It seems to record a sale of land by the mūlaparaḍai of a village apparently the
findspot, to Perumāṉaḍigaḷ of Śrīnārāyaṇapuram, receiving 16(1/2) kaḻañju weighed
by kuḍiñaikkal, It mentions Vēḷūr-kkūṟṟam and a person Vēṭkōvaṉ Kēśaṉakkaṉ.

l.ta: lattu mulaparaṭaiyom śrīnārāyaṇapurattup perumāṉaṭikaḷukku eṅkaḷ vaya••••

inscription.head:
No. 634.
(A.R. No. 586-B of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
On palaeographical grounds this may be assigned to the 10-11th century.

It seems to record a grant of land, purchased for 55 kaḻañju. It mentions Nak-
kaṉārāyaṇi-Tiruvi•••• the wife of Ōmachcha[Nārā]ṇa Kiṇaṉ and Tiyambakaṉ
Māṉaṉ, the madhyasthan of the paraḍai. Mahēndra[maṅgalam], a brahmadēyam
mentioned in this record is obviously an ancient name of Śrīnivāsanallūr.

l.ta: nilattukke teṟkkum pettu nilattukku vaṭakkum kavarukkuk kiḻakku [va]•••••

inscription.head:
No. 635.
(A.R. No. 586-C of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Māṟavarmaṉ Vikramapāṇḍya.

This inscription contains portion of the praśasti Tirumagaḷ Jayamagaḷ etc., of
Māṟavarmaṉ Vikramapāṇḍya.

This mentions Śrī Rājarāja-chaturvēdi[maṅgalam]. Other details are lost as the
inscription is fragmentary.

inscription.head:
No. 636.
(A.R. No. 587 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south and east walls of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 29: 935-36 A.D.
This inscription is dated in the reign of Madirai-koṇḍa Parakēsari i.e., Parāntaka I.

It records the sale of land as dēvadāna made free of the taxes iṟai and veṭṭi to Śrī
Kurakkuttuṟai-pperumāṉaḍigaḷ by the Mūlaparaḍai of Mahēndramaṅgalam, a brahma-
dēya for 156(1/2) kaḻañju received from Tirukkuṟakkuttuṟai Nambi alias Pāṉachchaṉ
Nārāyaṇañ[chokkaṉ].
The name of the madhyasthan of the paraḍai is given as Indaṇa Mūvaṇaṉ.

rakkuttuṟaip perumāṉaṭikaḷiṭaip palavakaiyālumākak koṇṭa tuḷaippoṉ kuṭiñai
tta[ṟu] kaḻañcey eṭṭumañcāṭip poṉṉukkut tirukkurakkutuṟaip perumāḷukku

inscription.head:
No. 637.
(A.R. No. 588 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 7.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 158.

inscription.head:
No. 638.
(A.R. No. 589 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 37: 944-45 A.D.
This inscription is dated in the reign of Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsarivarmaṉ.

It records a grant of 12 kaḻañju for a perpetual lamp, one nilaiviḷakku, a copper
pitcher for the occasion of the eclipse and one silver platter for aḍaikkāy-amudu to god
Paramēśvara of Tirukkurakkuttuṟai in Mahēndramaṅgalam by Pāgaṉ Śaṟpadēvaṉ
alias Śembiyaṉ Kiḻā[r]nāṭṭukōṉ of Kiḻinallūr in Kiḻā[r*]kūṟṟam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] matirai koṇṭa kopparakesari[pa]rmmaṟku yāṇṭu 37 ā[va]tu
kiḻā[r*] kūṟṟattuk kiḷi[na]llūr kiḷinallūr kiḻavaṉ pākaṉ caṟ-

haṇattukku ceppu kuṭam oṉṟum aṭaikkā

inscription.head:
No. 639.
(A.R. No. 589-A of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 30: 936-37 A.D.

This damaged inscription gives only the regnal year 30 of a king whose name
is lost. Palaeography, the mention of Kāppiyaṉ Eḻuvaṉ Gaṅgādharan
who figures in another inscription from the same place (S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 2.)
ascribed to the reign of Gaṇḍarāditya and the high regnal year point to the identity
of the king of the record with Parāntaka I.

It seems to record a sale of land made free of taxes, like iṟai, echchōṟu, veṭṭi
and vediṉai to the god for a tiruviḷakku by the mūlapariḍai for the gold received for
the purpose from Kāppiyaṉ Eḻuvaṉ Gaṅgādharaṉ.

l.ta: ṉum••••[ni]lattu muṉṟu mācceyyum iṟaiyiliyākak kuṭu[t*]teṉ•• iṭa

• eḻuvaṉ kaṅkā[tara]nen i[ni]lattāl vaṉta iṟaiyum e[c*]coṟum veṭṭiyum vetiṉai

inscription.head:
No. 640.
(A.R. No. 590 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 30: 936-37 A.D.

This inscription is dated in the reign of Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsarivarman i.e.,
Parāntaka I.

It records a gift of land to Tirukkurakkuttuṟai Perumāṉaḍigaḷ of Mahēndra-
maṅgalam, a brahmadēya, for a lamp by Kāḷi Nīli the brāhmaṇi of Pāṉachchaṉ
Nārāyaṇaṉ Kṛishṇaṉ of that place. Pāṉachchaṉ Nārāyanaṉ-Chēndan was her
guardian. The land is stated to have been acquired from her husband for her
maintenance (śōṟu-kūṟai).

maṅkalattu tirukkurakkuttuṟai perumāṉaṭikaḷuk-

inscription.head:
No. 641.
(A.R. No. 591 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Parāntaka I. Year [3.]

This inscription is dated in the reign of Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsarivarman. The
date portion is damaged. Only 3 is visible.

This records a sale of land to [Tirukkurakkuttuṟai]p-Perumāṉaḍigaḷ for 2 kaḻañju.
The portion containing the other details is damaged. This also records another
endowment of 5 kaḻañju for a day lamp to god Perumāṉaḍigaḷ.

kaḷukku%

inscription.head:
No. 642.
(A.R. No. 592 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 2[4].
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 306.

inscription.head:
No. 643.
(A.R. No. 593 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Parāntaka I. Year 20: 926-27 A.D.

This inscription is dated in the reign of Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsarivarman.
A transaction made in the 19th year is also mentioned.

It records a sale of land made free of the taxes, iṟai, echchōṟu, veṭṭi vedaṉai, to
Tirukkurakkuttuṟaip-Perumāṉaḍigaḷ, by the mūlaparaḍai of Mahēndramaṅgalam,
a brahmadēya receiving gold worth 233 kaḻañju and 5(1/2) mā, this amount being probably
the dues on account of the pañchavāranelluFor the exact significance of this term see. Ep. Ind., Vol. XXIII pp. 23-24.to Kuṟumbiluḍaiyār and Nāraṇamaṅgala-
muḍaiyār, by Gāṉgayattu Śōḻakōṉār.

nilam oṉṟe muṉṟu mākkāṇi nilattāl vanta iṟaiyum eccoṟum veṭṭivetaṉai

inscription.head:
No. 644.
(A.R. No. 594 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 14.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 340.

inscription.head:
No. 645.
(A.R. No. 595 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 21: 1090-91 A.D.
This record commences with the praśasti Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga, etc., of Kulōttuṅga I.

This mentions Mīmalai Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-chchaturvēdimaṅgalam and Rājēndrap-
perunteru. Other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 646.
(A.R. No. 596 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 2.

No. 648.
(A.R. No. 598 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
Rājēndra I.

This inscription contains only a portion of the praśasti Tirumaṉṉivaḷara of
Rājēndra I.

inscription.head:
No. 649.
(A.R. No. 599 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the Paṭṭābhirāmasvāmin shrine in the Viśvanāthasvāmin
temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 7.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 159.

inscription.head:
No. 650.
(A.R. No. 600 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the Paṭṭābhirāmasvāmin shrine in the Viśvanāthāsvāmin
temple.
Rājakēsarivarman. Year 8.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 329.

inscription.head:
No. 651.
(A.R. No. 601 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the Paṭṭābhirāmasvāmin shrine in the Viśvanātha-
svāmin temple.
Kulōttuṅga I. Year 22: 1091-92 A.D.

On palaeographical grounds this inscription may be assigned to the 11th century
and hence the king may be identified with Kulōttuṅga I.

This records the undertaking given by the anvayattār and the brahmachārins
(māṇ-śeydār) of the temple to burn a sandhi lamp with the interest accruing from
2 kāśu received from Nittal Kiḷaiyōmbi, the headman of Vāyaṟṟūr, a merchant
residing at Toṭṭiyam.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] kolottuṅka-

inscription.head:
No. 652.
(A.R. No. 602 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the west wall of the Paṭṭābhirāmasvāmin shrine in the Viśvanāthasvāmin
temple.
Rājarāja I. Year 22: 1006-07. A.D.

This record commences with the praśasti, Tirumagaḷ pōla, of the king and stops
after citing the regnal year.

inscription.head:
No. 653.
(A.R. No. 603 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the wall of the kitchen in the Viśvanāthasvāmīn temple.
Adhirājēndra. Year 3: 1070-71 A.D.

This fragmentary inscription contains a portion of the praśasti, Tiṅgaḷēr malarndu
of the king.

This records a gift of land for cultivating a garden. Mīmalai Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Vīrarājēndra-vaḷanāḍu is also mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 654.
(A.R. No. 604 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the floor of the Viśvanāthasvāmin temple.
Rājakēsarivarman.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 328.

inscription.head:
No. 655.
(A.R. No. 604-A of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone built into the floor of the Viśvanāthasvāmin temple.
Year 29.

This inscription is engraved in characters of about the 12th century. Only the
title Tribhuvanachakravartigaḷ of the king is seen and the other details are lost in the
right end portion.

This seems to record the gift of an ornament and a gōmukha (an outlet for water
attached to a pedestal in the form of a cow's mouth) by Nambippiḷḷai.

l.note: There is the figure of a lamp drawn in bold relief to the left of the first two lines.

inscription.head:
No. 656.
(A.R. No. 605 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east base of the Lakshmīnārāyaṇa Perumāḷ temple.

Parakēsarivarman. Year 38.
See Appendix

inscription.head:
No. 657.
(A.R. No. 606 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
At the entrance into the central shrine in the Rāmanāthasvāmin temple, right
side.
Rājarāja II. Year 7+1: 1153-54 A.D.

On grounds of palaeography this inscription may be assigned to the 12th century,
and the king may be identified with Rājarāja II.

It records the gift of a door-post to god Tāyilunallīśira(śvaram)uḍaiyār
by Tāyilunalla-vēḷāṉ, Jayaṅgoṇḍa-vēḷāṉ and Tiruchchiṟṟambala-vēḷāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 658.
(A.R. No. 607 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
At the entrance into the central shrine in the Rāmanāthasvāmin temple, left
side.
Rājarāja II. Year 7+1: 1153-54 A.D.

For the reason stated above in No. 657 the king of this inscription also may be
identified with Rājarāja II.

It records a gift of a gōmukha by one Śiṅgapperumāḷ alias Tirunīṟṟuchchōḻa-
Mūvēnda-vēḷāṉ to god Tāyilunallīśuramuḍaiyār.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] irā

inscription.head:
No. 659.
(A.R. No. 608 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Rāmanā-
thasvāmin temple.
Kulōttunga I. Year 42: 1111-12 A.D.
This inscription begins with the praśasti, Pugaḻśūḻnda puṇari, etc., of the king.

It records a gift of tax-free land for gardening by the sabhā of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam. It also refers to a previous gift in the 32nd regnal year of the
king (1102 A.D.) by the Peruṅguṟi-sabhā of the same place for performing the sacred
bath and for food offerings to god Tirunārāyaṇapurattāḻvān on the day of Puṇarpūśa,
the birth day asterismcf. A.R. Ep. 1947-48, Int., p. 2 where the star on the day of accession of Kulōttugga I is consi-
dered to be Pushya which is the next after Punarpūśa i.e., Punarvasu.(?) of Kulōttugga I.

inscription.head:
No. 660.
(A.R. No. 608-A of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Rāmanā-
thasvamin temple.
Kulōttuṅga I.

Of the three unconnected piecesThe first two lines of pieces 1 and 2 are engraved in characters different from those of the
other lines.below, piece 1 refers to some exchange of land,
and piece 2 contains a portion of the praśasti Pugaḻ śUḻnda etc., of the king.

edition.l: ttu terkkaṭaiya nilam iṅke 2- catirattu•tarat

l.ta: pola viramum tiyākamum viḷaṅ(ṅ)ka pār to
kku kiḻakku rājarāja vāykkālukku vaṭakku ma••

inscription.head:
No. 661.
(A.R. No. 609 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Gaṇēśa shrine in the Rāmanāthasvāmin temple.
Parāntaka I. Year lost.

This fragmentary inscription seems to record a transaction (details lost) made
by the mūlaparaḍai.

edition.l: svastiśrī ||— matiraikoṇṭa koppa- Fragmentary.

inscription.head:
No. 662.
(A.R. No. 610 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Māriyammaṉ temple.
Parakēsarivarman. Year 6.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 159.

inscription.head:
No. 663.
(A.R. No. 611 of 1904.)
VĪRAVALLI (NEAR LĀLĀPĒT), WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the rock in the Paḷḷar street.
Mallikārjuna. Ś. 1405: 1483. A.D.

The details of the date viz., Śaka 1405, Kumbha, Rōhiṇi are insufficient for veri-
fication.
Since the inscription is very badly damaged, other details are lost.

edition.l: •••••ṭāt[ āṉ pū] rvadakṣiṇa paści••••ta••

inscription.head:
No. 664.
(A.R. No. 612 of 1904.)
MĀMAṆḌŪR, CHEYYAR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Maṇḍūkanāthēśvara temple.
Rājarāja I. Year 27: 1011-12 A.D.
This inscription begins with the praśasti Tirumagaḷ pōla of the king.

This records the gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp to god Māndai Nakkar of
Māvaṇḍūr, a hamlet of Madhurāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

l.ta:
tirumakaḷ pola perunilac celviyum taṉakke yurimai pūṇṭamai [maṉakkoḷa]k kān
taḷūrccā-

l.note: There is apparently a mistake here and the continuation of the passage up to line 5 is lost.

edition.l: yāṇṭu [ 27- vatu maturā] ntaka caturvētimaṅkalattu

l.ta: nuntā viḷakku oṉṟiṉāl vaitta āṭu toṇṇūru [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 665.
(A.R. No. 613 of 1904.)
TIRUVANDAVĀR, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Tiruvandanāthēśvara temple.
Rājanārāyaṇa Śa[m]buvarāya. Year 8: 1346 A.D.

The details of the date, viz., Monday, dvitīyā and Aśvati will correspond to 1346
A.D., October 2, Monday, f.d.t. ·37, ·22. The month was Tulā and the paksha bahuḷa.

This fragmentary inscription seems to record some gift to a god and goddess
(nāyaka-nāchchiyār) and refers to Madhurāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

l.ta: ṟṟil nāyaka nācciyārkku vaṭapiṭākaippaṟṟil nāṭṭār ma

inscription.head:
No. 666.
(A.R. No. 613-A of 1904.)
TIRUVANDAVĀR, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine of the Tiruvanda-
nāthēśvara temple.

This fragmentary record may be assigned to the 11-12th century, on grounds
of palæography.

It mentions Vānavaṉ-nallūr apparently separated from Vaḍaśālaippākkam and
Śiṟṟaṇaikkāvūr in Kaḷattūrkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍa-Śōḻa-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 667.
(A.R. No. 613-B of 1904.)
TIRUVANDAVĀR, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine of the Tiruvanda-
nāthēśvara temple.

On palæographical grounds this fragmentary record may be assigned to the 11th-
12th centuries.

It refers to the tāṉattār of the temple and mentions a śivabrāhmaṇa, Kāśyapan
Āḷuḍaiyāṉ Kāyappāttu Tiruvēṅkaḍa-bhaṭṭan.

inscription.head:
No. 668.
(A.R. No. 614 of 1904.)
TIRUVANDAVĀR, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine of the Tiruvanda-
nathēśvara temple.
Vikramachōḷa.

The date is lost in the damaged portion of the inscription. This inscription
records a gift of 17 sheep () by one Nārāyaṇa alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyirakkōṉ of a village in
Kaḷattūrkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam towards part of the cost of burning
a perpetual lamp.

l.ta:
yaril nārāyaṇaṉāṉa nāṟpatteṇṇāyirak koṉeṉ innāyaṉāṟku araittirunun-
tāvi•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 669.
(A.R. No. 615 of 1904.)
TIRUVANDAVĀR, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the village.
Achyutarāya. 1536-37 A.D.

The details of date, viz., Duṉmukhi, daśami, Tiruvōṇam are insufficient for
verfication. The cyclic year will however correspond to 1536-37 A.D. falling within
the reign of Achyuta.

It seems to record the assignment of the income from jōḍi realised from Tiruvanda-
vāṟu for worship to the local deity (name lost) by Tīttāra Piḷḷai for the merit of
the king.

l.note: The Sāḷuva title is evidently a mistake of the draftsman.

inscription.head:
No. 670.
(A.R. No. 615-A of 1904.)
TIRUVANDAVĀR, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
On the same slab.
inscription.p:
This inscription in modern characters seems to record some gift made for the
merit of Kōdaṇḍarāmayaṉ, the agent of••••kkollā Sāyabu (Saheb) in the year Vikāri,
Āḍi 17.

edition.l: mātam tam 17- śrīmatu •

inscription.head:
No. 671.
(A.R. No. 616 of 1904.)
TIRUVANDAVĀR, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in a field to the east of the village.
Rāmadēva II. Ś. 1547: 1625 A.D.

The details of the date, viz., Śaka 1547, Krōdhana, Vṛiśchika, śu. 7 are insufficient
for verification.

It records a gift of land by one Veṅkaṭṭappa-nāyakkar, grandson of Tāmal Veṅ-
kaṭappa-nāyakkar, and son of Rāyasa Cheṉṉappa, to Pērambala-Paṇḍāram and others
for their services to god Chē(Kshē)ttira-īśvarar of Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟam.

edition.l: tam 1547- kku-

l.note: The rest of the inscription is much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 672.
(A.R. No. 617 of 1904.)
IRUGUṈṞAPPAḶḶI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in a garden.

This inscription in modern characters seems to record that Vēlikkoṭṭu Śiṅgama-
Nāyakkarayyaṉ arranged through Pārupatyam Śimmaṇa-Rāvuttar for the settlement
of people of different castes in the maḍaviḷāgam. It was also stipulated that they
should, besides contributing 1/4 paṇam to god Rāmanāthasvāmin reconsecrated in
Dunmati, Tai, 6, conduct the festivals and arrange for other connected services.

l.ta:
ḷā[ka*]tti[l*] ēṟiṉa kuṭi n[ā*]lā-
cātikkum yi-
ṟai mācam moṉṟukku kāl paṇam vi-

inscription.head:
No. 673.
(A.R. No. 618 of 1904.)
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the slopes of the hill.
Vēnkaṭa.

This inscription in late characters mentions Aṅgama Nāyaka, the daḷavāy of the
king in late characters king. It mentions the date Īśvara, Kārttigai.

It seems to record a royal order and mentions mariyādam. Other details are
lost as the inscription is fragmentary.

inscription.head:
No. 674.
(A.R. No. 618-A of 1904.)
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the slopes of the hill.

This inscription mentions Daḷavāy Ṉāyakar and the king's mudramanusha whose
name is damaged.

It seems to record a grant of land and mentions the villages Maṇappākkam,
Aṟupadukuppam, Udayambākkam and Paḻanai [Pūṇ][ḍi*].

l.ta: avarkaḷ muṟpāṟ paṭikāl a••• tarmamāka koṇṭu po[ka]-
poṭṭu kā[lume] koṇṭu pokakkaṭavarākavum utayampākkam tā-
viṭṭu manaiyum [kāc]āl koṇṭu po[ka]vum maṇappākkam kuca-
••kku aṟupatu kuppam viṭṭu paḻanai [pūṇ] [ṭi*] teṟke•• cama••
to•• ḷa a[ṟupatu] periya taḷavāy mā•

l.note: The inscription is fragmentary and damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 675.
(A.R. No. 619 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south, east and north walls of the central shrine of Vēdanārāyaṇasvāmin
temple.
Kṛishṇarāya. 1529 A.D.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, Part II, No. 526.

inscription.head:
No. 676.
(A.R. No. 620 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the wall of the Vēdavallittāyār shrine in the Vēdanārāyaṇasvāmin temple.
Śrīraṅga I. 1579 A.D.

The details of the date, viz., Pramāthin, Chittirai 28 are insufficient for verification.
The cyclic year, however will correspond to 1579 A.D.

This inscription records that certain honours like paraḍai and parivaṭṭam had been
cancelled and instead, the pañchāḷattār will continue to enjoy their traditional rights
in return for cultivation and service in the temple. This arrangement is stated to have
been made by Śekaturaḍḍi Krishṉamanāyaka, the agent of Śrīraṅga, the tāṉattar
and the ūravar of Nāgalāpuram.

inscription.head:
No. 677.
(A.R. No. 621 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north, west and the south walls of the Vēdavallittāyār shrine in the Vēda-
nārāyaṇasvāmin temple.
Kṛishṇadēva-mahārāya. Ś. 1436: 1514 A.D.

This inscription in Śanskrit verse describes at the beginning the lineage and exploits
of the king. The details of date given, viz., Śaka 1436, Bhāva, Kārttika, utthāna-
dvādaśī, Friday, Rēvatī, correspond to 1514 A.D., October 30, Monday and not Friday,
·50; ·97.

It records the gift by the king, of the village called Dēvarāyakuppam named as
Kṛishṇarāyapuram in Nārāyaṇapurappaṟṟu, in Nirṇaiyūr (Niṉṟaiyūr)-nāḍu in
Kuṉṟavarddhanakōṭṭa in Chandragiri-rājya in Tuṇḍīra-maṇḍalam as sarvamānya
to god Kariyamāṇikka Perumāḷ in Arigaṇḍapuram. The document is stated to have
been composed by Mallaṇāchārya.

l.gr:
śubhamastu [||*] harērllīlā varāhasya daṭhaṣṭrādaṇḍassa pātu vaḥ | hēmādrikalaśā
yatra dhātrīśchatraśriyandadhau | [1*] kalyāṇāyāstu taddh[āma] pratyūhatimirāpaham |
yatgajōpyagajōtbhūtam hariṇāpi ca pūjyatē | [2*] asti
kṣīramayāddēvai rmmathyamānānmahāmbudhēḥ | navanītamivōt bhūta mapanīta
tamō mahaḥ | [3*] tasyāsīttanayastapōbhiratulaira

rtthanāmā budhaḥ
puṇyairasya purūravā bhujabalairāyu[r*]dviṣānnighnataḥ |
tasyāyurnnahuṣōsya tasya [pa]ruṣō yuddhē yayāti[ḥ*] kṣitau
khyātastasya tu turvvasu rvvasunibhaḥ śrīdē(da)vayānīpatēḥ [||4*||]
tadvaṅśē dēvakī jānirddidīpē timmabhūpatiḥ [|*] [ya]śasvī[tu]luvēndrēṣu yadōḥ
kṛṣṇa ivā

[5*] tatōbhūt bukkamājāni rīśvara kṣitipālakaḥ | atrā
samaguṇabhraṅśam maundiratnam mahībhujām | [6*] sarasādudabhū ttasmā nnarasā
vanipālakaḥ | dēvakī nandanāt kāmō dēvakīnandanādiva | [7*] kāvē[rī] māśu
ba[ddhvā] bahuḷa jalacarām yō vilaṅgh[yai]va śatrum jīvagrāha
grahītvā samiti bhujabalāntañca rājyam tadīyam [|*] kṛtvā śrīraṅga-

pūrvvantadapi nijavaśē pa[ṭṭa]ṇam yō babhāsē kīrttistambha nnighāya
tribhuvana bhavana stūyamānāpadānaḥ | [8*] cērañcōḷañca pāṇḍyantamapica
madhurāvallabham mānabhūṣam vīryyōdagra[ntu]ruṣkam gajapatinṛpatiñcāpi
jitvā tadanyān | āgaṅgātīra laṅkā prathama caramabhūbhṛt taṭāntam nitāntam
khyātaḥ kṣōṇīpatīnām srajamiva śirasām śāsanam yō vyatānīt | [9*]
vividhasukṛtagrāmē rāmēśvara pramukhē muhurmmudita hṛdaya[ḥ*] sthānē
sthānē vyadhatta yathāvidhi | budhaparivṛtō nānādānāni yō bhuvi ṣōḍaśa
tribhuvanakanōtgītam sphītam yaśaḥ punaruktaya[n] | [10*] tippājī
nāgalādēvyōḥ kausalyā śrīsumitrayōḥ | dēvyōriva nṛsiṅhēndrāttasmāt
paṅktirathādiva | [11*] vīrau vinayinau rāmalakṣmaṇāviva nandanau [|*]
jātau vīranṛsiṅhēndra kṛṣṇarāya mahīpa[tī] | [12*] vīra śrīnārasiṅhassa
vijayanagarē ratnasiṅhāsanasthaḥ kīrttyā nītyā nirasya nnṛganaḷa nahuṣā
napyavanyāmathānyān | āsētō rāsumērō-

ravanisuranuta[ḥ*] svairamācōdayādrē rāpāścātyācalāntādyakhila hṛdaya
māvarjya rājyam śaśāsa | [13*] nānādānānyakārṣīt kanakasadasi yā
śrīvirūpākṣa dēvasthānē śrīkāḷahastīśiturapi nagarē vēṅkaṭādrau ca
kāñcyām | śrīśailē śōṇaśailē mahati hariharēhōbaḷē saṅgamē ca
śrīraṅgē kumbhaghōṇē hananamasi mahā nanditīrthē nivṛttau | [14*]
gōkarṇē rāmasētau ja[gati] taditarēṣvapyaśēṣēṣu puṇyasthānēṣvārabdha
nānāvidhabahaḷa mahādānavāri pravāhaiḥ | yasyōdañcatturaṅga prakara

purarajaśśuṣyadambhōdhimagna kṣmābhṛt pakṣaścidōdyattarakuliśadharōtkaṇṭhitā
kuṇṭhitābhūt | [15*] brahmāṇḍam viśvacakram ghaṭamudita mahābhūtakam
ratnadh[ē]num saptāmbhōdhiñca kalpakṣitiruhalatikē kāñcanīm kā[ma*]
dhēnum | sarṇakṣmām yō hiraṇyāśvarathamapi tulāpūruṣam gōsa
hasram hēmā[śva]m hēmagarbham kanakakariratham pañcalāṅgalyatānīt | [16*]
prājyam praśāsya nirvighnam rājyam jyāmi[va] śāsitum | tasmin
guṇēna vikhyātē kṣitērindrē divam

gatē | [17*] tatōpyavārya vīryaśrī[ḥ*] kṛṣṇarāya mahīpatiḥ | bibharti
maṇikēyūranirviśēṣam [mahīm] bhujē | [18*] [kīrtyā ya]sya samantataḥ
prasṛtayā [vi]śvam•••• jē[di]tyāśaṅkya purā purārirabhavat
phālēkṣaṇaḥ prāyaśaḥ | patmākṣōpi caturbhujōjani catu[r]vaktrōbhavat
patmabhūḥ kābī khaṭgamadhādramāca kamalam vīṇāñca vāṇīkarē || [19*]
śatrūṇām vāsamētē tadata iti ruṣākinnu saptāmburāśi rnnānā
śēnā[tu]raṅgatruṭita vasumatī dhūbikāpābikābhiḥ | saṅśōṣya
svai[ramētat pra]tinidhijaladhiśrēṇikā yō vidhattē brahmāṇḍa svarṇamē
[ru pra]mukha nija mahādānatōyai ramēyaiḥ | [20*] madda[tt]āmartthi
sā[rthāḥ śrīya]miha [suciram] bhuṅjatā[mityavētya] prā[yaḥ] pratyūha
hētōḥ•••••• tattatdit j[ai]travṛttyāpica [biruda]padai
[raṅkitāsta]tra tatra [staṅbhān] jātapratiṣṭhān vyatanuta bhuvi yō[bhūbhṛda]
bhram-

kaṣāgrān [||21*] kāṭhacī śrīśaila śōṇācala kanakasabhā vēṅka(ṭa)ṭātri
pramukhyēṣvāvarttāvarttya sarvēṣvatanuta vidhivat bhūyasē śrēyasē yaḥ ||
dēva[sthā]nēṣu tīrtthēṣvapi[ca] kanakatulāpūruṣā[dī]ni nānādānānyē
vōpadānai rapi sammakhilai rā[ga]mōktāni tāni [||22*] rōṣakṛtaprati
pārtthivadaṇḍa śśēṣabhuja kṣitirakṣaṇa śauṇḍaḥ [|] bhāṣagē tappuvarāyara
gaṇḍastōṣa kṛdartthiṣu yō raṇacaṇḍaḥ | [23*] rājādhirāja ityuktō
yō rājaparamēśvaraḥ | mūvarāya[ra*] gaṇḍaśca pararājabhayaṅkaraḥ | [24*]
indurāyasuratrāṇō duṣṭaśārdūla mardanaḥ | gajaukha gaṇḍabhēruṇḍa
ityādi birudānvitaḥ [||25*] ālōkaya mahārāya jayajīvētivādibhiḥ | aṅgavaṅga
kabiṅgādyai rājabhissēvyatē [va]raḥ | [26*] satyaudāryassudhībhissa vijaya
nagarē ratnasiṅhāsa[na]sthaḥ kṣmāpālā[n] kṛṣṇarāya kṣitipatiradharīkṛtya
nītyā nṛgādīn | āpūrvvādrērathāstakṣitidharakaṭakādāca hēmācalāntādāsē
tōrabdhisārthaśriyamiha

[ba]habīkṛtya kīrttyā samindhē | [27*] śālīvāhana nistrītē śakābdē
sacatuśśataiḥ | ṣaṭ triṅśatāca saṅyuktaissaṅkhyātē daśabhiśśattaiḥ | [28*]
bhāvasaṅvatsarē māsi kārttikē bhṛguvāsarē | rēvatyāmapi puṇyā
yāmutthānadvādaśī tithau [||29*] tuṅgabhadrāpagātīrē viṭhṭhalēśvara sannidhau |
khyātasya kariyamāṇikkavērumābiti bhūtalē | [30*] arigaṇḍapurasthasya
sudhāhārasya śārṅ[gi]ṇaḥ | tuṇḍīramaṇḍalē khyātam candragi(ri)ryyākhya
rājyakam | [31*] ku[ṇḍa]varddhana kōṭastham nirṇaiyūr nāḍukē sthitam |
nārāyaṇapurappa

bhūṣaṇatva mupāśritam | [32*] prācīmāśāntirukkāri
karaigrāmasam[ā*]śritam | vē[dādri] kānanōddēśā ndakṣiṇasy[ā*]ndiśisthitam |
[33*] arigaṇḍapurābhikhyām grāma[va]ryyācca paścimam | karaṇīsaritaścāpi
diśa[mā]śritamuttarām | [34*] kṛṣṇarāyapurañcēti pratināma samāśritam |
sarvva[śa]syāciēta ndēvarāyakuppam manōramam | [35*] sarvvamānyam catussī
māsaṅyutañca samantataḥ | nidhinikṣēpapā[ṣā*]ṇa siddhasāddhyañjalā

bhūruham | vāpīkūpataṭākaiśca kaśchēnāpi samanvitam | [37*] dānasyā
dhamanasyāpi vikrayasyāpi cōcitam | parīta[ḥ*] prayatai[ḥ*] [sni]gdhapurōhita
purōgamaiḥ |•••• budhaiḥ śrautapatha[g*]ai radhigērgirā |

[38*] pātivratyaguṇā kīrttyārundhatī manurundhatī | pēru(mā)mānācca
yārindhē yēnaputravatī satī | [39*] arigaṇḍapurādhīśastiruvēṅkaṭanāyakaḥ*
yam prasūyava satyantaḥ [sa]njayattyēna putriṇam | [40*] sāhityas[ā*]ra |
sauhityabhājā bhōjā[nukāriṇā |] śrīmatpaddhamalaṇṇēna cōdi[ta*]stēna
dhīmatā | [41*] kṛṣṇadēva mahārāyō mānanīy[ō] manasvinām | sahiraṇya
payōdhārā pūrvakandattavān mudā | [42*] taditamavanīvanīpaka vinutasārasya
kṛṣṇarāyasya | śāsanamati[ba]la śāsanatarukaradānasya sāvadhānasya | [43*]
kṛṣṇadēva mahārāya śāsanēna sabhāpatiḥ | abhāṇin mudrasandarbham kathitam
tāmraśāsanam | [44*] kṛṣṇadēva mahārāyabhūmipālasya śāsanāt | malmaṇā
cārivaryyēṇa likhitantāmraśāsanam | [45*] ēkaivabhaginī lōkē sarvvēṣā
mēva bhūbhujām | na•bhōjyāna karagrāhyā-

dēvadattā vasundharā [||46*] dānapālanayō rmmaddhyē dānāt śrēyōnu
pālanam | dā[nāt sva]rgamavāpnōti pālanādacyutam padam | [47*]
svadattāt dviguṇam puṇyam paradattānupālanam | paratattāpahāra[ñca]svadattanniṣphalam
bhavēt | [48*] sāmānyōyam dharmmasēturnṛpāṇām kālē kālē
pālanīyō bhavatbhiḥ | sarvvānētān bhāvinaḥ pārtthivēndrān bhūyō
bhūyō yācatē rāmacandraḥ [||49*]

inscription.head:
No. 678.
(A.R. No. 622 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Vēdavallittāyār shrine in the
Vēdanārāyaṇasvāmin temple.
The inscription is very faintly engraved and illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 679.
(A.R. No. 623 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Vēdavallittāyār shrine, Vēda-
mārāyaṇasvāmin temple.
Kṛishṇadēva-mahārāya. Ś. 1442: 1521 A.D.

The details of date given in this inscription, viz., Ś. 1442, Vikrama, Makara ba. 8
Tuesday, Svāti, corresponds to 1521 A.D., January 1, Tuesday, ·42; f.d.n. ·21.

It records the assignment of the levies on houses in each town, at the rate of one
paṇam on first rate houses, three quarter paṇam on second rate houses, half paṇam on
third rate houses, quarter paṇam on fourth-rate houses and the customs levies at ports
like Anantarāyan Paṭṭaṇam alias Paḻavēṟkāḍu, for the erection of two maṇḍapas
in front of god Kariyamāṇikka Perumāḷ, and for the food and other offerings to the
god when he stays at these maṇḍapas on festival days by the nagarattār of various
maṇḍalas including those of Śōḻa-maṇḍalam, Paḍaivīḍu and Chandragiri, who had the
birudas Kāvērīvallabha, Pāṇḍiyaṉaich-cheṇḍāḍiveṉṟōr, Uttarāpati Vaiśyar, etc.
Aṉaittaḻaguṅ-kaṇḍa Haridāsar Deyvaṅgaḷ Perumāḷ Mudaliyār, son of Āvini Kaḷappāḷar
Tiruvēṅkaḍamuḍaiyār, the stānattār of Harivāsapuram, on the banks of Āraṇi in
Kuṉṟavattaṉakkōṭṭam, in Vēdagiri-dēśam in Jayaṅgoṇḍa-śōḻa-maṇḍalam alias Toṇḍai-
maṇḍalam, was authorised to collect the levies and arrange for the specified worship.

l.gr:
svasti [||*] śrīmanmahāmaṇḍalēśvara rājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa
kṛṣṇa-

l.ta: mpoṉāl mentavar muṉṟāṅkuṭittalaivar muṭikku māṇikka[m] kottavar iravile[yu]-

mātittar āṉai yiṭai [mi]tittor pāṇṭiyaṉaic ceṇṭāṭi veṉṟor [iṭṭa]p
pīṭattukku
ti ceṭṭiyār peṅkāṇṭai ceṭṭiyār paṭṭappiḷḷai ceṭṭiyār paḻaveṟkā-

mikaḷ [ula]ku ceṭṭiyār ṇārāyaṇa ceṭṭiyār tiruvattippiḷḷai
ceṭ

ṭiyār mutalāṉa cantiṟakiri rācciyam cūḻnta nakarattārum paṭaivīṭu cūḻnta nakarattārum
co-
ḻamaṇṭalattiluḷḷa nakarattārum palamaṇṭalattiṉum uṇṭāṉa nakarattārum upaiya

rutāka irumaṇṭapamum atile perumāḷ tiruṉāḷtoṟum eḻuntaruḷiyiruntu amutu
ceytaruḷattakkatākavum eṅkaḷukku

paṇṇi taraveṇumeṉṟu ṉāṅkaḷ palavūr cūḻnta nakarattārum tāṅkaḷai
vantu keṭkaiyiṟ tāṅkaḷ a-

paṇṇittarukaiyil yinta upayam cantirātitta varaiyum naṭantu
varumpaṭikku ṉāṅkaḷiṭṭut tanta ka-

ṭṭaḷai ūr toṟum periya vīṭṭukku oru paṇamum iraṇṭām vīṭṭukku mukkāṟ paṇamum
muṉṟām vīṭṭukku

araippaṇamum ṉālāntarattu viṭṭukkuk kāṟpaṇamum āka intappaṭiyile varuṣan
toṟum

naṭattittantu aṉantarāyaṉ paṭṭaṇamāṉa paḻaveṟkāṭu mutalāka karaituṟaikaḷile
t[o]ṅku kappalukku eṟṟuki

ṟa puṭavaik kaṭṭukkup paṇam oṉṟum puṟappa[ṭuñ carak]kukkup paṇam [k]ālum varuṣan
toṟum sa

yākat taṅkaḷukku vir[āṭa]māka naṭattit tarakkaṭaivomākavum i[nta virāṭa]ma tāṅkaḷe
taṇṭik koṇṭu varuṣan

toṟum perumāḷ kariyamāṇikkap perumāḷ tiruṉāḷ naṭakkuṟapot[ellām na]karat
tāruṭaiya maṇṭapattile eḻuntaruḷiyi-

runtu nakarattāruṭaiya[t]ākap perumāḷ eḻuntaruḷumpaṭiyum kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭu perumāḷ kariya
māṇikkap perumāḷ nakarattār

maṇṭapattile eḻuntaruḷukuṟattukku veṇṭiṉa maṇṭa[pa*]c coṭippu ṉaivettiyakkaṭṭaḷai
aṭaikkāyamutu cantaṉak kāpput tirumā-

lai tirupparivaṭṭam tā[ṉa]veccam paricārakaveccam koyiṟkottuveccam maṟṟu
muṇṭāṉa aṅkaraṅka vai

kara(t)ttārupaiyamāka naṭatta keṭaivarākavum ṉāṅkaḷiṭṭa kaṭṭaḷaippaṭi•••••••
tiruntālum ṉāṅkaḷe jāti[yā]-

kkuṭuttom aṉaittaḻakuṅkaṇṭa ha••••••••• ḷ perumā[ḷ]•
••••••• la maṇṭalañ cūḻnta

l.gr:
svadattāt dviguṇam puṇyam paradattānupa••••
••••• pahārēṇa sadattam niṣphalam bhavēt | dāna pālanayō rmmaddhēm

inscription.head:
No. 680.
(A.R. No. 624 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vēdanārā
yaṇasvāmin temple.
Kṛishṇadēva-mahārāya. Ś. 1442: 1520 A.D.

The details of date given in this inscription, viz., Śaka 1442, Vikrama, Kaṉṉi śu 5,
Sunday, Aniḻam, correspond to 1520 A.D., September 16, Sunday, f.d.t. ·24; f.d.n. ·21.

It records the gift of 1000 paṇam in gold and lands in different places for the
construction of a maṇḍapa in front of the Kariyamāṇikkapperumāḷ temple in Harivāsa-
puṇyakshētram and for the provision of food and other offerings to the god when he
comes and stays there on festival days, by the nāṭṭavar of Puḻaṟkōṭṭam, Puliyūr-
kōṭṭam, Īkkāṭṭu-kkōṭṭam, Maṇavūr-kōṭṭam, [Kuṉṟava]ttana-kōṭṭam, in Vēdagiri-
dēśam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam alias Toṇḍa-maṇḍalam. The gift was made over
to Deyvaṅgaḷ Perumāḷ Mudaliyār, son of Aṉaittaḻaguṅ-kaṇḍa Haridāsar Tiruvēṅgaḍa-
muḍaiyār, the tānattār of Harivāsapuram, on the banks of Āraṇi in Kuṉṟavattana-
kkōṭṭam.

l.ta:
ttu [pu]ḻaṟ koṭṭam puliyūr koṭṭam yik
kāṭṭuk ko[ṭṭa]m

[maṇavū]rk koṭṭam [kuṉṟava]ttana[k] koṭṭam paiyyūrk koṭṭam mutalāna ṉāṭṭavarom
ṉāṭṭupayamaṇṭapattukku

tirumaṇṭapamum anta maṇṭapattile nāṭṭupayamākapperumāḷ eḻuntaruḷiyiruntu amutu cey
taruḷa[t]takkatākavum oru suta-

paṇṇittara veṇumeṉṟu nāṅkaḷ nāṭṭavarāka vantu taṅkaḷaik keṭkaiyil tāṅkaḷ
taṅkaḷ kaiyyile

mum paṇṇit
yil inta upayam naṭa[tti]

varumpaṭikku ṉāṅkaḷ aṉaittuk koṭṭattu ṉāṭṭavarum vantu eṅkaḷeṅkaḷ koṭṭattiluṇṭāṉa
kiṟā-

mamellān terappaṭṭuk koṭṭantoṟum ṉāṭutoṟum paṟṟukkaḷ toṟum ṉāṅkaḷ viṭṭa ṉila
mā[ṉṉi]-

yattukku viparam puḻaṟ koṭṭam [e]ḻumurṉāṭu ākuṭiṉāṭu kāṉappeṟuṉāṭu veṇkalṉāṭu amapat
tūr ṉā-

puliyūrk koṭṭam kulottuṅka coḻavaḷaṉāṭu
kiṟāmaṉ ṉūṟṟaṟu-
īkkāṭṭuk koṭṭam teṉtuṇṭam kiṟāmam aṟupatukku kuḻi
illattūrṉāṭu māṅkaḷurṉāṭu poḷiyūrṉāṭu taṇikaiṉāṭu āka ṉāṭu ṉālukku

āka yinta nilaṅkaḷellām tāṅkaḷ
paramparaiyākac can-

tiṟātittiya varaiyum aṉupavittukkoṇṭu perumāḷ ṉāṭṭu maṇṭapattile tiruttotatiruṉāḷ
toṟum eḻuntaruḷiyiruntu

amutu ceytaruḷumpaṭikkuk kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭu ṉāṭṭu maṇṭapattile perumāḷeḻuntaruḷukuṟa kaṭṭaḷaikku
veṇṭiṉa maṇ-

ṭapac coṭippu ṉai(v)vettiyakkaṭṭaḷai aṭaikkāyamutu cantaṉakkāpput tirumālai tirupparivaṭṭam
koyiṟtāṉa veccam

paricārakaveccam koyiṟkottu veccam maṟṟumuṇṭāṉa aṅkaraṅkavaipokaṅkaḷellām
cantiṟātittiya varaiyum tā-
nta vūrār nilam vi[ṭ*]ṭo meṉṟālum inta marapile oru [a]paki[r]t[ti] vantāl
kiccuk koḷḷuvom appaṭiye ṉāṅkaḷ ṉāṭākak kūṭi

teyvaṅkaḷ perumāḷ mutali[yā]rukku aṉaittuk koṭṭattum aṉaittu ṉāṭṭiṉum uṇṭā
[ki]ya ṉāṭṭavarom

l.gr:
svadattāt [dvi]guṇam puṇyam paradattānupālanam | paradattāpahārēṇa
svadattam niṣphalam bhavēt | dānapālanayōrmmaddh[yē]

dānāchśrēyōnupālanam | dānāt svargamavāpnōti pālanādaccyutam padam |
phvadattām paradattām vāyōharēta vasundha-

inscription.head:
No. 681.
(A.R. No. 625 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the second gōpura of the Vēdanārayaṇasvāmin temple.
Kṛishṇadēva-mahārāya.
The earlier portion of this inscription is lost.

It records the gift of some village to god Kṛishṇa-Māṇikkadēva by Tirumalāmbā,
his queen (?). It seems to mention a village consisting of hamlets such as Amaṇpākkam,
Kōṭṭaḷattūr in Niṉṟayūr-nāḍu and Veṅkaḷattūr. It also mentions Pōrēṟṟu Nayinā[r].

inscription.head:
No. 682.
(A.R. No. 626 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
At the entrance into the second gōpura of the Vēdavalli-Tāyār shrine in the
Vēdanārāyaṇasvāmin temple in the same village.
Kṛishṇadēvarāya.
This is a copy of No. 675 above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. IX, Part II, No. 527.

inscription.head:
No. 683.
(A.R. No. 627 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the left and right sides of the second gōpura of the Vēdanārāyaṇasvāmin temple.
Kṛishṇarāya. Ś. 1445: 1523 A.D.

The details of date given in this inscription, viz., Śaka 1445, Subhānu, Kārttika
śu. 12, utthānadvādaśī, Monday, are irregular. If the week day is taken to be Thursday,
the details may correspond to 1523 A.D., November 19.

It records the gift of the village Harigaṇḍapuram, renamed Śrī Nāgalāpuram
consisting of the hamlets such as Kōṭṭaikāḍu, Vaṭaśrīmadurā, Kīṭpākkam, etc., to
god Nīlamāṇikkadēva for food offerings, dance, music and musical instruments, by
the brother (?) of Vaḍamallaṇṇan, the best among the ministers. The gift of Pōndap-
pākkam in Īkkāṭṭu-kkōṭṭam in Kachchiyūr-nāḍu for Sāmagāna is also mentioned at
the end.

inscription.head:
No. 684.
(A.R. No. 628 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the north gōpura of the Vēdanārāyaṇasvāmin temple.
Kṛishṇadēvarāya. Ś. 1445: 1524 A.D.

The inscription is dated Śaka 1445, Svabhānu, Makara, śu. 3, Friday, Śadaiyam
corresponding to 1524 A.D., January 8, ·94, f.d.n. ·16. This and the following two
inscription (Nos. 684-A and 685) bear the same date.

The king is stated to have passed through Nāgalāpuram on his way to Kumba-
kōṇam after the erection of a pillar of victory at Poṭṭaṉūr and in appreciation of the
services rendered by Deyvaṅgaḷ-Perumāḷ-mudaliyār to god Kariyamāṇikka-perumāḷ
at Arikaṇḍapuram, he is stated to have directed the local authorities to recognise
his services appropriately.

It registers the gift of tāṉa mōvai vattaṉai at Nāgalādēvi-ammaṉpuram and the
conferment of the title Kṛishṇarāya Haridāsa-mudaliyar on Deyvaṅgaḷ-Perumāḷ-muda-
liyār, son of Ādittar Āvin Kaḷappāḷar Tiruvēṅgaḍamuḍaiyār in view of the meritorious
services rendered to the temple of Kariyamāṇikka-perumāḷ.

l.note:
The following portion of the inscription is very indistinct and damaged. It seems to contain
the names of a few signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 684A.
(A.R. No. 628-A of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the north gōpura of the Vēdanārāyaṇasvāmin temple.
Kṛishṇadēvarāya. 1524 A.D.

This inscription bears the same date as in No. 684 above and registers the document
assigning lands for various items enumerated in the same inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 685.
(A.R. No. 629 of 1904.)
NĀGALĀPURAM, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the outer prākāra of the Vēdanārāyaṇasvāmin temple.
Kṛishṇarāyadēva. Ś. 1445: 1524 A.D.

The inscription is dated Śaka 1445, Chitrabhānu, Makara, śu. 3, Friday, Śadaiyam
corresponding to 1524 A.D., January 8, ·94, f.d.n. ·16. The cyclic year was Subhānu,
not Chitrabhānu (See Nos. 683 and 684 above).

It records the royal grant of Nāgalādēvīyammanpuram as tax-free tirunāmattuk-
kāṇi to Kariyamāṇikkatt-emberumāṉ for worship and offerings, in appreciation of the
services rendered to the temple by the local people including Aṉaittaḻagum kaṇḍa
Deyvaṅgaḷ Perumāḷ Mudaliyār.

inscription.head:
No. 686.
(A.R. No. 630 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 11 : 1188-89 A.D.

This records the gift of 32 cows, entrusted to the care of Tillaināyakakkōṉ-vaḍugaṉ,
a shepherd, by Valantaruvān Kōsalattaraiyaṉ of Paḻaiyāṟai in Āmba (Ambar ?)-nāḍu,
for burning a perpetual lamp with one uḻakku of ghee daily, by the measure called
tirukkārikaraiyuḍaiyāṉ, in the temple of Tirukkārikarai-ppiḷḷaiyār, in Niṉṟaya-nāḍu,
in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśoḻā-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
tu naṭuvilmalai ninṟaiya nāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār tiru[kkārikaraip] piḷḷaiyāṟku kulottuṅka
coḻamaṇṭalattu tiruna-

ṟaiyūrnāṭṭuk kiḻpaḻaiyāṟṟukkut talaimāṟu[viṭṭa] āmpanāṭṭup paḻaiyāṟai kaḻa•••••
koṭ[ṭa]muṭaiyān kocalattarai-

can valantaruvān kocalattaraiyanen tirununtāviḷakkonṟukkuc cāvāmuvāpperā
•••• pacu muppattiraṇṭum immaṇṭalat-

tu innāṭṭu iṭaiyan tillai nāyakakkon vaṭukanen ippacu muppattiraṇṭuṅ kaikkoṇṭu
tirukkārikaraiyuṭaiyānāl nāḷon-
ṟu ne[y*]yuḻakkāka aḷakka kaṭavenāka upaiyaṅ koṇṭen

inscription.head:
No. 687.
(A.R. No. 631 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.

This inscription, engraved in characters of the 12th century is damaged at the
beginning and hence the king's name and the regnal year are lost.

It records the gift of 64 cows, by Niṉṟaiyūr-nāṭṭāṉ of Naḍuviṉ-malai, for burning
two perpetual lamps in the temple. The measure called tirukkārikaraiyuḍaiyāṉ
is also mentioned.

l.ta:
vanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī••••• tevaṟku yāṇṭu•• vatu [jayaṅ
koṇṭacoḻama]ṇṭalattup paiyūr koṭṭattup paiyūrāna rāje

maṇṭalattu naṭuviṉmalai niṉṟaiyūr nāṭṭāṉ v[ai]••••• yā[rk]kum••••cantirātit
tavarai••••• [vai]tta tirunantāviḷakku iraṇṭu iraṇṭukku viṭṭa pacu a[ṟu]pattu
nālum ip••••• nāṭṭu••••• ttikkoṉ makaṉ kāṇi-

edition.l:
koṇṭu nāḷoṉṟukkut tirukkārikaraiyuṭaiyānā laḷakku••••yākatteṉ ittanamam
panmākeśvararakṣai ||—

inscription.head:
No. 688.
(A.R. No. 632 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅgachōḷa III. Year 15: 1192-93 A.D.

This incomplete inscription mentions at the beginning, a certain Turai-araiśaṉ
who bore the titles Vīra-rākshasa Kāñchīpura-paramēśvara and Vīra-rājēndra Vīra
Nuḷamba.

This records the gift of 49 paḻaṅkāśu, as poliyūṭṭu, for burning a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Tirukkārikarai-piḷḷaiyār.

l.gr:
svastiśrī [||*] samasta prasi[ddha]maṇḍalēśvara vī[ra*]rākṣasa śrīka ñcīpura paramēśvara
vīrarājēntra

l.ta:
lottuṅkacoḻa tevaṟ kiyā[ṇ*]ṭu patinañcāvatu tirukkārikaraippiḷḷaiyāṟku tirununtā
viḷakkoṉ[ṟu]kku poliyūṭṭāka kuṭutta paḻaṅkācu

inscription.head:
No. 689.
(A.R. No. 633 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 5: 1220-21 A.D.

This records the gift of 1000 kuḻi of land, in Karumbēḍu in Neḍu-nāḍu made
free of taxes like kaḍamai, āyam, pāḍikāval, paṭṭippoṉ, and tarappaḍi by Vīranāraśiṅga
dēva of Yādavarāya for conducting the mid-day service (uchchi sandi) to god Tiru-
kkārikarai-uḍaiyār, in Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājarājatevaṟku yāṇṭu añcāvatu vi-

l.ta: viṉmalai niṉṟaiyūr ṉāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār tirukkārikarai uṭaiyāṟkku ucci can[ti]kku
neṭuṉāṭṭu karumpeṭṭil malli etta pūṭṭaiyil kuḻi āyiramum kaṭamaiyum ā-
yamum pāṭikāvalum paṭṭippoṉṉum tarappaṭiyum viṭṭeṉ viraṉāraciṅkate-

inscription.head:
No. 690.
(A.R. No. 634 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Kampaṇa. 1375 A.D.

The details of date, viz., Ānanda, Kumbha••••Tiruvādirai, Sunday
corresponds to 1375 A.D., February 10, Sunday, f.d.n. ·17.

This damaged inscription seems to record the grant of certain rights of irrigation,
by the inhabitants of Niṉṟai to the mahājanas of Payāṟṟūr. The temple accountant
Panmāhēśvara-vēḷāṉ alias Vīṟṟirunda perumāḷ of Pulvēḷūr figures as one of the signa-
tories.

inscription.head:
No. 691.
(A.R. No. 635 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Rājādhirāja II.

On account of the title Tribhuvanachakravarttigaḷ and the characters of the
inscription, the king of the record may be identified with Rājādhirāja II.

This inscription records the gift of 3 lamps (i.e. 2 twilight lamps to the deity
Tirukkārikarai-uḍaiyār and one twilight lamp to Piḷḷaiyār, (i.e., TirujñAṉa-sambandar),The distinction made here between the deity Tirukkārikarai-Uḍaiyār and the deity Piḷḷaiyar
(Āḻuḍaiya-piḷḷaiyār), the śaiva saint who had visited and honoured this holy place Tirukkārikarai
by a hymn in the Tēvāram, suggests that RAmagiri is identical with Tirukkārikarai and the reference
to Piḷḷaiyār in this inscription is to that saint.
by Śīyaṉaḍiyan Kaliśattakaṉ Ādittadēvan Nandipaṉmaṉ. KAri Piḷḷai, Āṇḍāṉ and
Āṭkoṇḍa-Piḷḷai, were to maintain one lamp each.

va[rai]

inscription.head:
No. 692.
(A.R. No. 636 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Year 8.

This inscription commences with the praśasti samasta-sakala-bhuvana, etc., of
Tirukkāḷattidēva Yādavarāya who is a well known feudatory of the Chōḷa king
Rājarāja III. Hence the year 8 cited in the body of the record is probably the regnal
year of the Chōḷa king.

This seems to record the gift of the villages Tirukkāḷattidēvanallūr and Muṉai-
mēḍu alias Tirukkāḷattidēva-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Chēḷa-nāḍu as a tax-free kāṇi,
for the merit of a certain Svāmidēvar whose name is lost and who is stated to have
belonged to the santāna of Lakshādhyāya-mudaliyār and who is also said to have
been the preceptor to the Chōḷa and Pāṇḍya kings.

inscription.head:
No. 693.
(A.R. No. 637 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.

The details of date i.e., Śaka 1338, Naḷa, Arpaśi, 25 are irregular. If the Śaka
year quoted is a mistake for Ś. 1358, the date would correspond to 1436 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of two door-ways in the shrine of Tiruvālīśvaram-
uḍaiya nāyanār and the setting up of an image of Tiruppaḷḷi-nāchchiyār for the
god, by Śakkaraśar, son of Ayyaluppi Kaḍaiyār Vide No. 725 below.

edition.l: svasti [||] sakāttam 1338

12 •••••to

inscription.head:
No. 694.
(A.R. No. 638 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine of the Vālīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 10: 1225-26 A.D.
On palaeographical grounds the inscription may be assigned to the 13th century.

This inscription records the gift of 32 cows left in the hands of the śivabrāhmaṇas
of the temple, by Niṉṟaikuḷattūḻāṉ Tiruvaṉpeṟṟāṉ alias Niṉṟaiyūrnāṭṭu-vēḷāṉ, for
burning a perpetual lamp to the deity Tirukkārikarai-uḍaiyār.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ ciri irācarācatevaṟku yāṇṭu pattāvatu ceyaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattu kuṉṟavattaṉak koṭṭattu niṉṟaiyūr nāṭṭil āḷuṭaiyār tirukkārikarai
uṭaiya(ṉā)[ārkku] niṉṟaikuḷattūḻāṉ tiruvaṉ peṟṟāṉ niṉṟaiyūr nāṭ-

ikkoyilil civappirāmaṇar āṇṭāṉ paṭṭaṉum aṉpaṉāyaka pa[ṭ*]ṭaṉum āḻkoṉṭap
piḷḷaiyum ivaṉ tampi kāripiḷḷaiyum ippacu muppattira-

[ti]ttavarai erikka kaṭavom ākac cammatittu kaikkoṇṭom ivvaṉaivom [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 695.
(A.R. No. 639 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Kampaṇa Uḍaiyar (II): 1365-66 A.D.

The details of date, i.e., Viśvāvasu, Mithuna 10, Monday and Rōhiṇi are irregular.
The cyclic year Viśvāvasu falls in Ś. 1287 (1365-66 A.D.).

This inscription records the gift of land as sarvamānya for lamp to the deities
Tiruvālīśvaramuḍaiya nayiṉār and Bhairava Nayiṉār in Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kuṉṟa-
vattanakōṭṭam, a sub-division of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

Below the inscription is another fragment in characters of about the 12th century.
It cites the 6th regnal year of a certain king (name lost) and seems to record a gift
given by the Nāṭṭavar of Niṉṟaiyūrnāḍu.

edition.l:
svasti [||*] śrīmaṉmakāmaṇṭalicura irāyairāya irāya paramecura virapi[ra*]tāpa [ku]m[āra]kampaṇa
uṭaiyarkku cellāniṉṟa vicuvāvacu varuṣattu mituṉaṉāyaṟ[ṟu] pūrva pakṣattu daśamiyum
tiṅkaḷkiḻamaiyum rohiṇiyum peṟṟa ṉāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa [coḻamaṇṭala*]

ttuk kuṉṟavattaṉak koṭṭattu niṉṟaiyaṉāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālicuramuṭaiya nayiṉā[r*]kkum
