ittaṉmam canti
vomākavum ippaṭikku niṉṟaiyūr nāṭṭu nāṭavarom paṇiyāl cittirameḻiveḷāṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 696.
(A.R. No. 640 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara
temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 32: 1209-10 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of the village Nīrvāyil, made free of levies (āyam)
in cash, gold and paddy by Śaśikulachaḷukki Tani-niṉṟu-veṉṟa Vīranārasiṅga alias
Yādavarāya for taking out the deity Tirukkārikarai-uḍaiyār in procession during
Chittirai festival.

Vīra Nārasiṅgadēva alias Yādavarāya, a well-known feudatory of Kulōttuṅga III
figures as the donor in this inscription and hence the king may be identified with
Kulōttuṅga III. The donor is mentioned with a string of birudas such as Karavāḷa-
Bhairava, Śrī-Kāḷahastīśa-pādārādhaka, Kanuppārapuravarādhīśvara, etc.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī[kulottuṅka]coḻatevarkku yāṇṭu [32]
vatu samadhigata pañcamahāśabda mahāmaṇḍalēśvara vēṅgivallabha gōdāviridīra
kanuppārapuravarādīśva[ra*] karavāḷa bhairava śrīmat śrīkāḷahastiśa pādārādhaka
parabalasādhaka prāpta samasta vidyāvicārabhūṣaṇa saptarājāśraya śaśikula
cā[ḷu*]ki taniniṉṟu veṉṟa viranāraciṅka-

l.ta:
nāna yātavarāyanen jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭa[lattu] [naṭuviṉ]malai niṉṟaiyūr [nāṭṭu uṭai]
yār] tirukkārikaraiyuṭaiyārkku eṉ perāl cittirait tirunāḷ eḻuntaruḷivikka itukku
tevatānamāka nirvāyil kācāyam poṉṉāyam nellāyam uṭpaṭa viṭṭeṉ vira nāraciṅka
tevanen itai māṟṟuvān keṅ[kaiyi*]ṭai kurāṟ pacuvai koṉṟa

inscription.head:
No. 697.
(A.R. No. 640-A of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara
temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 32: 1209-10 A.D.

This records the gift of the villages Vēlūr, Śāttamaṅgalam and Śūralūr
ttaḍai, made free of levies by Vīranāraśiṅgadēva alias Yādavarāya for pūjā and food
offerings to the deity Tirukkārikaraippiḷḷaiyār.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 32vatu tirukkārikaraiyuṭaiya piḷ[ḷai*]
yāṟku pūcaikkum amutupaṭikkum velūrum cāttamaṅkalamum cūralūrttaṭaiyum aḷa
••••• nellāyam uṭpaṭa sarvamāniyamāka viṭṭeṉ yātavarāyaṉāṉa viranāra
ciṅkatevanen ittai māṟṟuvarn keṅkai karaiyiṟ kurāṟ pacuvai koṉṟāṉ
brahmattippaṭu[vār*] [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 698.
(A.R. No. 641 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Kampaṉa Uḍaiyar II. 1370 A.D.

The cyclic year Sādhāraṇa fell in Śaka 1292. This records the gift of the pillar
by Vīśaṇar, the younger brother of Piḷḷappar.

inscription.head:
No. 699.
(A.R. No. 642 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the wall of the prākāra of the Vālīśvara temple.
Bukka II. 1382 A.D.

The details of date, i.e., Dundubhi, Chittirai 5, Wednesday, Chittirai are irregular.
On grounds of palaeography the inscription may be assigned to the latter half of the 14th
century and hence the cyclic year and month, viz., Dundubhi, Chittirai will correspond
to 1382 A.D., January, falling in the reign of Bukka II.

This records the gift of land in Kāva[n]da Kaṇḍanallūr, with rights of irrigation
from the lake at Veṇkuḷattūr in Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kuṉṟavattanak-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅ-
goṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, by the inhabitants (ūravar) of Veṇkuḷattūr to Mudaliyār
Satyadarśanigaḷ of the Tiruvāḷaṉ-maṭha at Tirukkārikarai.

l.ta: n rāyavipāṭan pukkarāya uṭaiyarkku cellā niṉṟa [tu]ntu-
jaya[ṅ*]koṇṭa coḻa maṇṭalattu kuṉṟavattanakkoṭṭattu niṉṟaiyūr nāṭṭu āḷuṭaiya
nam paṇṇina paricāvatu eṅkaḷ kāṇi āṭci
āṉa kāva[n]ta kaṇṭanallūr ṉattamum eriyum naṉcey puṉce[y*] nilamum veṇkuḷat-
[tū]r eri nirum vāykkālum maṟṟum ivvūrkku uḷḷa

inscription.head:
No. 700.
(A.R. No. 643 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Harihara II. 1400 A.D.

The details of date, viz., Pramāthin, Makara, ba. 10, Śōdi(svāti), Sunday are irregular.
Palaeographically the inscription may be assigned to the latter half of the 14th century
and hence the cyclic year and the month viz., Pramāthin, Makara will correspond to
1400 A.D., January, falling in the reign of Harihara II.

This inscription records the gift of Vēlūr as dēvadāna to the temple of Tirukkārikarai-
uḍaiya nāyaṉār in Naḍuvu-malai in Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu, a district of Kuṉṟavattanak-
kōṭṭam, a sub-division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam by the cultivators (ēr-vallavar)
of Vēlūr.

inscription.head:
No. 701.
(A.R. No. 644 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 6: 1184 A.D.

The title Tribhuvanachakravarttigaḷ and the palaeography of the record point
to the identity of the king with Kulōttuṅga III who also bore the title Vīra-Rājēndra.
The details of date viz., Kaṟkaṭaka ba. 12, Rōhiṇi, Thursday, point to 1184 A.D. July
5, Thursday, f.d.t. ·68, f.d.n. ·95.

This inscription records the gift of 96 sheep by Kuvaḷaiyam Aḻagiyān Oṟṟi-Aṟaiśaṉ
of Paiyūr (See No. 708 below) for burning a perpetual lamp to the deity Tirukkāri-
karaippiḷḷaiyār, of Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu, in Kuṉṟavattana-kōṭṭam, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī [vi]rarāceṉtara(ra) coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu
āṟāvatu kaṟkaṭaka nāyaṟṟu apara pa(k)kṣat[tu] tuvā

ciyum viyāḻakkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa urocaṇi
nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu kuṉṟavattaṉak koṭṭattu naṭuvil malai niṉṟaiyūrnāṭṭu
āḷuṭaiyār tirukkārikarai piḷḷaiyārkku im maṇṭalattu puḻaṟ koṭṭattu kulottuṅka
coḻa•••••

paiyyūr nāṭāḻvāṉ [ku]vaḷaiyam aḻakiyān oṟṟi araicaṉen vaitta tirunuṉtāviḷakku
oṉṟukku viṭṭa āṭu toṇṇūṟṟu āṟum maṉṟāṭikaḷil teluṅkarāyak koṉ kaṇṇāṭi
appaṉum cāttak koṉ makaṉ koṇṭikkoṉum pūmakkoṉ makaṉ vaṭukakkoṉum pot
takkoṉ malaiyaṉum puṭolaṉum caṉtirātittavarai āṇṭu oṉṟukku toṇṇūṟṟu aṟu
nāḻiney

inscription.head:
No. 702.
(A.R. No. 645 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 26: 1241-42 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 2 māḍai, left in the hands of the śivabrāhmaṇas
of the temple by Kāvaṉ Bōyiṇḍaṉ of Śōmiyūr in Pākkai-nāḍu for a twilight lamp to
the deity Tirukkārikarai Piḷḷaiyār, in Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kuṉṟavattana-kōṭṭam,
a subdivision of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] tiribhuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrāja[rā]-

l.ta: yār tirukkārikarai piḷḷaiyārkku pākkai nāṭṭu comiyūr••
llap poiṇṭar makaṉ kāvan poyiṇṭaneṉ ippiḷ••
ṟku vaitta caṉtiviḷakku oṉṟukku iṭṭa māṭai i[m*]māṭai iraṇṭum ik-
āḷkoṇṭapi[ḷ*]ḷaiyum yivaṉ tampi nāyakam pi[ḷ*]ḷaiyum kā
ku oṉṟum eri[k*]ka kaṭavom i[v*]vaṉaivom śrīmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 703.
(A.R. No. 646 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 16: 1231 A.D.

The details of date viz., Karkaṭaka 14, śu. 10, Aṉiḻam, Thursday, correspond regu-
larly to 1231 A.D., July 10, Thursday, ·65, ·97.

This inscription records the gift of 32 cows, left in the hands of the śivabrāhmaṇas
of the temple by Kuḷattuḻāṉ Ammai Appaṉ alias Villavarāyaṉ of Kuṉṟai, for burning
a perpetual lamp to the deity Tirukkārikarai-uḍaiyār of Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kuṉṟa-
vattana-kōṭṭam, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccak[ka]ravattikaḷ śrīirājairājatevarkku yāṇṭu patiṉāṟāvatu
kaṟkaṭaka nāyaṟṟu patiṉālānti-

l.ta:
miyum aṉiḻamumāṉa vaṉṟu jayaṅkoṇṭa
coḻa maṇṭa-

lattuk kuṉṟavattaṉak koṭṭattu niṉṟaiyūr nāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār tirukkārikkarai uṭai
yārkku

kuṉṟaiyiṟ kuḷattuḻāṉ ammai appaṉāṉa villavarāyaṉeṉ iṉṉāyaṉāṟku viṭṭa
tirinun-

tāviḷakku oṉṟukku viṭṭa pacu muppattiraṇṭum yikkoyilil kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāma
ṇaṉ āṇṭāṉ paṭ-

ṭaṉum aṉpaṉāyakapaṭṭaṉum āṭkoṇṭa piḷḷaiyum [mi]vaṉ tampi aṉpaṉāyakanum pūcāla
paṭṭaṉum

ivaṉ tampi••••••• yum āḻvāṉ paṭṭaṉ tiruvā[ṇ]ṭaiyum ippacu muppatti
raṇṭum upaiya-
mākak kaikkoṇṭu ivviḷakku oṉṟu caṉtirātittavarai erikka kaṭavomākak kaikko-

inscription.head:
No. 704.
(A.R. No. 647 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Bhairava shrine, in the Vālīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 9: 1224-25 A.D.

This inscription records gift of money (paḻaṅkāśu) [details lost] by Rājanārāyaṇa-
vēḷāṉ alias Tirūvēkambamuḍaiyāṉ of Kudir in Ōriyūr in Veṇkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam, left
in the hands of the śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple, for burning a twilight lamp to God
Tirukkārikarai-ppiḷḷaiyār.

l.ta:
[ya]ṇa veḷāṉṉāṉa tiruvekampam uṭaiyāṉ tirukkārikaraip piḷḷaiyāṟku vaicca canti
viḷakku oṉṟum ikkoyil

pirāmaṇaril [n]āṟpatteṇṇāyira paṭṭaṉum pūcālapaṭṭaṉ kāripiḷḷaiyum āṇṭāṉ
pa[ṭṭa]ṉum aṉpanāyakapaṭṭaṉ araicappiḷḷaiyum āṭkoṇṭāṉ

••••••• paḻaṅkācu kaikkoṇṭu caṉtirātittavarai erikka kaṭavom
ivvaṉaivomum [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 705.
(A.R. No. 648 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Yādavarāya. Year 36.

This inscription records the gift of land as tirunāmattukkāṇi-ubaiyam, made
free of the taxes poṉvari, kaḍamai, antarāyam, etc., by the inhabitants (ūravar) of
Arikaṇḍapuram for services during the festivals in the month of Vaigāśi, in the temple
of Tirukkārikarai-uḍaiyār.

Among the boundaries of the gift land, the record mentions the hamlet Kāḷaya-
kaṇḍachchēri.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] yātavarāyaṟku yāṇṭu 36 vatu tirukkārikarai uṭaiyār[k*]ku vaikācit
tirunāḷukku a[ri]kaṇṭapurattūrava-

l.ta:
rom ittirunāḷ aḻivukku pūvuṭaiyāṉ kuḻipaḷḷattukku meṟku kacciyekampar
tiruttu uṭpaṭa kāḷayakaṇṭacceri ellaikku

vaṭakkum vaṭapārkellai māvukku vaṭakku paḷḷippaṭṭi māvil ellai u[ṟa]vum i[v*]
vellai uṭpaṭṭa nilam kuṭi ṉiṅkiṉa nilam tiruṉāmattu-

kkāṇi upai[ya*]māka poṉvari kaṭamai pala antarāya maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭa upātikaḷu
[m*] iṟai kaḻittu kuṭinīṅkiṉa nilamāka viṭṭom
arikaṇṭapurattu ūravarom itukku ilaṅkaṇam coṉ[ṉa*]vaṉ keṅkai

karaiyil kurā[ṟ*]
ppacuvai koṉ[ṟa] piramatti paṭuvār [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 706.
(A.R. No. 649 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Alluntikkaraiśaṉ alias Gaṇḍagōpāla. Year 4: 1255 A.D.

The details of date, viz., Makara 25, ba. 8, Sunday, Viśākha corresponds to 1255
A.D. January 3, Sunday, f.d.n. ·59.

This incomplete inscription stops after mentioning Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu, a district in
Kuṉṟavattana-kōṭṭam, a subdivision of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l: [sva]sti[śrī] [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkarava-

inscription.head:
No. 707.
(A.R. No. 650 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 26: 1203-04 A.D.

This inscription seems to record a gift of money, the purpose of which is not clear.
Mentions Villavarāyaṉ, a vēḷāṉ of Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu, Vikramaśōḻa-villavarāyan and his
wife Piḻaiyilāvāchakar Veñjolañji Paḻiyañji of Paḻaiyanūr.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅka[co*]ḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu
irupattāṟāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu ninṟaiyū[r*] nāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār tiruk
kārikaraip-

l.ta: piḷḷaiyāṟkuk kuḷattuḻā[ṉ*] niṉṟaiyū[r*] nāṭṭu veḷān navikkan cu

ranāṉa villavarāyan
tiru(k)kkāḷatti uṭaiyān peṟṟānāṉa ap[pa]ñci vikkiramacoḻa villavarāyaṉ akamuṭai
yāḷ paḻaiyā-

nūṟ piḻai[yi]lāvācakar veñcolañci paḻiyañci kacci kaṭampaṉ ta[ḻuva*]kkuḻaiñ
cāṉ uyyavantāṉāna kulottuṅkacoḻac civapāta
l.note:
In these places some blank space is left due to the inadvertance; the engraver or for
later engraving.
l.ta:
••••• pi[ri*]yaṉ viḻup
paraiyaṉ•••••• caṭṭi oṉpatu māṟi

l.note:
In these places some blank space is left due to the inadvertance; the engraver or for
later engraving.

inscription.head:
No. 708.
(A.R. No. 651 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north and west walls of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 6: 1184 A.D.

The title Tribhuvanachakravarttigaḷ and the palaeography of the record point
to the identity of the king with Kulōttuṅga III, who also bore the title Vīra-Rājēndra.
The details of date, viz., Kaṟkaṭaka ba. 12, Rōhiṇi, Thursday point to 1184 A.D.,
July 5, Thursday, f.d.t. ·68, f.d.n. ·95. See No. 701 above which is dated on the
same day and which records another gift by the donor of this record.

This inscription records the gift of 96 sheep by Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Paiyyūr-
nāḍāḻvāṉ alias [Kuvalai]yamaḻagiyāṉ Oṟṟiyaraiśaṉ (See No. 701 above) for sacrificing
one sheep every Sunday in the temple of Tirukkārikarai-ppiḷḷaiyār, in Naḍuvil-malai,
in Niṉṟiyūr-nāḍu.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvirarācentiracoḻa••••••• ṭu āṟāvatu
kaṟkaṭaka nāyaṟṟu apara pakṣattu [tu]vādaśiyum viyāḻakkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa urocaṇi
nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu••••• koṭṭattu naṭuvilmalai niṉṟaiyūr
nāṭṭu āḷḷuṭaiyār tirukkārikaraip piḷḷaiyārkku immaṇṭalattu puḻak••••
lottuṅ[ka*]c[o]ḻa paiyyūr nāṭāḻvāṉ āṉa [ku]va[lai]yamaḻakiyāṉ oṟṟiyaraicaṉen
cantirātittavarai tirunāyaṟṟukkiḻamai toṟum oru āṭu veṭṭak kaṭavatāka viṭṭa āṭu
toṇṇūṟṟu āṟu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 709.
(A.R. No. 652 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga I.

This is a fragmentary inscription beginning with portions of the praśasti “Pugaḻ-
māduviḷaṅga”, etc., of the king.

l.ta:
••••• viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malar makaḷ puṇara urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta
maṇi muṭi cūṭi minavar ni[lai*]keṭa villava[r]
••••••••• lūṟṟiḻitarat tikkaṉaittun taṉ cakkara naṭātti vijaiya a

inscription.head:
No. 710.
(A.R. No. 653 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 27: 1242-43 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 11 māḍai by Koṭṭū[r] Vaḍukaṉ for burning a
twilight lamp to the deity Tirukkārikarai-piḷḷaiyār of Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu, a district of
Kuṉṟavattana-kkōṭṭam, a sub-division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l: [sva]stiśrī [||*] tiripuvanaccakka[ra*]va-

inscription.head:
No. 711.
(A.R. No. 654 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Vīranārasiṅga-Yādavarāya. Year 36.

The details of the date given, viz., Makara and Rōhiṇi are insufficient for verifica-
tion.

This inscription records the sale of the right of worship and other services, for 20
days in the temple of Tirukkārikarai-uḍaiyār by certain śivabrāhmaṇas of Nāṛpattiraṇḍu
vaṭṭam of the temple to the śivabrāhmaṇa Agavalan Śūriya Bhaṭṭaṉ.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tirupuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīviranāraci[ṅ]ka tevar yātavarāyaṟkku yāṇṭu
36 vatu

l.ta: makara nāyiṟu rocaṇināḷ tirukkārikarai uṭaiya nāyinār koyil civappirāmaṇar
innāḷ pattukku paṉmāyecura paṭṭaṉ viṟṟa ṉāḷ

l.note:
This word is engraved in a different hand and appears to have been inserted to justify the
discrepancy in the total.

inscription.head:
No. 712.
(A.R. No. 655 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 25: 1240-41 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 3 māḍai of gold left in the hands of the śiva-
brāhmaṇas of the temple, by Kārippiḷḷai, son of Śeṅgamiṭṭi Kuḍiyār of Paṇḍāri-
Śeṟuvaṉūr, in Mēlaippaḍaiyūr-nāḍu, for burning a twilight lamp in the temple of
Tirukkārikaraippiḷḷaiyār in Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu, in Naḍuvu-malai in Kuṉṟavattana-
kkōṭṭam, a sub-division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājarājate

l.ta: ṭaiyūr nāṭṭil paṇṭāri ceṟuvaṉūril ceṅkamiṭṭi kuṭiyā-
r makaṉ kārip piḷḷai ivaṉ ippiḷḷaiyārkkuc caṉti vi-
ṉum āḷkkoṇṭa piḷḷaiyum ivaṉ tampi nāyakappiḷḷaiyum
pūcāri paṭṭaṉṉum ivaṉ tampi kārip piḷḷaiyum tiruvāṇṭaiyum

inscription.head:
No. 713.
(A.R. No. 656 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 29: 1245 A.D.

The details of date viz., Mithuna 32, śu. 1, Monday, Pūśam regularly corresponds
to 1245 A.D., June 26, Monday, ·70; f.d.n. ·05.

This inscription records the gift of 16 māḍai by Vaḍugappiḷḷai alias Vayirādarāyaṉ
of Koṭṭaḷattūr, for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirukkārikarai-piḷḷ-
aiyār. The gift was entrusted to the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple who under
took to maintain the lamp. (See No. 712 above.)

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīirājarāja

l.ta:
kārikaraip piḷḷaiyāṟku koṭṭaḷatnūr koṭṭaḷattūr kiḻavaṉ vaṭukappi[ḷ*]ḷaiyāṉa vayi
rātar[āya]neṉ innāyaṉāṟku viṭṭa tirunantāviḷakku oṉṟukkum i-
ṭṭa māṭai patiṉāṟu immāṭai patiṉāṟum ik koyiṟ kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmaṇar kā
ttirattu nāṟpatteṇṇāyira paṭṭaṉ ammai appaṉum pūcāli paṭṭaṉ koṇṭaṉum

ttirattu taḻuvakkuḻaintāṉ pa[ṭ*]ṭaṉu[m*] koṇṭap piḷḷaiyum ivvaṉaivom im
māṭai patiṉāṟum kaikkoṇṭu upaiyamāka caṉtirātittavarai erikka kaṭavom ivva
[ṉai]vom [|*] śrīmāhecurarakṣai [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 714.
(A.R. No. 657 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Year 2.

This inscription records an order of Madhurāntaka Pottappi Chōḷa regarding the
remission of portion of the tax realised in kind from the different classes of land viz
tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam, paḷḷichandam, agarapaṟṟu, vanniyappaṟṟu, jīvitappaṟṟu and paḍaipaṟṟu
from the month of Aippaśi in the 2nd year.

inscription.head:
No. 715.
(A.R. No. 658 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 10: 1225-26 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 32 cows, left in the hands of the śivabrāh-
maṇas (names specified) of the temple by Niṉṟai-kuḷattuḻāṉ Tiruvaṉ Peṟṟāṉ alias
Niṉṟaiyūr-nāṭṭu-vēḷāṉ for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirukkārikarai-piḷ-
ḷaiyār situated in Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu, a sub-division of Kuṉṟavattaṉa-kōṭṭam, a division
of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ ciriirājairājatevaṟku yāṇṭu pattāvatu ceyaṅ
koṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu kuṉṟavattaṉak koṭṭattu niṉṟaiyūr nāṭṭu nāyanār
tirukkārikarai piḷḷaiyāṟkku niṉṟaik kuḷattuḻāṉ tiruvaṉ peṟṟāṉ niṉṟaiyūr nāṭṭu
veḷāṉ ippi[ḷ*]ḷai[y*]ā-

r[k*]ku vaitta tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟukku viṭṭa pacu muppattiraṇṭu ikkoyiṟ civap
pirāmaṇaṉ āṇṭāṉ paṭṭaṉum aṉ[pa*]nāyakapaṭṭaṉum āṭkoṇṭa piḷḷaiyum ivaṉ
tampi nāyakapiḷḷaiyum pūcāripaṭṭaṉum ivan tam[pi] kārippiḷḷaiyum ippacu muppat
tiraṇṭu tirununtāviḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 716.
(A.R. No. 659 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Bhairava shrine in the Vālīśvara
temple.
Vīragaṇḍagōpāla. Year 3: 1294-95 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 16 buffaloes left under the charge of the Śiva
brāhmaṇas of the temple, by Nakula[n] Viḻuppādarāyan, a mudali of Vīranāra
siṅgadēva-yādavarāya who bears the birudas Rājāśraya and Śaśikula-chāḷukki, for
burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirukkārikarai-piḷḷaiyār, at Niṉṟaiyūr-nāḍu
in Kuṉṟavattaṉa-kōṭṭam, a division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvirakaṇṭakopālatevaṟku yāṇṭu 3vatu

l.ta:
cāḷukki viranāraciṅkatevanāṉa yātavarāyar mutali nakulan
viḻuppāta

niṉ[ṟai*]yūr nāṭṭu āḷuṭaiy[ā*]r tirukkārikaraip piḷḷaiyārkku vaitta tirunun
nuntāviḷa

inscription.head:
No. 717.
(A.R. No. 660 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the back of the stone bull through the mouth of which water flows into
the tank in the Vālīśvara temple.

This inscription in characters of about the 9-10th century A.D., records the
setting up of the bull by Peruṅtachchaṉ, a carpenter of Kūvam, and the son of
Chāmuṇḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 718.
(A.R. No. 661 of 1904.)
RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the ruined gōpura in the Vālīśvara temple.
Virūpāksha III. 1480 A.D.

In the details of date given viz. Śaka 1 ·17, Śārvari, ba. 7, Monday, the Śaka year
does not agree with the cyclic year. The mention of the king Virūpāksha as the son
of Gajavēṭṭai-Pratāpa-Dēvarāya and the mention of Narasiṅgarājauḍaiyār who may be
identified with the Sāḷuva chief of that name, enables us to identify the king with Virū-
pāksha III (1465-1485 A.D.) Hence the cyclic year Śārvari may be equated with 1480
A.D.

This damaged inscription seems to record a gift for lamp and flower offerings to
Vayirava-nayiṉār and other deities in the temple of Tirukkārikarai-uḍaiyār, for the
merit of Narasiṅgarāja-udaiyār and Obaḷayarāja.

l.ta: ••••• mamāka vilaikiṟeyam tirukkārikarai tevarkku nam cirmai ellā••••

edition.l: ka kaṭavatākavum ||— The inscription is very badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 719.
(A.R. No. 662 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Year 35.

Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa figuring in this inscription is the well-known
feudatory of Rājarāja III and the year 35 cited may perhaps be taken as an year of
Rājarājā's reign, i.e., 1251-52 A.D.

This inscription registers the order of Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻaṉ granting
the village Tirumullaivāyal free from the payment of taxes such as poṉvari, nāṭṭuvari,
kāṇikkai, uḷḷūr-puṟa-kkalaṉai, etc., for conducting a festival and a service called after
Vijaya Gaṉḍagōpālaṉ and for pūja and other food-offerings to the deity Tirumullaivā-
yal-uḍaiya Nāyaṉār n Kāṉappēṟūr-nāḍu, in Puḻal-kōṭṭam, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇ-
ḍalam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] maturāntakap pottappic coḻa[ṉ] olai 35 ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu
puḻal koṭṭattu

l.ta:
kāṉappeṟu[r] nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyaluṭai[ya*] nāyaṉārkku vicaiyakaṇṭakopālaṉ
cittirait ti

runāḷ eḻuntaruḷavum vicaiyakaṇṭakopālaṉ canti amutuceyyavum pūcaikkum amutupaṭikkum
tiru

mullaivāyal poṉvari kaṭamai nāṭṭuvari kāṇikkai uḷḷūrppuṟakkalaṉai maṟṟum epperp
paṭṭa varikaḷum

inscription.head:
No. 720.
(A.R. No. 663 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅgachōḷa III. Year 24: 1201-02 A.D.

This incomplete inscription mentions the dēvadāṉa village Pañjoṭṭi and the deity
Tirumullaivāyil-uḍaiyār in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravarttikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ mu-

l.ta: r[k*]ku innāyanār tevatāṉam pañcoṭṭiyiṟ kāṇi uṭaiya

inscription.head:
No. 721.
(A.R. No. 664 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Harihara II. 1403 A.D.

The details of date viz., Subhānu, Makara, śu. Pūrṇimā, Friday, Punarpūśam,
regularly correspond to 1403 A.D., December 28, Friday, ·64 ·84.

This records the gift of a share (viḻukkāḍu) of land in the village Aṇṇalūr in Veṇṇaiyūr
nāḍu, by Vallaṅgiḻāṉ Śōḻappallavadaraiyaṉ Aruḷāḷanāthar-Śeyyanārāyaṇa-
dēvaṉ of Ambattūr in Ambattūr-nāḍu to the deity Tirumullaivāyal-uḍaiya nāyiṉār,
in Kāṉappēṟūr-nāḍu, in Vikkirama-śōḻavaḷanāḍu, for conducting the
Tiruvūḍal festival on the seventh day of the festival in the month of Paṅguṉi.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] hariyararāyaṉukkuc cellāniṉṟa cupāṉu caṅvaṟcara makara nāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu
veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum [pūr*]naiyum peṟṟa puṇarpūcattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa-

l.ta:
coḻamaṇṭalattu puḻaṟ koṭṭam āṉa vi[k*]kiṟamacoḻavaḷaṉāṭṭu cāṉappeṟur ṉāṭṭu uṭai
yār tirumullaivāyal uṭaiya ṉāyi[ṉā]ṟku i[m*]maṇṭalattu ikko[ṭṭa]ttu ampa-

tturṉāṭṭu ampattur vallaṅkiḻāṉ coḻap pallavataraiyaṉ aruḷāḷaṉātar ceyya nārāyaṇa
tevaṉeṉ kāṇi utakapūṟuvap piṟamāṇam paṇṇik kuṭuttapaṭi veṇṇai-

yūr nāṭṭu eṅkaḷ kāṇiyāṉa aṇṇalūr ūr oṉṟāṉa karai nālil eṉ viḻukkāṭu
nālil oṉṟum nāyiṉār tirupaṅkuṉit tiruṉāḷ tiruvūṭal tirunāḷ eṉ upaiyattukku tiru
nāmattu-

kkāṇi āka utakam paṇṇik kuṭutteṉ inta utaka pūṟuvap paṭiye naṭattikkoṇṭu eḻāntiru
nāḷ tiruvūṭal tiruṉāḷ cantirātittavaraikku tāḻvaṟa naṭattikkoḷ-

ḷavum ippaṭikku tirumalaiyile cilālekai paṇṇik kuṭutteṉ aruḷāḷanātar ceyya
nārāyaṇatevaṉeṉ ippaṭikku ivai aruḷāḷanātar ceyyanā-

rāyaṇatevaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭi aṟiveṉ coḻap pallavataraiyaṉ ulaku uyyakkoṇṭār
veṅkaṭattāṉ eḻuttu ippaṭi aṟiveṉ coḻa-

p pallavataraiya[r] vaṭukanātar tiruveṅkaṭanātaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭi aṟiveṉ coḻappallava
taraiyaṉ aṟam vaḷa[r]tta nāyaṉ eḻuttu ip-
[paṭi] aṟiveṉ coḻappallavataraiyaṉ malai niṉṟa perumāḷ camai[ya] muta[li] eḻuttu
ippaṭi aṟiveṉ coḻap pallavataraiyaṉ [ulaku] toḻā niṉṟāṉeṉ i[p*]paṭia[ṟi]

inscription.head:
No. 722.
(A.R. No. 665 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Dēvarāya II. 1424 A.D.

The cyclic year Krōdhin cited in the inscription fell in Śaka 1346. The palaeo-
graphy of the record would not militate against this. The details of date, viz.,
Krōdhin, Aṟpaśi 2 may correspond to 1424 A.D. September 30, in which case the
king may be identified with Dēvarāya II.

This inscription records the gift of 4000 kuḻi of land by Oṟṟi Maṉṉaṉ alias Uḍaiyār
Oṟṟi-Araśar and Araśuperumāḷ alias Kāḍavarāya for conducting certain special
festivals in the Kāḍavarāyar tiruttōppu (sacred grove) and at the
Aśaṅgādagaṇḍaṉ-maṇḍapa in the temple of Tirumullaivāyal-uḍaiya-nāyanār. The
land was given away with irrigation facilities from the donor's lake (periyēri).
Also mentions the measuring rod Aśaṅgāḍagaṇḍaṉ-kōl.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] irāyar pratāpadēvarāyaṟkkuc cellāniṉṟa krōdhi varuṣam aṟpci
mātam iraṇṭāntiyati

l.ta:
uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyal uṭaiya nāyiṉār tirunāḷ upayattukkum acaṅkāta kaṇṭaṉ maṇṭa
pattil ciṟappukkum

kāṭavarāyar tiruttoppil āna tirunāḷc ciṟappukkum oṟṟimaṉṉarāṉa uṭaiyār oṟṟi aracarum
aracu perumāḷ āṉa

kāṭavarāyarum inta upayattukku eṅkaḷ kāṇi āṭci āṉa veḷḷāṉ ūril kiḻaik kaḻaniyil
māṉkuḻikku teṟkku

kaḷaṅkuḻikku vaṭakku muṉṉāḷ uṭaiyavaṟku viṭṭa viḷakkuppaṭṭikku meṟkku acaṅkātakaṇṭaṉ
aḷavu kolā-

ikkuḻi nālāyiramum kaikkoṇṭu inta upayam iraṇṭum cantirātitta
varaiyum naṭattak kaṭa-

vatākavum ippaṭikku tirukkoyilile tirukkaṭṭaḷaiyile cilālekai[yu]m paṇṇi iṉṉila[t*]
tiṟ tiruccūlattāpanamum paṇṇi

kkuṭuttom iṉṉilattukku eṅkaḷ periyeri perunirum āṟu nirum pāyakkaṭavatākavum
ippaṭikku cantiṟātittavaraiyum naṭakkak ka-

ṭavatāka kalveṭṭikkuṭuttom oṟṟi aracar kāṭavarāyarom ippaṭikku ivai oṟṟi aracar
eḻuttu ippaṭi-

inscription.head:
No. 723.
(A.R. No. 666 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya I. Year 15: 1265-66 A.D.

The provenance of this record so far to the north and its palaeography would
point to the identity of the king with Sundarapāṇḍya I.

This records the gift of the proceeds from certain taxes like ūr-kaḍamai, āyam,
peṟaśādavari etc. by the inhabitants (nāṭṭavar) of Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam and Īkkāṭṭu-kōṭṭam,
for repairs, and unguents in the temple of Tirumullaivāyil-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, at Kāṉap
pēṟūr-nāḍu, in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] koṟcaṭapaṉmar tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīcuntarapāṇṭiyatevarkku
yāṇṭu 15 vatu ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇ-

l.ta:
ṭalattu puḻaṟ koṭṭattu kāṉappeṟūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyiluṭaiya nāyaṉārkku
puḻaṟ koṭṭattu nā[ṭ*]ṭavarum

īkkāṭṭu koṭṭattu nā[ṭ*]ṭavarum iṉṉāyaṉārkkut tiruppaṇikkum cāttuppaṭi pūccuppaṭik
kum uṭalāka iṭṭa tirumullaivā-

yilil puṟakkalaṉai iṟukkum palakalaṉaip perāl ūrkaṭamai āyamum maṉṟāṭikaḷ peṟacāta
variyum maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭ-
nā[ṭ*]ṭavarom ivai paṇiyāl cittirameḻiveḷāṉ eḻuttu paṉ

inscription.head:
No. 724.
(A.R. No. 667 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 21: 1198-1199 A.D.

The king of this record who is described as Maduraiyum Pāṇḍiyaṉ-muḍittalaiyum
koṇḍaruḷiṉa may be identified with Kulōttuṅga III.

This inscription records the gift of 24 Bhujabala pudumāḍai, left in the hands of
the śivabrāhmaṉas (names specified) of the temple, by Śivabhūtaṉ Madi-
śūdaṉa Vāṉavar Nāyakaṉ of Kōyaṁbēḍu in Māṅgāḍu-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam
a sub-division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, for burning two perpetual lamps in the
temple of Tirumullaivāyil-uḍaiyār in Kāṉappēṟūr-nāḍu in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalai[yum*] koṇ
ṭaruḷiṉa śrī kulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 21 jayaṅkoṇṭaco-

l.ta:
ḻa maṇṭalattup puḻaṟ koṭṭattuk kāṉappeṟūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyiluṭaiyāṟku
immaṇṭalattup puliyūr koṭṭattu māṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭuk koyampe-

ṭṭuc civapūtaṉ maticūtaṉa vāṉavar nāyakaṉ cantirātittavarai celvatāka vaitta tirunantā
viḷakku iraṇṭukkum ikkoyiliṟ kāṇiyuṭaiya civappirāmaṇaril

paṉ āḷumpirāṉāṉa kulottuṅkacoḻapaṭṭaṉeṉum śrīkaṇṭanātaṉāṉa tirucciṟṟam
pala paṭṭaṉeṉum kā

māril ceyya periyāṉ paṭṭaṉ uḷpaṭṭa ivvaṉaivomum yivaṉ pakkal kaikkoṇṭa puca
palappu[tu]māṭai
kku iraṇṭum cantirātittavarai erippatākac cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭuttom [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 725.
(A.R. No. 668 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Bukkarāya II. 1406 A.D.

The cyclic year cited in the record, i.e., Pārthiva fell in Saka 1327. The details
of date, viz., Pārthiva, Māśi 1 probably correspond to 1406 A.D., January 26.

This records the gift of 800 kuḻi of land in Veḷḷāṉūr by Ayyaluppa Kaḍaiyār, son
of Payiṇḍi-Arāśar, to the temple of Tirumullaivāyil-uḍaiya Nāyanār in Kāṉappēṟūr-
nāḍu, in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam, for the service of tiruppaḷḷi-eḻuchchi. The gift land is stated
to have been formerly granted to the donor by Vīranārāyaṇaṉ [Iṉa]kku nalla perumāḷ
alias Oṟṟi Araśar, on the occasion of a solar eclipse. The inscription also mentions
the measuring rod Aśaṅgādagaṇḍaṉ kōl. Vide No. 693 above.

l.ta: kkoṭṭattuk kāṉappeṟūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumullaivā[yi*]l uṭaiya ṉāyināṟkku

r ayyaluppa kaṭaiyār iṉṉāyanāṟkku tiruppaḷḷieḻiccikku amutupaṭikku nāḷ oṉṟukku
eṭṭu nāḻikkā(l)lāl arici a[ñ*]ñāḻi āka varuṣa-

[ma] oṉṟukku mātam paṉṉiraṇṭukku arici irupatiṉ kalattukku añcil iraṇṭu paṭikku
kuṭukkum nellu aiyampatiṉ kalam [iṉ]-

ṉellu aiyampatiṉ kalattukku vīranārāyaṇaṉ [iṉa]kku nalla perumāḷāṉa oṟṟi aracar
namakku so
haṇa puṇṇiya kālattile tammuṭaiya kāṇi āṭ(c)ci-

āṉa veḷḷāṉūrile muṉṉāḷc ciyaṉāyaṉār āṉa oṟṟi aracar viṭṭa tiruviḷakkuppaṭṭikkut
teṟkku namakku utaka pūṟuvam āka viṭappaṭṭa viṟutti acaṅkātakaṇṭaṉ ko-

lālk kuḻi eṇṇūṟum iṉṉāyaṉārkkut tiruppaḷḷieḻuccikku a(m)mutupaṭikku iṉṉilam
eṇṇūṟu kuḻiyum cantirātittavaraiyum cel(la)-

edition.l:
vatāka iṉṉilattile tiruccūlattāpanam(ma) paṇṇit tirumalaiyile cilālekam paṇṇik
kuṭuttom ||—[text to be inserted]

inscription.head:
No. 726.
(A.R. No. 669 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Uttamachōḷa. Year 14.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 141, p. 287.

inscription.head:
No. 727.
(A.R. No. 670 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya I. Year 17: 1267-68 A.D.

The provenance of this record and its palaeography suggest the identity of the
king with Sundarapāṇḍya I.

This inscription records an agreement among the inhabitants of Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam
alias Vikramaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, regarding a gift of 148
māḍai, by Śendāmaraikkaṇṇan of Tirumullaivāyal, in Kāṉappēṟūr-nāḍu, for conducting
a special service called Vikkiramaśōḻa-vaḷanāṭṭār Sandi, the Vaikāśi festivals and
for repairs in the temple of Tirumullaivāyil-uḍaiya nāyaṉār.

l.ta:
iṉṉāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumullai
vāyiluṭaiya nāyaṉārkkup pūcaikkum tiruppaṇikkum nāṭṭup pe-

rāl vikkiramacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭār canti amutu ceytaruḷavum tiruvaikācit tirunāḷ eḻuntaruḷu
vikkavum innāyaṉār tevatāṉam tirunā-

mattuk kāṇiyāṉa kāṉappeṟūr nāṭṭut tirumullaivāyal centāmaraikkaṇṇan tarappaṭi māṭai
ṉūṟṟuṉāṟpattu eṭṭukku nāṭṭār viva-

ppaṭi paṇattil iṟukka niccayitta oṉṟu pāti [pa]ṇa nikki nikkip pūcaikkum tiruppaṇik
kum tirunāḷukkum kaḻitta oṉṟu pā[ti*]

oṉṟu pātiyāl vanta paṇam nāṭṭile iṟukkak kaṭavomākavum payir tiṭṭik kaṭamai koḷḷum
āṇṭukku tiṭṭiṉa payirukku pattil

aiñcu nikki nikki niṉṟa mutalil oṉṟu pātiyāl varu mutal nāṭṭile eṟṟi iṟukkak kaṭavom
ippaṭikku cantirātittavarai celvatāka cempi-

lum kallilum veṭṭikkoḷḷac cammatittom puḻaṟ koṭṭamāṉa vikkiramacoḻa vaḷa
nāṭṭārom ivai cittirameḻiveḷāṉ eḻuttu itu

inscription.head:
No. 728.
(A.R. No. 671 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇiśvara temple.
Harihara II. 1400 A.D.

The cyclic year Vikrama cited in the record fell in Śaka 1322. The king may be
identified with Harihara II.

This inscription records the gift of some materials used in worship (tirumuṭṭu)
such as tiruvaṭṭaṇaikkāl, tiruvandikkāppu-taṭṭi and taḷigai, in all weighing 200 (palam)
by Naṟpākkiḻāṉ Koṟṟaḷappāṉ Śūriyadēvaṉ of Ākuḍi-nāḍu to the deity Tirumullaivāyal-
uḍaiya nāyiṉār at Kāṉappēṟūr-nāḍu. It also records an endowment of 25 paṇam
by Śūriyappiḷḷai, towards the supply of ghee when the tiruvandikkāppu ceremony is
conducted.

l.ta:
ariyararkkuc cellā niṉṟa vikkirama varuṣam jayaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattu puḻa[ṟ*](k)koṭṭattu

kāṉappeṟūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyil uṭaiya nāyiṉāṟkku ikkoṭṭattil ākuṭināṭ
ṭil kolaṭiyil

naṟpākkiḻāṉ koṟṟaḷappāṉ cūriyatevaṉ iṭṭa tirumuṭṭu tiruvaṭṭaṇaikkā liraṇṭiṉā liṭai
nūṟum

tiruvantik kāppu taṭṭi iraṇṭiṉā liṭai aimpatum taḷikai oṉṟuṉā liṭai aiympatum āka tiru
muṭṭu añcunāl

iṭai irunūṟum caṉtirātittavarai naṭakkumpaṭikku [ci]lālekai paṇṇikkuṭutteṉ ippaṭikku
ivai

inscription.head:
No. 729.
(A.R. No. 672 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Year 4.

This inscription records the order of Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḷa to the effect
that taxes like poṉvari, etc., should be levied only on 10 vēli of land and that the levies
kuṟṟappaṭṭi and śīkāriyappēṟu, were remitted, for services in the temple of Tirumul-
laivāyal-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār.

Gaṇḍagōpāla figuring as the signatory of the grant is obviously the same as
Madhurāntaka-Pottappichchōḷa. (cf., No. 719 above.)

edition.l:
[svastiśrī] [||*] [maturā]ṉtakap pottappic coḻaṉ olai uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyiluṭaiya
nāyaṉār koyil [tāṉattār]

l.ta:
kaṇṭu viṭai tirumullaivāyil nālā[vatu] mutal tarattil varum poṉvari [uḷ]ḷiṭṭa varikaḷ
koḷḷumiṭattu pattu veli nilattukku

kkoḷḷavum kuṟṟappaṭṭiyum cikāriyappeṟu tiruppaṇikku uṭalāka viṭṭ[om i*]ppaṭi ceyvate
ivai kaṇṭakopālaṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 730.
(A.R. No. 673 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine, in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 39: 1216-17 A.D.

This records the grant of lands in Mudalaimēḍu alias Parakēsarinallūr in Vaḍa-
Paiyyūr-kōṭṭam and made tax-free by the king at the instance of Vāṇādarāyaṉ as
dēvadāna and maḍappuṟa-iṛaiyili to the temple of Tirumullaivāyil-uḍaiyār and to the
Tribhuvanavīran-maṭha founded at the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam by Tapasya Kāśyapaṉ,
a Śivabrāhmaṉa of the temple.

l.ta:
ṉāl koṉeriṉmaik[oṇṭāṉ ja]yaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭa[la*]ttu puḻaṟkoṭṭattu
kānappeṟūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyiluṭaiyār koyiṟ patipāta mulappaṭṭuṭai pañcā
cā

rak kaṇkāṇi ceyvārka[ḷu]kku cikāriyañ ceyvāṉukku it
tevaṟku veṇṭu nivantaṅka-

inscription.head:
No. 731.
(A.R. No. 674 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Rājarāja III. Year 16: 1231-32 A.D.

This inscription records the sale of 15 vēli of land with rights of irrigation, for
60 paḻampuḷḷi-māḍai, by the mahāsabhā of Agaram Kōḻippadāgai alias Kulōttuṅgasōḻa-
chatūrvēdimaṅgalam, in Kāṉappēṟūr-nāḍu, in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam, as tirunāmattukkāṇi,
to the deity.

In detailing the boundaries of the land, the record mentions the highway at
Niṉṟavūr

l.ta: ṇṭalattup puḻaṟkoṭṭattuk kāṉappeṟūr nāṭṭu akaram koḻippatākaiyāṉa ku-
vatu kiḻkaḻaṉi aiyyaṉ eriyum kaḻaṉikkum kiḻpā kellai tirumullaivāyil
ellaikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai niṉṟavūr peruvaḻikku vaṭakkum melpā-
ṟkellai taṇṭaṅkaḻik[ku]m aiyyaṉeri melkaṭaikkum vaṭakkil puḷiyaṅku-

ṭikkum kuṭṭiceṟuvukkum kiḻakku ce[ri]meṭunattamum vaṭapāṟkellai viḷakkuppaṭṭikkum
tirukkāṉerikaraikku teṟkum

[yi]ṉṉāṉkellaiyuḷakappaṭṭa nilam patiṉai[y] veliyum ittevaṟku viṟṟu nāṅkaḷ
kaikkoṇṭa paḻampuḷḷimā-

immāṭai aṟupatum kaikkoṇṭu yiṉṉilam tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka viṟṟu
iṉṉilattukku eṅkaḷur peri
yeri nirum pāyakkaṭavatākac cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭuttom koḻippatākai

inscription.head:
No. 732.
(A.R. No. 675 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Harihara II. 1388 A.D.

The details of date, viz., Prabhava, Makara śu. 5, Monday, Aṉiḻam are irregular
as the combination of the paksha, tithi and the star are impossible in the month of
Makara. The cyclic year Prabhava fell in Śaka 1309.

This inscription records the gift of 400 kuḻi of land made tax-free by the inhabitants
of Ambattūr in Ambattūr-nāḍu to the deity Tirumullaivāyal-uḍaiya-Nāyiṉār, at
Kāṉappeṟūr-nāḍu, in Vikkiṟamaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu alias Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam for the conduct of hunting festivals on saṅkrama days (Saṅkramappāḍivēṭṭai,)
taking out the deity to the Ulaguyyakkoṇḍāṉ-ttiruttōppu.
The record also mentions the measuring rod muppattiraṇḍaḍikkōl.

l.ta:
pakṣattu tiṅkaḷ kiḻamaiyum pañcamiyum peṟṟa
aṉiḻattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu puḻaṟkoṭṭa-

m āṉa vikkiṟamacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu kāṉappeṟūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyal
uṭaiya nayināṟku immaṇṭalattu ikkoṭṭattu ampattūr nā-

ṭṭu ampattūr vallaṅkiḻāṉ coḻappallavataraiy(ā)rkaḷom uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyal
uṭaiya nāyiṉār caṅkṟamap pāṭiveṭṭaikku eṅka-

ḷūril tiruvitiyum eḻuntaruḷi ulakuyyak koṇṭāṉ tiruttop[pil eṟi]aruḷukai
yil maṇṭapak kuṟ[aivaṟuppu]kku eṅkaḷ ūril y[e]rietir vara[mpi]le a[manippa]

ttil ellaikku kiḻakku ellai ācaiyile peraḷavukolāl inta upaiyattukku
iṟaiyili māṉya(m)māka viṭṭa[tu] kuḻi

kkoṇṭu maṇṭapakkuṟaiva[ṟu]ppāl uḷḷatu śrīpaṇṭārattile naṭatti koḷḷak kaṭavarka
ḷākavum inta nilattukku aṟunir perunīr kūṭai iṟaittukoḷḷavum

uḷḷa iṭattile kuṭalai turavu iṭṭu iṟaittukkoḷḷakkaṭavarkaḷākavum ippaṭikku
tirunāmattilkkāṇi iṟaiyilimāṉ[ya]māka

kai paṇṇik kuṭuttom uṭaiyār tirumu[l*]laivāyal uṭaiya nāyiṉāṟku ampattūril
ūravarom ippaṭikku ivai coḻapallavaraiyarkaḷ kotai āḻvār eḻuttu

ivai āṇṭār eḻuttu ivai veṅkaṭavar eḻuttu ivai aruḷāḷa nātar eḻuttu
ivai tirumaṅkai āḻvār eḻuttu ivarkaḷ colla cilārekai paṇṇina[m*]aikku

upayattukku akitam paṇṇi
navarkaḷ keṅkai karaiyil kapilaiyai koṉṟa pāvattile poka kaṭavarkaḷ[ā]-

inscription.head:
No. 733.
(A.R. No. 676 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab built into the floor of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Pārthivēndravarman. Year 5.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 174.

inscription.head:
No. 734.
(A.R. No. 677 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Rājēndrachōḷa I: Year 3: 1014-15 A.D.

This inscription records the construction of the maṇḍapa, by Iśakkaṉ Aṟivāḷaṉ
a servant of Tirunalluḻān of Śrī Parāntakanallūr alias Pūśipākkam, in Ūṟṟukkāḍu
nāḍu, in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭu-kōṭṭam, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] kopparakesarivarmmarāna śrirā-

inscription.head:
No. 735.
(A.R. No. 678 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a second pillar in the maṇḍapa of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara
temple.
Rājēndrachōḷa I. Year 7: 1018-19 A.D.

This damaged inscription begins with the praśasti commencing with Tirumaṉṉi
vaḷara, etc., of the king.

It records the deed pertaining to the sale of 4000 kuḻi of land, by the members
of the sabhā of Ambattūr in Ambattūr-nāḍu in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam to a Veḷḷān of Villip-
pākkam and entrusted to the authorities of the temple of Tirumullaivāyil-uḍaiyār,
for burning a prpetual lamp.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] tirumaṉṉivaḷara iru nila maṭantaiyum porcceyap pāvaiyum cirttaṉic-

l.ta:
celviyum taṉ perunteviyarāki yiṉpuṟa neṭitiyalūḻiyuḷ ḷiṭaituṟai nāṭum tuṭarva
ṉaveli-

ppaṭar vaṉavāciyuñ cuḷḷic cūḻ matiṭk koḷḷippākkaiyum naṇṇaṟkarumuraṇ maṇ
ṇaikkaṭakkamum po-

rukaṭaliḻattaraicar tam muṭiyu māṅkavar teviya roṅkeḻil muṭiyum muṉṉavar pakkal(ta)

ṟkkellai•••••••

inscription.head:
No. 736.
(A.R. No. 679 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
At the entrance into the maṇḍapa in the central shrine of the Māśilāmaṇīśvara
temple.
Dēvarāya II. Undated.

This inscription records the royal order (irājakam) granting the proceeds from taxes
like, śōḍi mugampārvai, aggaśālaikal, śampaḍam, viśēsha-Adāyam, ariśikāṇam, nal-erudu,
naṟ-paśu, veṭṭi and kaṭṭāyam in favour of the temples at Kāñchīpuram,
Kāḷahasti, Tiruvoṟṟiyūr, Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruppālaivanam, Tirumullaivāyal and
other Śaiva and Vaishṇava temples, of Chandragiri for the conduct of daily services
and festivals without break and stating that the temple lands should
not be leased out. This order was issued at the instance of VayiravanāyiṉAr and the
māhēśvaras of the temples, who represented that the services and festivals in the temple
were not conducted properly as the incomes from taxes and from the temple lands
were misappropriated by the Jīvitakkāṛar, the state officials and the Sthānattār.Similar inscriptions are found at Tiruvaigāvūr (A.R. Ep., 1915, pp: 106-108) and at Vṛiddhācha-
lam (A.R. Ep.; 1913; App: C; No. 92).

l.ta:
kāñcipuram tirukkāḷatti tiruoṟṟiūr tiruvālaṅkāṭu
tiruppālaivanam tirumullaivāyal maṟṟum uḷḷiṭṭa civa

••••••••• kaṭaikkūṭṭāna coṭi mukampārvai aṅkacālaikaḷ campaṭam
viceṣātāyam ivaiyiṟṟileyum tevamaṇṭala ūrkaḷ civitakārar atikāri kaṭaik
kūṭṭar tāṉattār kuttakai koḷḷukaiyālum ivaiyiṟṟuṉāle kuṭiyuḷḷatum kulaintu

l.note: may be restored as ºla potukiṟate.
l.ta:
••••••• ṉṟu panmāhecurarum va[yi]ravanāyiṉārum [nama]kku viṇṇap
ppam ceytārkaḷ anta tevamaṇṭalaṅkaḷil atikāri coṭi mukampārvaiyum kaṭaikkūṭṭāṉa
coṭimukam pārvaiyuṅ aṅkacālaikaḷ campaṭam viceṣātāyam aricikāṇam nal erutu naṟpacu
veṭṭi kaṭṭāyam inta vakaip paṭi mutal uḷḷa

l.note: may be restored as ºrayum vayiravayināyiṉā
l.ta:
•••• raiyum mun iṭṭuk koṇṭu śrīpaṇṭārat[ti]le mutal iṭṭu
mahāpūcai tiruppaṇi tiṅkaḷ tivasa

uṟcamaṅkaḷum tāḻvaṟa naṭattik
koḷḷak kaṭavatu ākavum

vamaṇṭalaṅkaḷ iyātoruttaṟkum kuttakai kuṭukka
kaṭavatu allavākavum inta irājakappaṭi cantirātittavaraiyu[m] naṭatta kaṭavatu ā-

kavum ita••••••••• keṅkaik karaiyil kapilaiyaik koṉṟa pāvattile pokak kaṭavaṉ
śrīmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 737.
(A.R. No. 680 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
At the entrance to the maṇḍapa in the central shrine of the Māśilāmaṇīśvara
temple.
Mallikārjuna. 1452 A.D.

The inscription is dated in the cyclic year Prajāpati which fell in Śaka 1374,
The details of date viz., Prajāpati, Makara śu. 10 Friday, Pūśam are irregular, as
the combination of the tithi and the star in the month of Makara is impossible. If
however, the tithi śu. 10, is taken as a mistake for 15, the given date will regularly
correspond to 1452 A.D., January 7, Friday, ·01; ·47.

This inscription records the gift of land as sarvamānya by the members of the
mahāsabhā of Kōḻippadāgai alias Kulōttuṅga-śōḻachaturvēdimaṅgalam for various
offerings to the goddess Koḍiyiḍai-nāchchiyār on the occasion of Tiruppūrattirunāḷ.

In detailing the boundaries of the gift land the record mentions the bank of the
lake called Tiruttānēri; and Veḷḷāṉūr.

l.gr: svasti [||*] śrīman mahāmaṇḍalīśvara rājādhirāja rāja paramēśvara

l.ta: pakṣattu t[e]ca[mi]yum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa pūcattu nāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭa-
co[ḻa maṇ]ṭalattu puḻaṟkoṭṭattu kāṉappeṟūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyal uṭaiya
vaṟku akaram koḻippatākai āṉa kulottuṅkacoḻa caturvetimaṅka-
paiyam amutupaṭi kaṟiamutupaṭi veñcaṉam cāttupaṭi pūccuppaṭikkum e[ṅ*]kaḷ ūr kiḻkaḻa-
ṉiyil i[ṟaiyiḻi]cci caṟuvamāṉiyam tirunāmattilkāṇi āka viṭṭa nilam kiḻpāṟkellai tiru-

mullaivāyal ellaikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai tiruttānerikaraikku vaṭakku melpāṟkellai
ceri-

[m]eṭṭu nattam ce[r]nta melaittarucu vaṭakku veḷāṉūr ellaiyuṟak kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai
veḷḷāṉūr

ellaikku teṟkum innānkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa nilam cantirātittavaraiyum caṟuvamāṉiyam celva
tāka cilā-
yār paṇiyāl koḻip patākai uṭaiyā[n] periyamutali eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 738.
(A.R. No. 681 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the first prākāra in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Rājēndrachōḷa I. Year 3: 1014-15 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 90 sheep left in the hands of some shepherds
by Nāgaṉ Veḷḷi of Ugumbil, in Māṅgāḍu-nāḍu, a district of Puliyūr-kōṭṭam, a division
of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḷa maṇḍapam, for burning a perpetual lamp daily in the temple
of Tirumullaivāyil-āṇḍār, at Tirumullaivāyil in Kāṉappēṟ[ūr]-nāḍu, a district
of Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 739.
(A.R. No. 682 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar built into the first prākāra of the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Rājēndra I. Year [4]: 1015-16 A.D.
This inscription cites both the years 3 and 4 of the king.

This records the gift of some precious jewels set with stones such as rubies (māṇik-
kam), sapphire (maragadam), diamonds and pearls (in all 105) and ten flowers made
of gold for the deities Mullai Aḻagar alias Śrī Pāśupatadēva and Astradēva in the temple
of Tirumullaivāyal, in Kāṉappēṟūr-nāḍu, in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam and refers to some provision
for food offerings and for the conduct of services like abhishēka and for taking out
the deities in procession.

inscription.head:
No. 740.
(A.R. No. 683 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab built into the floor of the maṇḍapa in front of the Latāmadhyāmbā
shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Pārthivēndrādhipativarman. Year 13.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 196.

inscription.head:
No. 741.
(A.R. No. 684 of 1904.)
TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar lying at the southern entrance to the maṇḍapa in front of the Latā-
madhyāmba shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Rājēndrachōḷa I. Year 3: 1014-15 A.D.

This inscription records the gift, of 1200 kuḻi of land by Tirunalluḻāṉ Iśakka-
naṟivāḷaṉ of (vide No. 734 above) Achcharapākkam alias Śrī Parāntakanallūr in
Ūṟṟukkāḍu-nāḍu in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭu-kōṭṭam for food offerings to the deities Astradēva
and Pāśupatadēva in the temple of Tirumullaivāyil Aṇṇar. The land was purchased
by the donor from the Ūrār of Urōḍagam in Puḻal-nādu, who made it also tax-free.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] kopparake-

l.note:
Another piece connected with this contains a few more illegible lines which appear to give the
names of the signatories.

inscription.head:

inscription.head:
No. 751.
(A.R. No. 694 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Kharapurīśvara temple.
Dēvarāya II. 1442 A.D.

The details of date viz., Śaka 1364, Durmati, Kumbha śu. 11 Wednesday,
Pūśam regularly correspond to 1442 A.D., February 21, Wednesday, ·20; ·79. The
Śaka year 1364 was current.

This inscription records the remission of the taxes śūlavari and jōḍi in favour of
the temples of Tirukkarapuram-uḍaiya nāyaṉār, Tiruvālīśvaram-uḍaiya nāyaṉār,
Tirukkuṟuṅguḍi Appaṉ, and Perumāḷ Paḷḷikoṇḍaperumāḷ at Tiruppāṟkaḍal by Sāḷuva
Tippayadēvamahārāja, for worship and offerings on all occasions to be conducted
regularly by Mahattumallayasvāmigaḷ.

edition.l:
svasti [||*] śrīmanmakāmaṇṭaliśvaran hariḷarāya vipāṭan pā[ ṣaik*] kut tappuvarāyara kaṇṭan
muvarāyakaṇṭan intuirāya-

inscription.head:
No. 752.
(A.R. No. 695 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the first prākāra in the Kharapurīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅga III. Year 30: 1207-08 A.D.

The damaged inscription is dated in the reign of Tribhuvanavīradēva who is identi-
cal with Kulōttuṅga III.

It seems to record the gift of a lamp to the deity Tirukkarapuram-uḍaiya-nāyanār
at Tiruppāṟkaḍal.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaviratevaṟku yāṇṭu 30tāvatu tiruppāṟkaṭalā-

l.note: This inscription is completely worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 753.
(A.R. No. 696 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Kharapurīśvara temple.
Kampaṇa Uḍaiyar. 1372 A.D.

The details of date viz., Śaka 1294, Paridhāvi, Kaṉṉi, śu 7, Sunday, Mūla corres-
pond to 1372 A.D., September 5, Sunday f.d.n. ·16.

This damaged inscription seems to record the gift of money by Poṉṉuḷāṉ Mallinā-
thar of Poṉṉūr and a transaction by the mabāsabhā consisting of the Bhaṭṭas and
Viśishthas of Kāviripākkam alias Vikramachōḷa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a free-hold
taṉiyūr) of Paḍuvūrkōṭṭam, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

nāḷ jayaṅko-

inscription.head:
No. 754.
(A.R. No. 697 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the first prākāra in the Kharapurīśvara temple.
Harihara II. 1382 A.D.

The date discussed here points to the identity of the King Ariyaṇṇa-Uḍaiyar with
Harihara II. The details of date viz. Śaka 1304, Dundubhi, śu. 14, Sunday, Śadaiyam
will correspond to 1382 A.D. August 24 if the month is Siṁha as indicated by the
combination of the paksha, thithi and star. The thithi ended however at ·45
of the previous day.

This inscription records the gift of land as Kāṇiyāṭchi, by the trustees (sthānattār)
of the Tirukkarapuram-uḍaiya nāyaṉār temple at Tiruppāṟkaḍal, to the inhabitants
of Tiruppāṟkaḍal in return for contributions towards the expenses of the services to
the deity.

l.ta: ṉ muvarāyara kaṇṭaṉ pūṟuvatekṣiṇa paccima camuttiṟātipati śrī virapukkaṇa uṭaiyar-

maiyum peṟṟa cataiyattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu tāmaṟ koṭṭattu vaṭakarai

ūr(ā)varil puṉṉai kiḻār-

inscription.head:
No. 755.
(A.R. No. 698 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the first prākāra of the Kharapurīśvara temple.
Kulōttuṅgachōḷa III. Year 30: 1207-08 A.D.

This damaged inscription seems to record a gift of land (detailst lost), made free
of taxes such as kāśāyam, āśuvikkaḍamai, āḷamañji tēvai etc., as jīvitam to
Aṅgada-vallavar, at Tiruppāṟkaḍal, a southern hamlet of Kāvirippākkam. The donor's
name is lost.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattika[ḷ śrī] kulottuṅka coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu muppa[tāvatu]
•••• kaṇavirācaṉ••••••• kaya teva•• [neṉ kāvirippākkattu] teṉ piṭākai

tiruppāṟkaṭal tevatāṉamum tiruviṉ••••••• kkiṉ•• ṟkellaikku naṭuppaṭṭa
nañcai puñcai••••••••• ācuvikkaṭamai yuḷḷiṭṭa kācāyamum āḷ a-

mañci tevaikaḷum maṟṟum nām koḷḷum kaṭamaikaḷum••••m aṅkatavallavarkku civita
māka vi[ṭṭu kal]veṭṭik kuṭutte•••••••••

kil pa[l*]lavaraiyaṉ cattiyanum ippaṭi ca[n]tirātittavarai ce[l*]vatākavum itukku ilaṅ
kaṉam paṇṇuvār keṅkai iṭai kumari iṭai kurālpacu ko[ṉṟavaṉ] pāvamkoṇṭu••••
aṅkata vallava turohikkum•

inscription.head:
No. 756.
(A.R. No. 699 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra of the Kharapurīśvara temple.
Vijayagaṇḍagōpāla. Undated.

This inscription records the gift of 4 vēli of land in Tiruppāṟkaḍal, as dēvadāna,
along with the income from all levies to the deity Tirukkarapuram-uḍaiya nāyaṉār
at Tiruppā ṟkaḍal, by Vijayagaṇḍagōpāla alias Madhurāntaka-Pottappichchōḻa.

l.ta: vāṉ keṅkai iṭai kumari iṭai kurāṟpacu koṉṟān pāḻakattu

inscription.head:
No. 757.
(A.R. No. 700 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the first prākāra in the Kharapurīśvara temple.
Kōpperuñjiṅgadēva. Year 16: 1259-60 A.D.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XII, No. 190.

inscription.head:
No. 758.
(A.R. No. 701 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the first prākāra in the Kharapurīśvara temple.
Kampaṇa Uḍaiyar. Śaka 1391: 1469 A.D.
The details of date viz Mēsha śu 9, Saturday, Hasta are irregular.

This badly damaged inscription seems to record the sale of a number of houses
by the Mahārudrar Śrī-māhēśvarar and Kaikkōḷa-mudalis, who had assembled in the
temple of Tirukkarapuramuḍaiya Nāyaṉār at Tiruppāṟkaḍal, for meeting the expenses
of taking out the deity mudaliyār in procession.

kumārar virakampaṇa uṭaiyarkku[c cellā niṉṟa]

m āyirattu irunūṟṟu toṇṇūṟṟu [o]ṉṟiṉmel [meṣa]nāyaṟṟu pūṟuva pakṣattu navami

inscription.head:
No. 759.
(A.R. No. 702 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the first prākāra, to the right of the entrance in the Kharapurīś
vara temple.
Sundarapāṇḍya I Year 14, 181st day: 1264 A.D.

The king mentioned in the record as Emmaṇḍalamum koṇḍaruḷiya Dēvēndra val-
lavan (vallabhan) Sundara-pāṇḍya is evidently identical with Jaṭāvarman Sundara-
pāṇḍya I. He is also stated to have issued this memorandum (niṉaīppu) from Kaṇ-
ṇaṉūr which may be easily identified with Kaṇṇaṉūr near Tiruchirāppaḷḷi, which was
till then, the Hoysaḷa capital in the south.

This records the royal order, issued from Kaṇṇaṉūr, registering the gift of 3 vēli
of land made free of taxes such as antarāyam, poṉvari etc in Tiruppāṟkadal to Kākku-
nāyaka-pichchar, as maḍappuṟattu-iṟaiyili

There is another incomplete inscription below this, of the same king, which is
apparently connected with this.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravatti koṉeriṉmai koṇṭāṉ emmaṇṭalamuṅkoṇṭaruḷiya teventira
[vallavaṉ] cuntarapāṇṭiyatevarkku yāṇṭu patinālāvatu nāḷ nūṟṟeṇpattu oṉ
ṟiṉāl kaṇṇaṉūri-

tam ceytaruḷiṉapaṭi niṉaippu [|*]kākku nāyaka picca[r*]kku
toṇṭaimaṇṭalattu paṭuvūrk koṭṭattu kāvirippākkap paṟ[ṟu] tiruppāṟkaṭalil iva
rukku kāṇi-

yāṉa nilattil ivarukku maṭappuṟattu iṟaiyili āka ā•• [ṇi paṭi] muṉṟu ve[li] nilam
kuṭu[t*]tom innilattukku innāḷ mutal eppeṟpaṭṭa varikaḷum kaṭamaikaḷum antarāyam
poṉvari maṟṟum eppe-

ṟpaṭṭa upātikaḷum iṉṉilattāl varumatu tavirttom iṉṉilam tāṉ veṇṭu
payircceytu koṇṭu iṉṉāḷ mutal cantirātittavarai cellak kallilum cempilum
pūtāṉa iṟaiyiliyāka peṟuka veṉ-

[ṟu] veṭṭik koḷka ippaṭikku nāḷ nūṟṟeṇpattu oṉṟiṉāl tiruvāymoḻintaruḷiṉa
paṭikku ippaṭikku putukkuṭi uṭaiyāṉ peṟṟāṉ vaḻutiyataraiyaṉ eḻuttu [||*]

edition.h4: There is another inscription in four lines below this:

l.ta:
tirupuvaṉac cakkaravatti koṉeriṉmai koṇṭāṉ emmaṇṭalamuṅ koṇṭaruḷiya teven
tira [vallavaṉ] cuntarapāṇṭiya [tevarkku] nāyakar tirumuṉpu ceyta vayirāta•••••
kku kākku nāyakap piccarkkut toṇṭaimaṇṭa-

lattu paṭuvūrkkoṭṭattuk kāvirippākkap paṟṟil tiruppāṟkaṭalil kaṇkāṇi arai nilattile
patiṉālāvatu••••mātamutal taṉakku maṭappuṟa iṟaiiliāka ivvūr aḷaviṉpaṭi
muṉṟu veli nilam••••ttu iṉ-

••• antarāyam poṉvari••••pperum eppeṟpaṭṭa aṉaittu āyavaṟkaṅkaḷum upātikaḷum iḻantu
pūtāṉa iṟaiiliyākavum••••ippaṭikku ivvolai piṭipāṭākak ko-

ṇṭu cantirātittavar[ai] celvatāka kallilum cempilum veṭṭik kaikkoṇṭu aṉupa
vikka ivai taccaṉeṉmali uṭaiy[ā*]ṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 760.
(A.R. No. 703 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west and south walls of the Raṅganāthaperumāḷ temple.
Dēvarāya II. 1442 A.D.
This record is connected with No. 751 above.

l.ta:
ṣaikkut tappuvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ
muvarāyara kaṇṭaṉ in[tu] irāya curattāṇaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭanāṭu kuṭātāṉ
pūṟuva tekṣaṇa paccima uttira camuttirātipati kecaveṭṭai kaṇṭaruḷiya tevarāya

[miyum tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum] peṟṟa pūcattu
nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa maṇṭala[ttu] tāmaṟ koṭṭattu vaṭakarai nāṭṭu taṉiyūr
tiruppā•••••••••

pūcaipuṉaṟ
kāram tiṅkaḷ tirunāḷ tiruppaṇi ṉiṟkku

jayakaḷe k[ā*]ttāvāka intak koyil
kaḷukku naṭatta kaṭavarkaḷākavum naṭattu[m*]paṭi tiṅkaḷ tivacaṅkaḷum tirunāḷuṅ aṅka
raṅka vayipokaṅka••••

mallaiyya cu[vāmiai]yyakaḷe naṭattumpaṭikku coṭicūlavariyum kaḻittu tārā pūṟuvamāka
kuṭuttom tippayatevamāirācar āna kāriyattukku kaṭava nāyak[ka]r irukaṇa
nāyakkarkku samuka appaṇaiyum paṇṇit taṉma cātaṉamum kuṭuttu aruḷiṉapaṭi ik ko
yilkaḷukku coṭicūlavariyuṅ kaḻittu tiṅka[ḷ*] ti[va*]ṣaṅkaḷu

ṅka[raṅka*] vayipoka[ṅka]ḷum naṭakkumpa[ṭi] kallu ve[ṭṭi]kkuṭuttom intap
piṟamāṇam cantirātitta••••

kaṭavatu ākavum intappiṟamāṇattukku ayitam paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ keṅkaik karaiyil kurālkapilaip
pacuvaik koṉṟa toṣattile pokakkaṭavarkaḷ ākavum tāṉa māṉṉiyam pūṟuvamatikāri
naṭakkak kaṭavatu ākavum ippaṭikkut taṟma cātaṉam paṇṇik kuṭuttom
l.gr:
svadattāt dviguṇampuṇyam paradattānupālanam [|*] paradattāpahār[ēṇa] svadatta
nniṣphalambhavēt [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 761.
(A.R. No. 704 of 1904.)
NEḌUṄGUṄAM, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Rāmachandraperumāḷ temple.
Achutadēva-mahārāya. 1544 A.D.

The details of date, viz. Śaka 1466, Krōdhin, Makara śu. 12, Thursday, Rōhiṇī,
correspond to 1544 A.D., December 25, Thursday, f.d.n. ·07.

This inscription records that the village Teṉṉāṟampaṭṭu alias Rāmachandrapur
ram, which had been granted as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam by Mallappa-nāyaka of Vēppanpaṭṭu
was reendowed for Sarvādhyayana by the authorities of the Rāmachandra-perumāḷ
temple at Neḍuṅguṉṟam and Mallappa-nāyaka was to maintain 12 Śrīvaishṇavas taking
part in the tiruvadhyayana••

l.ta:
ttu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭa
lattu paṭaiviṭṭu rācciyattu kuḻaippalūr nāṭṭu murukuma[ṅka]lappaṟṟu palkuṉṟak koṭ
ṭattu me[ṟ]kuṉṟa nāṭṭil neṭuṅkuṉṟattu

cantirap perumāḷukku vep
panpaṭṭu mallappa nāyakkar uḷḷiṭṭār tiruviḷaiyāṭṭamāka viṭṭa teṉṉā[ṟa]mpaṭṭukku

ttumallappa nāyakkaruḷḷiṭṭārumrāmacan
tiṟaperumāḷ koyil tāṉattārum rāmacantirap perumāḷ tiruṉāḷ tiruvatti
yeṉam celuttuk koḷ••• kkumpaṭikku śrīvaiṣṇavarkaḷukku caruvattiyeṉa taṟma
māka viṭṭu niccayitta

l.gr:
svadattāt dvigu
ṇam puṇyam paradattānupālanam [|*] paradattāpahārēṇa svadattanniṣphalam
bhavēt ||* svadattāmparadattām vā yōharēta vasundharām ṣaṣṭirvvarṣa sahasrāṇi
viṣṭhāyāṇḍāyatē kṛmiḥ [||*]••

inscription.head:
No. 762.
(A.R. No. 705 of 1904.)
NEḌUṄGUṆAM, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the main gōpura of the Rāmachandraperumāḷ temple, right of the entrance

This record is dated in Rākshasa leading to Ānanda, (apparently a mistake for
Ānanda leading to Rākshasa), Vaikāśi 19. The cyclic year may be equated to 1614
A.D., on account of the identity of Raghunātha-nāyakkar mentioned in the
inscription with the homonymous ruler of Tañjāvūr.

This inscription records the gift of 3 kāṇi of land in Tōṟṟampaṭṭu village by
Śōṇādri Ayyaṉ, the agent of Raghunāthanāyakkarayyaṉ as [pēr-mānyam] to Muttu,
daughter of Attagiri, one of the dēvaḍiyār belonging to the Vaḍuga group on the
occasion of conferring the title of Vijayarāghava-māṇikkam on her, during the fes-
tival in the month of Vaikāśi, in the Perīyaṇṇaṉ-maṇḍapa of the temple.

l.ta: śrī kāriyattukku kaṟtarāṉa tirumalaiyyaṅkārum śrī paṇṭārattārum
rakuṉāta ṉāyakkar ayyaṉ kāriyattukku kaṟttārāṉa coṇāttiri
muttuvukku cuvāmi rāmacantirap perumāḷ tiruvaiyyāci tiruṇāḷil
toṟṟampaṭṭu eri kiḻ cīrāmaveḷḷaiyaṉ piṟavaṭai kāṇi ṉālukkum

••ri nir erivāykāṇi [o]ṉṟukku ka•••• ma kam cillaṟai poṟuppu
uḷpaṭa
kūṭiṉa poṉ āṟukku eri etuvāy kāṇi oṉṟukku oru poṉṉum pa[ṇam]

ṉālum tiruttu kāṇi oṉṟukku oru poṉṉu[m] paṇam oṉṟum āka iraṇṭu poṉ[ṉum]
paṇam
añcu poyi nikki mūṉṟu poṉṉum paṇam añcukku kūṭiṉa [po*]ṉ
muṉṟum itukku uḷḷa yiṟaiyum vicaiyarākuvamāṇikkattukku permāṉi-
yattukku kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭom nāḷatu mutal kaikkoṇṭu putti-
varākavum aṉupavikkumiṭattu yinta••••makkum miṟaivari eccoṟu āḷ amañci
nirkūli yippaṭi[k*] kotta cilliṟaiyaḷum veṇṭāmeṉṟu ca[ṟu]vamāṉi[yamā]-
ka kaṭṭa[ḷai] iṭṭom yinta māniya[m*] allāmalum perumāḷ koyi[li]l•[ḷakku]
yirāma piṟacātam kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭom yinta tammattukku yātāmoruvar viṭam[pa]
m colli yitukku niṉaiccaper keṅkaikkaraiyile kārām pacuvai
yum taṉṉiṭa tāy takappaṉaiyum koṉṟa tosattile po

inscription.head:
No. 763.
(A.R. No. 706 of 1904.)
NEḌUṄGUṆAM, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the main gōpura of the Rāmachandra-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription is dated in Rākshasa, Paṅguṉi. 4, which will correspond to 1616
A.D., February-March. This records the gift of 6 paṇam per month and 150 kuḻi
of land to Mārgasahāya Paṇḍitar, a physician, by Śōṇādri Ayyaṉ, the agent of
Raghunātha Nāyakar, Tirumalai-Ayyaṅgār, the Śrīkāryam officer of the Rāmachandra-
perumāḷ temple and the Śrībhaṇḍārattār.

l.ta: ku kaṟttarāṉa coṇāttiri a[y*]yaṉ avarkaḷum aṉma
mātam oṉṟukku paṇam āṟum tirumā•• tirumalaiya-

inscription.head:
No. 764.
(A.R. No. 707 of 1904.)
NEḌUṄGUṆAM, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the main gōpura of the Rāmachandaraperumāḷ temple, left of the entrance

This inscription is dated Virōdhikṛit, Paṅguṉi 11. It records gift of land by
Śōnādri Ayyaṉ, the agent of Ariyappa Aiyyaṉ, Tirumalai Ayyaṅgār, the Śrīkāryam
officer of the temple and the officers of the temple treasury as pēr-māṉyam to Rāma-
chandra-māṇikkam (cf. No. 762 above).

l.ta: pa ayyaṉ kāriyattukku kaṟtarāṉa coṇāttiri ayyaṉum rāmacan-
atti piṟavaṭai kāṇimuṉṟum pu[tti]ra pavittira pārampari-
[o]ruvar akitam paṇ[ṇi] āḷ amañci•• kūli piṟavari eṉṟa-
vaṉ keṅkai karaiyile kārāmpacuvai koṉṟa to-

inscription.head:
No. 765.
(A.R. No. 708 of 1904.)
NEḌUṄGUṆAM, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Dīrghāchalēśvara temple.
Kṛishṇadēvarāya. 1527 A.D.

The Śaka year 1454 and the cyclic year Sarvajit do not tally. If the Śaka year
1454 is taken to be a mistake for Śaka 1450 (current) the details of date viz., Sarvajit,
Makara śu. 7, Rēvati, Sunday, would correspond to 1527 A.D. December 29, ·60;
·97.

This inscription records a gift of land, for the merit of Namaśśivāya Nāyakkar,
the agent of Immaḍi Tirumalai Nāyakkar, to the deity Īśāṇḍa Nayiṉār at Neḍuṅ-
guṉṟam, in Śrī-Rāmamaṅgala-ppaṟṟu, in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅ-
goṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
hāmaṇṭalecura metiṉīmīcara kaṇṭa kaṭṭ[ā*]ri cāḷuva kaṇṭa nāṭu koṇṭu
koṇṭa nāṭu koṭātāṉ pūṟuvatakṣiṇa paccima

lattup palkuṉṟakkoṭṭattu melkuṉṟa nā[ṭṭil] śrīṟāma[ma*]ṅkalap paṟṟil neytal
neṭuṅkuṉṟam nayiṉār īcāṇṭa nayiṉārkkuc cakala pūcaiyum naṭakka śrīmatu immaṭi tirumalai
nāyakkar kāriyattukkuk kaṭava

namaccivāya nāyakkar taṉmamāka allāḷa nātar piḷḷai neṭuṅkuṉṟam ērikkut teṉkiḻakku
mātapparaiyar tāṅkalum tevar mātapparaiyar puṟavaṭai nālum uḷpaṭac caṅki-
ṟama puṇṇiyakālattile ūravar pala paṭṭaṭaiyārum āka

ka tārāpūṟuvamākap paṇṇik
kuṭuttom cantiṟātittavaraiyum cellak kaṭavatākavum inta taṉma-

aik karaiyil kārāmpacuvaik koṉṟa pāvattile
povarkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 766.
(A.R. No. 709 of 1904.)
NEḌUṄGUṆAM, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine, right of the en-
trance in the Dīrghāchalēśvara temple.

This inscription is dated in Vishu, Tai 19. On account of the mention of Vijaya
rāghava-nāyakkar who commenced to rule from 1633 A.D., the cyclic year Vishu,
a mistake for Vṛisha will correspond to 1641-42 A.D., It records the gift of two
villages Kiḻavaṉ Pūṇḍi and Mōśupāḍi, made free of the levies poṟuppu and meṉkā-
val, by Śiṅgama-nāyakkar, the agent of Vijayarāghava-nāyakkar for the conduct of
pūjā regularly by the śeṭṭis in the temple of Īśāṇḍār Īśvarar.

l.ta:
śrīmatu accuta vicaiyarākavaṉāyakkarayyaṉavarkaḷ kāriyattukku kaṟtarāṉa
[ci]ṅkama ṉāyakkar yicāṇṭacar yicuparar-

koyil pūcai paṭittarattukku kiḻavaṉpūṇṭi mocupāṭi kiṟāma [mi]raṇṭum muṉ koyilukku
naṭantapaṭiye kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭom anta kiṟāma

ttil poṟuppu meṉkāval uṇṭāṉatu veṇṭāmeṉṟu kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭom antapaṭikku koyil
ceṭṭimār vacamāka naṭa-

ttikkoṇṭu pūcai tāḻvaṟa naṭattivarac colli kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭom antapaṭi uttarottiramāka
naṭattikkoṇṭu

varavum yitukku akutam niṉaicca per keṅkai karaiyile kārāmpacuvai koṉṟa toṣattile
povāṉ

inscription.head:
No. 767.
(A.R. No. 710 of 1904.)
NEḌUṄGUṆAM, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Dīrghāchalēśvara temple.
Veṅkaṭapati. 1597 A.D.

The details of date viz., Śaka 1518, Durmukhi, Makara śu. 7, Friday, correspond
1597 A.D., January, ·14, ·78.

This inscription records the gift of 15 poṉ being the amount realised from the tax
levied from the village Mōśipāḍi, by Dinakara Piḷḷai, of Tōvūr, on behalf of
Achchutappa-nāyakkarayyaṉ for conducting the daily pūjā for the deity Iśāṉa-nayiṉār,
with 1/2 paṇam daily, for the merit of Dīkshitarayyaṉ.

l.ta: pakṣattu cattamiyum cukkiṟa vāramum peṟṟa iṟṟai

nāḷ neṭuṅkuṉṟam yīcāṉa nayiṉār tampirāṉāṟku tiṉappūcaikku tiṉamoṉṟukku paṇam
[arai]yāka accutappa nāyakkar ayyaṉ

puṇṇiyamākak (ka)kaṭṭaḷaikkut tovūrt tiṉakara piḷḷaik kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭa mocipāṭikku kovappaṭṭu
meṉkāval

inta taṟmam cantiṟātittavaraiyum naṭakkak kaṭavatākavum mocipāṭi meṉkāval ca[ṟu]vamāṉiya
mākak kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭom inta taṟmattukku akitam paṇṇi-

ṉa per uṇṭāṉāl keṅkai karaiyile kārāmpacuvaik koṉṟa toṣattile poka
kaṭavarkaḷākavum

inscription.head:
No. 768.
(A.R. No. 711 of 1904.)
TIRUMALA (TIRUPATI), CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the first prākāra (called Mukkōṭi-ēkādaśi-pradakshiṇa) in
the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.
Vijayagaṇḍagōpāla.
Published in T.T. Devasthanam Inscriptions, Vol. I, No. 67.

inscription.head:
No. 769.
(A.R. No. 712 of 1904.)
TIRUMALA (TIRUPATI), CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the first prākāra (called the Mukkōṭi-ēkādaśi-pradakshiṇa)
in the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumaḷ temple.
[Jaṭāvarmaṉ] Sundarapāṇḍya I.

This fragmentary inscription contains portions of the praśasti of the king commens
cing with Samasta-jagad-ādhāra etc. It mentions the regnal year 7 in line 4.

l.note: The stone containing portion of the first line is lost and the further portion is built in.

l.ta:
vatu
mutal ko[yi]l kaṇakkum karaṇattār kaṇakkum jaya•••

inscription.head:
No. 770.
(A.R. No. 713 of 1904.)
TIRUMALA (TIRUPATI), CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the first prākāra (called Mukkōṭi-ēkādaśi-pradakshiṇa) in
the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.
[Kulōttuṅga I].
Published in T.T. Devasthanam Inscriptions Vol. I, No. 22.

inscription.head:
No. 771.
(A.R. No. 714 of 1904.)
TIRUMALA (TIRUPATI), CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the first prākāra (called the Mukkōṭi-ēkādaśi-pradakshiṇa)
in the Veṅkaṭeśa-perumāḷ temple.
Yādavarāya Vīra Narasiṅgadēva.
Published in T.T. Devasthanam Inscriptions, Vol. I, No. 93.

inscription.head:
No. 656.
(A.R. No. 605 of 1904.)
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: ON THE EAST BASE OF THE LAKSHMĪNĀRĀYAṆA PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE.
Parāntaka I. Year 38. 944-45 A.D.

This inscription is dated in the 38th regnal year of Parakēsarivarman who con-
quered Madurai and Īḻam i.e., Parāntaka I.

This records the sale of land by the Mūlaparaḍai (Mūlaparishad) of Mahēndra-
maṅgalam to Kāppiyaṉ Eḻuvaṉ-Gaṅgādharaṉ who endowed the same as tiruviḷakkup-
puṟam to god Tiruvagnīśvarattu-pperumāṉaḍigaḷ. The land was made tax-free by
the Mūlaparaḍai. The donor figures also in some more inscriptions from the place
(vide Nos. 639 above, S.I.I. XIII, No. 2, No. 656-A, below).

l.ta:
ttup perumāṉaṭikaḷukku tiru
viḷakku oṉṟiṉukku viḷakkuppuṟamāka paraṭaiyom viṟṟukkuṭutta nilamāvatu•••
pāl•••••• eṉṟum•yñca•••••

lamuta•peṟpaṭṭa nilam viṟṟukkuṭuttaṟkicaitta vellai kiḻpāṟkellai vañcivāykkā
lukku mekkum teṉpāṟkellai vañci vāykkālukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai
toṭṭiyattu p[e]runilattukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai toṭṭiyattu

• lai••••• lukku teṟkum ivvicaitta perunāṉkellaiyuḷ akappaṭṭa nilat
tukku [uṇṇila moḻi]viṉṟi iṟaiyili tiruviḷakkuppuṟamāka viṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇañceytu
kuṭuttom mūlaparuṭaiyom tiru

tināl vanta iṟaiyum eccoṟum veṭṭi vetiṉaiyum eppeṟpaṭṭa iṟaiyum paraṭaiyom viṟ
kiṉṟa nilattiṉ meleṟṟikkoṇṭu iṟuppatāka iṟaiyiḻittu eḻuvaṅkaṅkātiraṉukku
ṉṟum

kuṭuttom paraṭaiyom perumāṉaṭikaḷukku[*] vilaipporuḷ aiṅkaḻañcum eḻuvaṅkātiraṉiṭai
āvaṇakkaḷiye poruḷaṟakkoṇṭu innilam viḷakkuppuṟamāka viṟṟuk kuṭuttu poruḷ
māvaṟutip poruṭ celavolaiyumituveyāka poruḷmāvaṟu
tip poruṭ cilavu [a]ṟṟu•••• lai••••kuṭuttom mūlaparaṭaiyom tiru

inscription.head:
No. 656A.
(A.R. No. 605 A of 1904.)This is not noticed in the Annual Report.
ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: ON THE SOUTH AND EAST BASE OF THE LAKSHMĪNĀRĀYAṆA PERUMĀL TEMPLE.
Parāntaka I.

This inscription belongs to the reign of Maduraikoṇḍa Parakēsarivarman i.e.,
Parāntaka I and the portion containing the regnal year is damaged.

It records the gift of land by Kāppiyaṉ Eḻuvaṅ-Gaṅgādharaṉ (See No. 656
above) for burning a perpetual lamp to god Tirukkurakkutturai-pperumāṉaḍigaḷ
in Mahēndramaṅgalam, the sabhā of which place made the land tax-free.

l.ta:
